In a large plastic resealable container (or in a large glass mixing bowl), mix yeast and salt into 3 c lukewarm (about 100 degrees) water. Using a large spoon, stir in flour, mixing until mixture is uniformly moist with no dry patches. Do not knead. Dough will be soft and loose enough to conform to shape of container.
Cover, but not with an airtight lid (I use parchment paper).
Let dough rise at room temperature, until dough begins to flatten on top or collapse, at least 2 hours and up to 5 hours (At this point dough can be refrigerated up to 2 weeks; refrigerated dough is easier to work with than room-temperature dough, so the author recommends that first-time bakers refrigerate dough over night or at least 3 hours.)
When ready to bake, sprinkle cornmeal on a pizza peel or cutting board. Place a broiler pan on bottom rack of oven. Place baking stone on middle rack and preheat oven to 450 degrees, preheating baking stone for at least 20 minutes.
Sprinkle a little flour on dough and on your hands. Pull dough up and, using a serrated knife, cut off a grapefruit-size piece (about 1 pound). Working for 30 to 60 seconds (and adding flour as needed to prevent dough from sticking to hand; most dusting flour will fall off, it’s not intended to be incorporated into dough), turn dough in hands, gently stretching surface of dough, rotating ball a quarter-turn as you go, creating a rounded top and bunched bottom.
Place shaped dough on prepared pizza peel and let rest, uncovered, for 40 minutes. Repeat with remaining dough or refrigerate lidded container. (Even one day’s storage improves flavor and texture of bread.) Dough can also be frozen in 1-pound portions in airtight containers and defrosted overnight in refrigerator prior to baking day. I typically just put what’s left back in the fridge, covered with plastic wrap, and bake a fresh one each day for four days)
Dust dough with flour. Using a serrated knife, slash top of dough in three parallel, ¼ -inch deep cuts (or in a tic-tac-toe pattern). Slide dough onto preheated baking stone. Pour 1 cut hot tap water into broiler pan and quickly close oven door to trap steam. Bake until crust is well-browned and firm to the touch, about 30 minutes. Remove from oven to a wire rack and cool completely. Some say that flavor improves if you bake early and reheat at serving time.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Osterizer Cookbook
Ingredients:
1 c buttermilk
1 egg
1 Tbsp. sugar
1 c sifted flour
1 tsp. baking soda
½ t salt
1 Tbsp. cooking oil
Instructions: Put all ingredients into blender container, cover and process at mix until well blended. Bake on lightly greased griddle.

Ingredients:
10 c. all-purpose flour (3 lbs.)
2 1/2 c. instant nonfat dry milk
1/2 c. sugar
1/4 c. baking power
2 tbsp. salt
Instructions:
Combine all ingredients in a large bowl. Stir together to mix well. Put in a large airtight container. Label. Store in a cool, dry place. Use within 6 to 8 months. This mix works for both waffles and pancakes.
To make pancakes or waffles:
2 c. homemade pancake mix
1 c. water
1 egg
2 tbsp. oil (waffles – 3 tbsp.)
Beat smooth with a wire whisk or fork. This makes eight (4 inch) pancakes or four large waffles.

Ingredients:
1 ½ c self-rising flour
2 Tbsp. sugar
1 c milk
1 large egg, lightly beaten
1 Tbsp. vegetable oil
Instructions:
Preheat griddle to 400 degrees or heat lightly oil skillet until a few droplets of water sprinkled on surface evaporate quickly.
Combine flour and sugar in mixing bowl. Combine milk, egg and oil. Add liquid ingredients to flour mixture. Stir just until well blended. (Batter will be slightly lumpy.)
Pour about ¼ c batter into hot griddle for each pancake. Cook until golden brown, turning once. Serve warm.

Ingredients:
1-1/2 c all-purpose flour
3 Tbsp. granulated sugar
4 tsp. baking powder
1/8 tsp. salt
1/8 tsp. ground allspice
2 eggs
1 c milk
1/4 c vegetable oil
Butter, maple or pancake syrup, confectioners' sugar, for serving
Instructions:
Heat a griddle or large pan to medium heat. Heat oven to warm (180°F) and place a foil-lined baking sheet inside.
In a large bowl, whisk together flour, granulated sugar, baking powder, salt and allspice.
Separate eggs and whisk egg yolks in small bowl with milk and oil. With an electric mixer, beat egg whites to stiff peaks, about 1 minute.
Stir milk mixture into flour mixture just until combined. Fold in egg whites in two additions, until no white streaks remain.
Using a ladle, drop 1/4 to 1/3 c batter onto heated griddle. Repeat, spacing at least 1 inch apart. Cook 1 minute, or until bubbles in batter pop and remain open. Carefully flip pancakes and cook an additional 30 seconds to 1 minute.
Transfer to baking sheet in warm oven; repeat with remaining batter. Serve warm with butter and syrup or confectioners' sugar, if desired.

Ingredients:
1 c self-rising flour
½ tsp. baking soda
2 large eggs, separated
1/3 c butter, melted
1 c buttermilk
Instructions:
Follow instructions for preheating and preparing your waffle iron for use.
Beat egg whites until stiff.
Combine flour and baking soda. Combine egg yolks, butter and milk. Stir into flour mixture. Fold in egg whites.
Pour batter into center of waffle iron. Bake until no steam shows around edges or iron. Lift top and loosen waffle with a fork.

Ingredients:
1 c boiling water
6 tbsp shortening
¼ c sugar
1 tsp salt
1 (1/4-oz) envelope active dry yeast
¼ c warm water (105 to 115 degrees)
1 large egg, lightly beaten
4 c all-purpose flour
¼ c butter, melted and divided
Instructions:
Pour boiling water over shortening and next 2 ingredients in bowl of a heavy-duty electric stand mixer, and stir until shortening melts and sugar and salt are completely dissolved. Let stand 10 minutes or until about 110 degrees.
Meanwhile, combine yeast and warm water in a 1-cup liquid measure cup; let stand 5 minutes.
Add yeast mixture and egg to shortening mixture; beat at low speed until combined. Gradually add flour, beating at low speed 2 to 3 minutes or until flour is blended and dough is soft and smooth.
Place dough in a lightly greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and chill 8 to 24 hours.
Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface, and knead until smooth and elastic (about 1 minute). Gently shape dough into 60 (1-inch) balls; place 3 dough balls in each cup of 2 lightly greased 12-cup muffin pans. (You will fill only 20 cups.) Brush rolls with half of melted butter.
Cover pans with plastic wrap, and let rise in a warm place (85 degrees), free from drafts, 45 minutes to 1 hour or until doubled in bulk.
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Bake rolls for 8 to 12 minutes or until golden brown. Brush with remaining melted butter.

Ingredients:
2 c flour
1 tbsp. sugar
Pinch of salt
1 ¾ tsp. baking powder
1 heaping tbsp. shortening
1 egg, slightly beaten
¼ c milk
Butter
Instructions:
Sift together the dry ingredients. Cut in shortening. Stir in egg then add milk. Roll out and cut in rounds. Fold each round in half over a small piece of butter.
Bake at 400 degrees for about 15 minutes.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Eva Hurdle
Ingredients:
1 c plus 2 tbsp. milk
6 tbsp. Crisco shortening
4 tbsp. sugar
2 tsp. salt

½ c lukewarm water
1 tsp. sugar
1 pkg dry yeast

4 c flour

1 stick butter
Instructions:
Scald milk, add Crisco, sugar and salt. Let cool to lukewarm.

Put yeast in lukewarm water with sugar and let rise to top of cup. Mix two liquids together when the first has cooled to lukewarm, add flour,

Let rise for 2 hours. Melt butter.

Make out rolls, dip in butter. Shape for pocket book rolls. Put in freezer or let rise for 1 hour. Let frozen rolls rise for 3 hours before baking.

Notes:
Although you’ll only need about three tbsp. batter to make each crepe, we found a ¼-cup dry measuring cup is the best tool to scoop and pour the batter into the pan so the crepes cook evenly. The small amount of sugar gives the crepes a golden appearance and crisp edges without adding noticeable sweetness, so this recipe works in both savory and sweet applications.
13 crepes
Ingredients:
1 cup all-purpose flour (about 4 ½ oz.)
2 tsp. sugar
¼ tsp. salt
1 cup milk
½ cup water
2 tsp. butter, melted
2 large eggs
Instructions:
Lightly spoon flour into a dry measuring cup; level with a knife. Combine flour, sugar, and salt in a small bowl. Combine milk, water, melted butter, and eggs in a blender. Add the flour mixture to milk mixture, and process until smooth. Cover batter; chill for 1 hour.
Heat an 8-inch nonstick crepe pan or skillet over medium heat. Pour a scant ¼ cup batter into pan; quickly tilt pan in all directions so batter covers pan with a thin film. Cook about 1 minute. Carefully lift the edge of the crepe with a spatula to test for doneness. The crepe is ready to turn when it can be shaken loose from the pan and the underside is lightly browned. Turn crepe over, and cook for 30 seconds or until center is set.
Please crepe on a towel; cool completely. Repeat procedure with the remaining batter, stirring batter between crepes. Stack crepes between single layers of wax paper to prevent sticking.

Ingredients:
2 c all-purpose flour
2 tsp. baking powder
1/8 tsp. salt
¼ c sugar
1/3 c butter, cubed
½ c whipping cream
1 large egg
1 ½ tsp. vanilla
1 egg white
1 tsp. water
Sugar
Instructions:
Combine first 4 ingredients. Cut in butter with a pastry blender or two forks until mixture resembles coarse meal.
Whisk together cream, egg, and vanilla; add to flour mixture, stirring just until dry ingredients are moistened.
Turn dough out onto a lightly floured surface. Pat dough to ½-inch thickness; cut with a 2 ½-inch round cutter, and place on baking sheets.
Whisk together egg white and 1 tsp. water; brush mixture over tops of scones. Sprinkle scones with additional sugar.
Bake at 425 degrees for 13 to 15 minutes or until scones are lightly browned.

Ingredients:
2 ¼ c all-purpose flour
¼ c sugar
1 ½ tsp. baking powder
¼ tsp. baking soda
¼ tsp. salt
¼ c butter
2/3 c buttermilk
1 large egg, lightly beaten
Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
Instructions:
Combine flour, sugar, baking powder, and salt in a large bowl. Divide butter into several pieces and scatter on top of flour mixture. Work pieces into flour with a pastry cutter, two knives, or your fingertips until pieces are about the size of peas.
Combine buttermilk and egg. Gradually stir into flour.
Turn dough onto a lightly floured surface. Knead gently two or three strokes. Using a light touch, pat or roll dough to ¾-inch thickness. Slice into 6 wedges. Place on a baking sheet about ½-inch apart. Brush tops with beaten whole egg.
Bake for 12 to 15 minutes or until lightly browned. Serve warm or cooled. Makes six scones.

Notes: Makes 16 servings. Extra-Fudgy Brownies: Use 4 eggs.
Ingredients:
1 pkg. (4 oz.) BAKER'S Unsweetened Chocolate
3/4 cup butter or margarine
2 cups sugar
3 eggs
1 tsp. vanilla
1 cup flour
1 cup coarsely chopped Pecans
Instructions:
HEAT oven to 350°F.
LINE 13x9-inch pan with foil, with ends of foil extending over sides. Spray foil with cooking spray.
MICROWAVE chocolate and butter in large microwaveable bowl on HIGH 2 min. or until butter is melted. Stir until chocolate is completely melted. Stir in sugar. Blend in eggs and vanilla. Add flour and nuts; mix well. Pour into prepared pan.
BAKE 30 to 35 min. or until toothpick inserted in center comes out with fudgy crumbs. (Do not overbake.) Cool completely. Use foil handles to remove brownies from pan before cutting to serve.
kraft kitchens tips
SIZE-WISE Pack one of these brownies in a lunch box as a special treat.
WHITE CHOCOLATE GLAZE Prepare brownies as directed, but do not cut brownies. Microwave 1/3 cup whipping cream in medium microwaveable bowl on HIGH 45 sec. or just until cream comes to boil. Add 6 oz. BAKER'S White Chocolate, chopped. Stir until white chocolate is completely melted and mixture is well blended. Drizzle over brownie. Let stand until set before cutting to serve.
VARIATIONS Prepare as directed, using one of the following options: Super Chunk Brownies: Stir 2 pkg. (4 oz. each) BAKER'S Semi-Sweet Chocolate, coarsely chopped, and 1/2 cup raisins into prepared batter before pouring into prepared pan and baking as directed. Cake-Like Brownies: Stir in 1/2 cup milk along with the eggs and vanilla, and increase the flour to 1-1/2 cups. Extra-Thick Brownies: Spread prepared batter into 9-inch square pan. Bake 50 min. Cool, then cut to serve.

Ingredients:
1 egg
1 c light brown sugar, packed
1 tsp. vanilla
½ c sifted flour
¼ tsp. salt
¼ tsp. baking soda
1 c chopped nuts
Instructions: Beat egg. Add sugar and vanilla. Blend in dry ingredients. Add nuts. Pour into well-greased 8-inch square pan. Bake at 350 degrees for 20-25 minutes. Allow to cool completely. This will be slightly soft when removed from the oven.

Ingredients:
8 Tbsp. unsalted butter, cut into pieces, plus more for pan
1 c all-purpose flour (spooned and leveled)
½ c unsweetened cocoa powder
½ tsp. baking powder
½ tsp. salt
8 ounces semi-sweet or bittersweet chocolate, chopped
1 ¼ c sugar
3 large eggs
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350. Brush a 9-inch square baking pan with butter. Line bottom and two sides with a strip of parchment paper, leaving a 2-inch overhang on the two sides. Butter paper, and set pan aside. In a small bowl, whisk flour, cocoa, baking powder, and salt; set aside.
Place butter and chocolate in a large heat-proof bowl set over (not in) a saucepan of gently simmering water. Heat, stirring occasionally, until smooth, 2 to 3 minutes; remove bowl from pan. Add sugar; mix to combine. Add eggs, and mix to combine. Add flour mixture; mix just until moistened (do not over mix). Transfer batter to prepared pan; smooth top.
Bake until a toothpick inserted in center comes out with a few moist crumbs attached, 50 to 60 minutes. Cool in pan for 30 minutes. Using paper overhang, lift brownies out of pan; transfer to a rack to cool completely (still on paper). On a cutting board, using a dampened serrated knife, cut into 16 squares. Store in an airtight container at room temperature, up to 2 days.

Cream-Cheese Brownies
Before transferring the batter to the pan at the end of step 2 in the basic recipe, prepare cream-cheese mixture: Whisk 4 ounces room-temperature bar cream cheese with 2 Tbsp. room-temperature unsalted butter. Whisk in ¼ c sugar, 1 large egg, and 2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour. Alternately spoon chocolate batter and cream-cheese mixture into pan; with the tip of a paring knife, swirl to marble.

Nut Brownies
After adding flour mixture at the end of step 2, add 1 c nuts, coarsely chopped, to the batter.

Chocolate-Chip Brownies
After adding flour mixture at the end of step 2, add 1 c semisweet chocolate chips to the batter.

Mocha Brownies
At the beginning of step 2, add 2 tsp. instant espresso powder to the butter and chocolate.

Tips for Perfect Brownies
My recipe-generous on the chocolate, butter and sugar – makes a very fudgy brownie. When you test a fudgy brownie for doneness, the toothpick you insert in the center should come out with a few moist crumbs attached (it it’s clean, it’s over baked).

A Cut Above
For neat squares, a couple of things really make a difference. Most important, cut your brownies on a cutting board, never in the pan. Also I like using a serrated knife; first, I gently saw through the crust and then I push straight down to cut all the way through. Too keep edges extra neat, before each cut, I clean the blade under war water and leave it slightly wet.

About Storage
It’s very easy to make brownies in advance. Their shelf life really depends on whether they’ve been cut. Store cut brownie squares in an airtight container at room temperature; they’re best within 1 to 2 days. A whole pan’s worth uncut and well wrapped in plastic, will keep at room temperature up to 4 days, or in the freezer up to 3 months. Cut into squares before serving.

Ingredients:
2 c old-fashioned oatmeal
1 c sliced almonds
1 c shredded coconut, loosely packed
¼ c toasted wheat germ
3 Tbsp. unsalted butter
2/3 c honey
¼ c light brown sugar, lightly packed
1 ½ tsp. vanilla extract
¼ tsp. kosher salt
½ c chopped pitted dates
½ c chopped dried apricots
½ c dried cranberries
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Butter an 8 x 12-inch baking dish and line it with parchment paper.
Toss the oatmeal, almonds, and coconut together on a sheet pan and bake for 10 to 12 minutes. Stirring occasionally until lightly browned. Transfer the mixture to a large mixing bowl and stir in the wheat germ.
Reduce the oven temperature to 300 degrees.
Place the butter, honey, brown sugar, vanilla, and salt in a small saucepan and bring to a boil over medium heat. Cook and stir for a minute, then pour over the toasted oatmeal mixture. Add the dates, apricots, and cranberries and stir well.
Pour the mixture into the prepared pan. Wet your fingers and lightly press the mixture evenly into the pan. Bake for 25 to 30 minutes, until light golden brown. Cool for at least 2 to 3 hours before cutting into square. Serve at room temperature.

Ingredients:
1 ½ c cornflakes
1 stick butter, melted
3 tbsp. sugar
6 oz. chocolate chips
3 ½ oz. canned coconut
1 cup pecans
1 can condensed milk
Instructions: Mix cornflakes, melted butter and sugar together and press in 9x9 pan. Layer chocolate chips, then coconut, then pecans and then condensed milk on top. Bake at 350 degrees for about 25 minutes.

Ingredients:
Crust
½ c butter, softened
¼ c granulated sugar
1 1 1/4c all-purpose flour

Filling
2 large eggs, lightly beaten
¾ cup granulated sugar
1/3 cup fresh lemon juice
3 tbsp. flour
1 tsp. grated lemon peel
¼ tsp. baking powder
Confectioners’ sugar
Instructions:
Make crust: Heat oven to 350 degrees. In a medium bowl, beat the butter and sugar with a wooden spoon until creamy. Gradually stir in the flour. Press evenly into the bottom of an ungreased 9-inch square baking pan. Bake until golden, 20 to 25 minutes.
Make filling: In a medium bowl, whisk together eggs, sugar, lemon juice, flour, lemon peel and baking powder and pour over the hot crust. Bake until set, 12 to 15 minutes. Cool completely. Just before serving, sift confectioners’ sugar over top.

Ingredients:
16 Tbsp. unsalted butter, plus more for greasing
8 ounces bittersweet chocolate, cut into ¼” pieces
4 eggs
1 c sugar
1 c firmly packed dark brown sugar
2 tsp. vanilla
½ tsp. fine salt
1 c flour
Instructions:
Heat oven to 350. Grease a 9” x 13” baking pan with butter and line with parchment paper; grease paper. Set pan aside.
Pour enough water into a 4-quart saucepan that it reaches a depth of 1”. Bring to a boil; reduce heat to low. Combine butter and chocolate in a medium bowl; set bowl over saucepan. Cook, stirring, until melted and smooth, about 5 minutes. Remove from heat; set aside.
Whisk together eggs in a large bowl. Add sugar, brown sugar, vanilla, and salt; whisk to combine. Stir in chocolate mixture; fold in flour. Pour batter into prepared pan; spread evenly. Bake until a toothpick inserted into center comes out clean, 30-35 minutes. Let cool on a rack. Cut and serve.

Ingredients:
1 ½ c pitted Medjool dates, coarsely chopped
1/3 c Frangelico (hazelnut liqueur)
2 c blanched hazelnuts, coarsely chopped
½ tsp ground cinnamon
½ tsp ground nutmeg
½ tsp ground cardamom
½ tsp ground ginger
8 tbsp (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted
8 (12- x 8-inch) sheets of thawed phyllo dough
2/3 c fresh orange juice
2 tbsp grated orange zest
½ c sugar
Instructions:
Soak the dates in the liqueur at room temperature for at least 6 hours, stirring occasionally to make all of the dates soften. Drain the dates and mix them in a bowl with the hazelnuts, cinnamon, nutmeg, cardamom and ginger.
Preheat the oven to 375 degrees. Brush a rimmed baking sheet with some of the melted butter.
Place a phyllo sheet on a clean, dry work surface and brush it lightly with butter. Cut it in half crosswise, creating 2 (6- x 8-inch rectangles) (Keep the rest of the phyllo covered so it won’t dry out.). Spread about 3 tablespoons of the date filling across one of the short sides of the phyllo. Roll the pastry into a log and place on the buttered baking sheet. Repeat with the remaining phyllo and filling to make a total of 16 logs. Brush the tops with butter. Bake until the rolls are crisp and golden brown, about 30 to 40 minutes.
Meanwhile, bring the orange juice, orange zest and sugar to a boil in a saucepan over high heat. Reduce the heat to medium and simmer until the mixture reduces to the consistency of pancake syrup, about 15 minutes. Remove from the heat and set aside.
Remove the pastries from the oven and pour the orange glaze over, turning them with a fork to coat all sides.
Let stand at room temperature for at least 2 hours and up to overnight. The longer they rest, the better they taste. These will keep at room temperature for up to 4 days.

Ingredients:
1/3 cup honey
1/3 cup sugar
3-inch-long strip lemon zest
1 cinnamon stick
½ cup (1 stick) unsalted butter, melted, plus more for the pan
1 box (1 pound) frozen phyllo, thawed overnight in refrigerator
1 cup (4.5 oz.) shelled salted pistachio nuts, finely ground in food processor
Orange slices (optional)
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. In a small saucepan, combine honey, sugar, 1/3 cup water, lemon zest, and cinnamon. Bring to a boil, stirring until sugar has dissolved. Remove syrup from heat; set aside.
Butter bottom and sides of an 8-inch square baking dish. Cut two 8-by-13-inch sheets of parchment paper; line bottom and sides of dish, overlapping sheets in a crisscross pattern, leaving an overhang.
Unroll phyllo sheets on a flat surface; lift the top half, and carefully roll into a tight tube. Using a sharp knife, cut crosswise to make 1-inch-wide ribbons. Roll remaining phyllo sheets, and store, tightly wrapped in plastic, in refrigerator up to a week.
In a large bowl, unroll and loosen phyllo ribbons, Using your hands, gently toss with butter and all but 2 tbsp. pistachios; press firmly into prepared baking dish. Bake until lightly browned, about 45 minutes.
Remove zest and cinnamon stick from syrup; discard; reheat syrup over medium heat; pour over the hot pastry. Sprinkle with remaining 2 tbsp. pistachios; let cool completely.
Using paper overhang, lift baklava onto a cutting board. Remove paper. With a sharp knife, gently cut into 8 triangles; transfer to a serving dish. Serve with orange slices, if desired. If not serving right away, leave in dish. Cover with plastic; store at room temperature up to 3 days.

Ingredients:
3 tablespoons butter
1 package (10 oz., about 40) JET-PUFFED Marshmallows
OR
4 cups JET-PUFFED Miniature Marshmallows
6 cups Kellogg's® Rice Krispies® cereal
Instructions:
In large saucepan melt butter over low heat. Add marshmallows and stir until completely melted. Remove from heat. Add KELLOGG'S RICE KRISPIES cereal. Stir until well coated. Using buttered spatula or wax paper evenly press mixture into 13 x 9 x 2-inch pan coated with cooking spray. Cool. Cut into 2-inch squares. Best if served the same day.
MICROWAVE DIRECTIONS:
In microwave-safe bowl heat butter and marshmallows on HIGH for 3 minutes, stirring after 2 minutes. Stir until smooth. Follow steps 2 and 3 above. Microwave cooking times may vary.
Note
-For best results, use fresh marshmallows.
-1 jar (7 oz.) marshmallow crème can be substituted for marshmallows.
-Diet, reduced calorie or tub margarine is not recommended.
-Store no more than two days at room temperature in airtight container.
-To freeze, place in layers separated by wax paper in airtight container. Freeze for up to 6 weeks. Let stand at room temperature for 15 minutes before serving.

Ingredients:
20 graham crackers (individual squares), regular or chocolate
1 1/2 sticks butter
3/4 c brown sugar
1 tsp. vanilla
1/2 c chopped pecans
Instructions: Line a jelly roll pan with foil; arrange graham crackers in the pan in a single layer. Combine butter and sugar in a heavy medium saucepan; bring to a boil over medium heat, stirring occasionally. Boil for 2 minutes; stir in vanilla and chopped pecans. Pour the hot mixture over crackers and spread evenly. Bake 10 minutes at 350. Remove at once from pan to flat surface to cool. When cool, break into smaller pieces.

Ingredients:
1 box Duncan Hines all butter cake mix plus 2 oz
4 large eggs
3/4 c vegetable oil
1/2 c granulated sugar
8 ounces sour cream
1 tsp. vanilla extract
Instructions: All ingredients should be at room temperature. Preheat oven to 350. Combine cake mix, oil, sour cream and sugar. Add eggs one at a time until thoroughly mixed. Stir in vanilla. Spray Bundt pan and sugar coat the inside of the pan. Pour batter into prepared pan and bake approximately 45 minutes or until cake test is done.

Ingredients:
1 2 pound can pie apples
1/3 c brown sugar
1 box spice cake mix plus 2 oz
1 c chopped pecans
2 sticks butter
Instructions: Place ingredients into a well-greased cake pan in the order that they are listed. Do not stir the ingredients together. Bake at 350 degrees for about 50 minutes. Serve with ice cream.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Better Homes and Gardens
Ingredients:
1 c shortening
3 c sugar
4 eggs
4 c sifted flour
2 tsp. baking powder
2 tsp. cinnamon
1 tsp. soda
1 tsp. cloves
2 c thick applesauce
*2 c raisins
Instructions:
Cream shortening and sugar. Add eggs one at a time.
Sift dry ingredients. Add alternately with applesauce
Fold in raisins.
Bake in large tube pan or two loaf pans.
*1 cup chopped pecans can be used in place of 1 cup of raisins.

Ingredients:
1 package Duncan Hines Butter Recipe Golden plus 2 oz
1 package 3 ¾-ounce vanilla instant pudding and pie filling
4 eggs
1/2 c cold water
1/2 c Bacardi Dark rum
1/2 c vegetable oil
Rum glaze
Chocolate Glaze Topping
2 Tbsp. chopped walnuts
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 325. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan. Mix cake mix, pudding, eggs, water, rum and oil until smooth. Pour into prepared pan. Bake 1 hour. Cool in pan 25 minutes. Invert onto serving plate. Prick top. Spoon and brush Rum Glaze evenly over cake, allowing the cake to absorb the glaze. When cake is cooled, drizzle with Chocolate Glaze Topping; sprinkle with nuts.
Rum Glaze: 1/4 pound (1 stick) butter, 1/4 c water, 1 c granulated sugar, 1/2 c Bacardi Dark rum. Melt butter in saucepan. Stir in water and sugar. Boil 5 minutes. Remove from heat; stir in rum.
Chocolate Glaze Topping: 4 ounces semi-sweet chocolate, 1 tsp. butter or shortening. Melt chocolate and butter over very low heat in heavy saucepan.

Ingredients:
Butter, for greasing the pans
1 ¾ c all-purpose flour, plus more for pans
2 c sugar
¾ c good cocoa powder
2 tsp. baking soda
1 tsp. baking powder
1 tsp. kosher salt
1 c buttermilk, shaken
½ c vegetable oil
2 extra-large eggs, at room temperature
1 tsp. pure vanilla extract
1 c freshly brewed hot coffee
1 recipe Chocolate Frosting for Beatty’s Chocolate Cake
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Butter 2 (8-inch) round cake pans. Line with parchment paper, then butter and flour the pans.
Sift the flour, sugar, cocoa, baking soda, baking powder, and salt into the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment and mix on low speed until combined. In another bowl, combine the buttermilk, oil, eggs, and vanilla. With the mixer on low speed, slowly add the wet ingredients to the dry. With mixer still on low, add the coffee and stir just to combine, scraping the bottom of the bowl with a rubber spatula. Pour the batter into the prepared pans and bake for 35 to 40 minutes, until a cake tester comes out clean. Cool in the pans for 30 minutes, then turn them out onto a cooling rack and cool completely.
Place 1 layer, flat side up, on a flat plate or cake pedestal. With a knife or offset spatula, spread the top with frosting. Place the second layer on top, rounded side up, and spread the frosting evenly on the top and sides of the cake.
Related Recipes: Chocolate Frosting for Beatty’s Chocolate Cake

Ingredients:
1 c butter, softened
½ c shortening
1 (16-oz) package light brown sugar
5 large eggs
1 (5-oz) can evaporated milk
½ c bourbon
3 c all-purpose flour
½ tsp baking powder
½ tsp salt
1 tbsp vanilla bean paste
2 tbsp powdered sugar
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 325 degrees. Beat butter and shortening at medium speed with a heavy-duty electric stand mixer until creamy. Gradually add brown sugar, beating at medium speed until light and creamy. Add eggs, 1 at a time, beating just until blended after each addition.
Stir together evaporated milk and bourbon in a bowl. Stir together flour, baking powder, and salt in another bowl. Add flour mixture to butter mixture alternately with milk mixture, beginning and ending with flour mixture. Beat at low speed just until blended after each addition. Stir in vanilla bean paste. Pour batter into a greased and floured 10-inch (12-cup) Bundt pan.
Bake at 325 degrees for 1 hour and 5 minutes to 1 hour and 10 minutes or until a long wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool in pan on a wire rack 10 to 15 minutes; remove from pan to wire rack. Cool completely (about 1 hour). Dust top lightly with powdered sugar.

Ingredients:
1 c butter
3 c sugar
5 eggs, separated
2 tsp. vanilla
½ tsp. soda
1 c buttermilk
3 c plain flour
Instructions: Cream butter and sugar; add egg yolks, 1 at a time. Beat well. Stir in vanilla. Dissolve soda in 1 tsp. warm water. Add to milk. Add flour and milk alternately to sugar mixture. Fold in well beaten egg whites. Bake at 350 degree for about 1 hour and 10 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 cup butter
3 cup sugar
5 eggs, separated
2 tsp. vanilla
5 tsp. baking soda
1 cup buttermilk
3 cup all-purpose flour
Instructions: Cream butter and sugar; add egg yolks 1 at a time and beat well. Stir in vanilla. Dissolve soda in 1 tsp. warm water; add to milk. Add flour and milk alternately to sugar mixture. Fold in well-beaten egg whites. Bake in greased and floured tube pan at 350 degrees for about 1 hour and 15 minutes.

Ingredients:
2 cups all-purpose flour
2 teaspoons baking soda
1/2 teaspoon salt
2 teaspoons ground cinnamon
3 large eggs
2 cups sugar
3/4 cup vegetable oil
3/4 cup buttermilk
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
2 cups grated carrot
1 (8-ounce) can crushed pineapple, drained
1 (3 1/2-ounce) can flaked coconut
1 cup chopped pecans or walnuts
Buttermilk Glaze
Cream Cheese Frosting
Instructions:
Line 3 (9-inch) round cake pans with wax paper; lightly grease and flour wax paper. Set pans aside.
Stir together first 4 ingredients.
Beat eggs and next 4 ingredients at medium speed with an electric mixer until smooth. Add flour mixture, beating at low speed until blended. Fold in carrot and next 3 ingredients. Pour batter into prepared cake pans.
Bake at 350° for 25 to 30 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean.
Drizzle Buttermilk Glaze evenly over layers; cool in pans on wire racks 15 minutes. Remove from pans, and cool completely on wire racks.
Spread Cream Cheese Frosting between layers and on top and sides of cake.
Related Recipes: Cream Cheese Frosting for Carrot Cake

Recipe Name: Carrot Cupcakes with Lemony Cream Cheese Frosting
Ingredients:
For the cupcakes
¾ c whole-wheat pastry flour or regular whole-wheat flour
½ c all-purpose flour
1 tsp. baking soda
¼ tsp. salt
½ tsp. ground cinnamon
¼ tsp. ground nutmeg
¼ c canola oil
¾ c firmly packed light brown sugar
2 large eggs
½ c natural unsweetened applesauce
½ tsp. vanilla
1 ½ c finely shredded carrots (about 2 carrots)
¼ c finely chopped walnuts

For the frosting
4 ounces Neufchatel cheese (reduced-fat cream cheese) , softened
¾ c confectioners’ sugar
½ tsp. finely grated lemon zest
For garnish
2 tbsp finely chopped walnuts
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 350 degrees. Line 12 muffin cups with paper liners.
In a medium bowl, sift together both flours, the baking soda, salt and spices. In a large bowl, whisk together the oil, brown sugar, and eggs until well combined. Whisk in the applesauce, vanilla and carrots. Add the dry ingredients and mix until just combined. Stir in the walnuts.
Divide the batter between the muffin cups. Bake until a toothpick inserted in a cupcake comes out clean, about 20 minutes. Transfer to a wire rack to cool completely.
To make the frosting, with an electric mixer, beat together the cream cheese, confectioners’ sugar and lemon zest until smooth and creamy. Frost the cooled cupcakes and sprinkle with the walnuts. The cupcakes should be stored in the refrigerator, where they will keep for about 3 days.
Use a spice cake mix, and add 1 c chopped nuts and 1 1/2 c finely grated carrots to the cream cheese filling. Proceed as directed above.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Aunt Jesse
Ingredients:
3 eggs
1 c butter
2 c sugar
3 c flour
1 tsp. soda
1 tsp. baking powder
1 tsp. cinnamon
1 tsp. cloves
1 c pecans
1 tsp. vanilla
1 c cooked apples
2 small bottles chopped cherries
1 scant c buttermilk
Instructions: Cream butter and sugar; add eggs one at a time; add vanilla. Sift dry ingredients and add alternately with buttermilk. Add pecans, apples and cherries. Bake in layers. Ice with caramel icing.

Ingredients:
4 Tbsp. cake flour (that’s plain flour, not self-rising)
4 Tbsp. sugar
2 Tbsp. baking cocoa
1 egg
3 Tbsp. milk
3 Tbsp. oil
A small splash of vanilla
Instructions:
Add dry ingredients to mug, and mix well. Add the egg and mix thoroughly. Pour in the milk and oil and mix well. Add the vanilla, and mix again.
Put your mug in the microwave and cook for 3 minutes. The cake will rise over the top of the mug, but don’t be alarmed. Allow to cool a little, and then tip out onto a plate. Enjoy!

Ingredients:
1 c sugar
1 ½ c flour
½ tsp. salt
¼ c Dutch process cocoa
1 tsp. baking soda
¼ c vegetable oil
½ c water
¼ c sour cream
1 Tbsp. lemon juice
1 egg
1 tsp. vanilla
1 recipe Rum Truffle Filling
1 recipe Butter cream frosting
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Instructions:
Cut parchment paper to fit the bottom of an 8-inch or 9-inch cake pan; great pan.
Using stand mixer with the whip attachment on low speed, combine sugar, flour, and salt, cocoa and baking soda.
In a separate bowl, combine vegetable oil, water, sour cream, lemon juice, egg and vanilla. Add these wet ingredients to the dry ingredients mixing just until combined and lump-free. Do not overwork the batter.
Pour batter into prepared cake pan. Bake 30 to 40 minutes until a toothpick inserted into the center of the cake comes out clean. Cool in pan. When cake is cool, remove from pan.
For a perfectly flat top, you may carefully slice off and discard the domed top of the cake. Slice the cake horizontally in half, creating two layers. Flip the top layer so that the original domed side is down, and place it on a cake plate.
Cover the top of this layer with the Rum Truffle Filling. Flip the bottom cake layer and place it on top of the Rum Truffle Filling. Cover the top and sides of the cake with a thin layer of Butter cream Frosting. This layer is the crumb coat, and will prevent crumbs from marring the finished surface of the frosting.
Refrigerate until the crumb coat hardens. Frost the cake with the remaining Butter cream Frosting. Refrigerate to set frosting. Remove from refrigerator 1 hour before serving.

Ingredients:
2 c all-purpose flour
2 c sugar
1 ½ tsp ground cinnamon
¼ tsp salt
1 c water
½ c vegetable oil
½ c butter, cubed
¼ c baking cocoa
2 eggs
½ c buttermilk
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 tsp baking soda
1 recipe Frosting for Cinnamon Chocolate Cake - Deann
Instructions:
In a bowl, combine the first four ingredients. In a saucepan, combine the water, oil, butter and cocoa; bring just to a boil over medium heat. Pour over dry ingredients; mix well. Add eggs, buttermilk, vanilla and baking soda; mix well. Pour into a greased 15x10x1 baking pan.
Bake at 375 for 15-20 minutes or until a toothpick inserted near the center comes out clean. Please on a wire rack.

Ingredients:
2 c unsifted flour
2 c sugar
2 sticks butter
3 Tbsp. cocoa
1 c Coca-Cola
1/2 c buttermilk
1 tsp. baking soda
2 eggs, beaten
1 tsp. vanilla
1/4 tsp. salt
1 1/2 c small marshmallows
1 recipe Icing for Coca Cola Cake
Instructions: Sift flour and sugar into bowl. Heat butter, cocoa and coke to a boil; pour over flour and sugar mixture. Add milk, soda, eggs, salt and vanilla. Mix well. Add marshmallows. This will be a thin batter and marshmallows float on top. Bake in greased and lightly floured oblong pan at 350 for 30-35 minutes. Ice while hot.

Ingredients:
1 c butter
½ c shortening
3 c sugar
6 eggs
½ tsp. almond flavoring
1 tsp. coconut flavoring
3 c flour
1 c milk
1 3 ½ oz. can flaked coconut
Instructions:
Cream butter, shortening and sugar. Add eggs one at a time. Add flavorings. Sift flour, and add alternately with milk. Stir in coconut.
Bake at 350 degrees for about 1 hour and 15 minutes.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from South Central Bell
Ingredients:
1 pkg butter flavor cake mix plus 2 oz
2 c sugar
8 oz. sour cream
12 oz. frozen coconut, thawed
1 ½ c shipped cream or whipped topping
Instructions: Prepare cake, make 4 layers. Blend together the sugar, sour cream and coconut, chill. Spread all but 1 cup between the layers. Blend the remaining cup with the whipped cream and spread on top and sides. Seal in an airtight container and refrigerate for three days. Store in refrigerator.

Ingredients:
8 ounces semisweet chocolate, coarsely chopped
1 (16-ounce) can chocolate syrup
1 c butter, softened
2 c sugar
4 large eggs
2 ½ c all-purpose flour
½ tsp. baking soda
¼ tsp. salt
1 c buttermilk
1 tsp. vanilla extract
Instructions: Melt chocolate in a microwave-safe bowl at high for 30-second intervals until melted (about 1 ½ minutes total). Stir in chocolate syrup until smooth. Beat butter at medium speed with an electric mixer until creamy. Gradually add sugar, beating at medium speed until light and fluffy. Add eggs, 1 at a time, beating just until blended after each addition. Sift together flour, baking soda and salt. Add to butter mixture alternately with buttermilk, beginning and ending with flour mixture. Beat at low speed just until blended addition. Stir in vanilla and melted chocolate just until blended. Pour batter into a greased and floured 14-cup Bundt pan. Bake at 325 for 1 hour and 20 minutes or until a long wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool cake in pan on a wire rack 15 minutes; remove from pan to wire rack, and let cool 30 minutes or until completely cool.

Ingredients:
2 tsp. butter
1 Tbsp. plus 2 c flour
2 tsp. baking powder
1 1⁄2 tsp. fine salt
6 large eggs, at room temperature, separated
1 1⁄4 c sugar
1⁄2 c whole milk
1 1⁄2 Tbsp. dark rum (I substituted 1 Tbsp. water for the rum)
1 Tbsp. vanilla extract
1 14-ounce can sweetened condensed milk
1 12-ounce can evaporated milk
1 c heavy cream
1 16-ounce container Dulce de Leche (available mostly at Hispanic markets - you may find a 17.6-ounce container, but that's fine (I used an entire 17.6-ounce container, and felt like I needed it all))
Note that the Dulce de Leche is NOT the "La Lechera" Dulce de Leche flavor - that's a caramel flavored condensed milk in a can.
Instructions:
Heat the oven to 350°. Grease a 9" × 13" baking pan with the butter and dust with 1 Tbsp. of the flour. Invert the dish, tap out the excess flour, and set aside.
Sift the remaining flour, baking powder, and salt into a bowl and set aside. Put the egg whites into a large bowl and beat with an electric mixer fitted with a balloon whip (or with a paddle) on medium speed until soft peaks form, about 2 minutes. While the mixer is still running, add the sugar in a gradual stream and continue beating again to soft peaks, 3-5 minutes. Add the egg yolks one at a time, beating well after each addition. Alternately add the reserved flour mixture and the whole milk in 3 parts, beating until smooth after each addition. Add the rum and vanilla and beat again briefly until smooth. The batter will be quite wet.
Pour batter into reserved baking pan and bake until golden brown, about 30 minutes. The cake will puff slightly when baked. Remove from oven and cool on a cooling rack 30 minutes - the cake will still be a little warm.
Whisk together the sweetened condensed milk, evaporated milk, and heavy cream in a bowl (it’s a lot of liquid!). Using a knife, poke the cake with holes all over, penetrating to the bottom of the pan (I poked MANY holes, which is key for absorption - I'd say about 1/2 inch apart, all over the cake). Slowly pour the milk mixture by the cupful over the warm cake (you must pour slowly to allow the liquid to drain into the holes you've poked) and set aside to let cool completely, 30-45 minutes. There will be liquid visible around the edges of the cake.
Cover the cake with plastic wrap and refrigerate until well chilled and liquid is absorbed, at least 4 hours. Spread the Dulce de Leche across the top of the cake (I recommend starting in the corners and working toward the center - the top of the cake is wet and may peel as you spread the topping) and serve. (Can be served cold or at room temp).

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Duncan Hines
Ingredients:
1 box yellow cake mix plus 2 oz
1 20 oz. can crushed pineapple in syrup (undrained)
1 21 oz. can cherry pie filling
1 c chopped pecans
½ c butter
Instructions:
Dump undrained pineapple into greased 13x9 pan. Spread evenly. Dump in pie filling and spread into even layer. Dump dry cake mix onto cherry layer; spread evenly. Sprinkle pecans over cake mix. Put shredded butter over top.
Bake at 325 for 50-55 minutes.

Ingredients:
6 Tbsp. unsalted butter, plus more for pan
1 1/2 c semisweet chocolate chips (or 8 ounces chopped bittersweet chocolate)
6 large egg yolks
6 large egg whites
1/2 c granulated sugar
Instructions: Preheat oven to 275 with rack in center. Butter 1 9-inch spring form pan. Place butter and chocolate in large bowl. Microwave in 30-second increments, stirring each time, until melted; cool slightly. Whisk in yolks. In another bowl, beat egg whites to soft peaks. Gradually add granulated sugar; beat until stiff and glossy. Whisk 1/4 of whites into chocolate mixture; gently fold mixture into remaining whites. Pour into prepared pan; smooth top. Bake until cake pulls away from sides of pan and is just set in center, 45 to 50 minutes. Cool completely on a wire rack. Serve dusted with confectioners’ sugar.

Ingredients:
1 package German sweet chocolate
½ c boiling water
1 c buttermilk
1 c butter
2 c sugar
4 egg yolks, unbeaten
1 t vanilla
1 t baking soda
2 ½ c flour
4 egg whites, stiffly beaten
1 recipes Coconut Pecan Frosting for German Chocolate Cake
Instructions: Melt Chocolate in boiling water. Cool. Cream butter and sugar. Add egg yolks, one at a time; add melted chocolate and vanilla. Sift together dry ingredients and add alternately with buttermilk. Fold in egg whites. Bake in layers.
Related Recipes: Coconut Pecan Frosting for German Chocolate Cake

Ingredients:
1 box Duncan Hines Butter Recipe Golden plus 2 oz
1 egg
1/2 c (1 stick) butter, melted
1 (8-ounce) package cream cheese, softened
2 eggs
1 tsp. pure vanilla extract
1 (16-ounce) box confectioners' sugar
1/2 c (1 stick) butter, melted
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Lightly grease a 13x9x2-inch baking pan.
Instructions:
In the bowl of an electric mixer, combine cake mix, egg, and butter and mix well. Pat into the bottom of prepared pan and set aside.
Still using an electric mixer, beat cream cheese until smooth; add eggs and vanilla. Dump in confectioners' sugar and beat well. Reduce speed of mixer and slowly pour in butter. Mix well.
Pour filling onto cake mixture and spread evenly. Bake for 40 to 50 minutes. Remove from the oven and allow to cool completely. Cut into squares.

Ingredients:
1 c (2 sticks) butter, softened
2 ½ c all-purpose flour
Dash of salt
½ c Hershey’s syrup
1 c chopped pecans (Mama never added these)
1 ¼ c granulated sugar
6 (1.55 oz.) bars Hershey’s Milk Chocolate
¼ tsp. baking soda
1 c buttermilk or sour milk*
2 tsp. vanilla extract
Powdered sugar (optional) (Mama never used this)
Additional Hershey’s syrup (Mama never used this)
Instructions:
Heat oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour a 10-inch tube pan or 12-cup fluted tube pan. (Mama always used a Bundt)
Beat butter in large bowl until creamy; gradually add granulated sugar, beating on medium speed of mixer until well blended. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Add chocolate; beat until blended.
Stir together flour, baking soda and salt; add to chocolate mixture alternately with buttermilk, beating until blended. Add syrup and vanilla; beat until blended. Stir in pecans. Pour batter into prepared pan.
Bake 1 hour and 15 minutes or until wooden pick inserted in center of cake comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes; remove from pan to wire rack. Cool completely. Sift powdered sugar over top and drizzle with chocolate syrup, if desired. 12 to 16 servings.
*To sour milk: Use 1 tablespoon white vinegar plus milk to equal 1 cup.

Ingredients:
12 servings
2 cups sugar
1-3/4 cups all-purpose flour
3/4 cup HERSHEY'S Cocoa
1-1/2 teaspoons baking powder
1-1/2 teaspoons baking soda
1 teaspoon salt
2 eggs
1 cup milk
1/2 cup vegetable oil
2 teaspoons vanilla extract
1 cup boiling water
"PERFECTLY CHOCOLATE" CHOCOLATE FROSTING
Instructions:
Heat oven to 350°F. Grease and flour two 9-inch round baking pans. Stir together sugar, flour, cocoa, baking powder, baking soda and salt in large bowl. Add eggs, milk, oil and vanilla; beat on medium speed of mixer 2 minutes. Stir in boiling water (batter will be thin). Pour batter into prepared pans. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until wooden pick inserted in center comes out clean. Cool 10 minutes; remove from pans to wire racks. Cool completely. Frost with "PERFECTLY CHOCOLATE" CHOCOLATE FROSTING. Makes 12 servings.
VARIATIONS:
ONE-PAN CAKE: Grease and flour 13x9x2-inch baking pan. Heat oven to 350° F. Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake 35 to 40 minutes. Cool completely. Frost.
THREE LAYER CAKE: Grease and flour three 8-inch round baking pans. Heat oven to 350°F. Pour batter into prepared pans. Bake 30 to 35 minutes. Cool 10 minutes; remove from pans to wire racks. Cool completely. Frost.
BUNDT CAKE: Grease and flour 12-cup fluted tube pan. Heat oven to 350°F. Pour batter into prepared pan. Bake 50 to 55 minutes. Cool 15 minutes; remove from pan to wire rack. Cool completely. Frost.
CUPCAKES: Line muffin cups (2-1/2 inches in diameter) with paper bake cups. Heat oven to 350°F. Fill cups 2/3 full with batter. Bake 22 to 25 minutes. Cool completely. Frost. About 30 cupcakes.

Ingredients:
1 box butter recipe golden plus 2 oz
1 box instant vanilla pudding (4 servings)
½ cup vegetable oil
8 oz. can drained crushed pineapple
Reserved juice plus enough water for 1 cup
4 eggs
1 tsp. cinnamon
¼ cup chopped cherries
½ cut up banana
½ c chopped nuts
Instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Prepare Bundt pan. Combine cake mix, pudding, oil, pineapple, juice mixture, eggs and cinnamon. Beat at low speed with mixer until moistened. Beat at medium speed for 2 minutes. Stir in banana, pecans and cherries. Pour into pan. Bake 50 to 60 minutes or until done. Cool in pan 25 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 c chopped pecans
1 package yellow cake mix plus 2 oz
1 large package instant vanilla pudding mix
4 eggs
¼ c water
½ c vegetable oil
¾ c Irish cream liqueur
½ c butter
¼ c water
1 c white sugar
¼ c Irish cream liqueur
Instructions: Preheat oven to 325 degrees. Grease and flour a 10 inch Bundt pan. Sprinkle chopped nuts evenly over bottom of pan. In a large bowl, combine cake mix and pudding mix. Mix in eggs, ¼ c water, ½ c oil and ¾ c Irish cream liqueur. Beat for 5 minutes at high speed. Pour batter over nuts in pan. Bake in the preheated oven for 60 minutes, or until a toothpick inserted into the cake comes out clean. Cool for 10 minutes in the pan, then invert onto the serving dish. Prick top and sides of cake. Spoon glaze over top and brush onto sides of cake. Allow to absorb glaze repeat until all glaze is used up. To make the glaze. In a saucepan, combine butter, ¼ c water and 1 c sugar. Bring to a boil and continue boiling for 5 minutes, stirring constantly. Remove from heat and stir in ¼ c Irish cream.

Ingredients:
For the Cupcakes:
1 cup stout (such as Guinness)
1 cup (2 sticks) unsalted butter
¾ cup unsweetened cocoa powder (preferably Dutch process)
2 cups all-purpose flour
2 cups sugar
1 ½ tsp. baking soda
¾ tsp. salt
2 large eggs
2/3 cup sour cream
1 recipe Ganache for Irish Trinity Cupcakes – Smitten Kitchen
1 recipe Frosting for Irish Trinity Cupcakes – Smitten Kitchen
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Line 24 cupcake cups with liners. Bring 1 cup stout and 1 cup butter to simmer in heavy large saucepan over medium heat. Add cocoa powder and whisk until mixture is smooth. Cool slightly.
Whisk flour, sugar, baking soda, and ¾ tsp. salt in large bowl to blend. Using electric mixer, beat eggs and sour cream in another large bowl to blend. Add stout-chocolate mixture to egg mixture and beat just to combine. Add flour mixture and beat briefly on slow speed. Using rubber spatula, fold batter until completely combined. Divide better among cupcake liners, filling them 2/3 to ¾ of the way. Bake cake until tester inserted into center comes out clean, rotating them once front to back if your oven bakes unevenly, about 27 minutes. Cool cupcakes on a rack completely.
Let the ganache cool until thick but still soft enough to be piped (the fridge will speed this along but you must stir it every 10 minutes). Meanwhile using your 1-inch round cookie cutter or an apple corer, cut the centers out of the cooled cupcakes. You want to go most of the way down the cupcake but not cut through the bottom – aim for 2/3rds of the way down. A slim spoon or grapefruit knife will help you get the center out. Those are your tasters. Put the ganache into a piping bag with a wide tip and fill the holes in each cupcake to the top.
Ice and decorate the cupcakes.

Ingredients:
1 stick butter
1 c vegetable oil
1 c sugar
5 whole eggs (separated)
3 tsp. vanilla
1 c coconut
2 c all-purpose flour
1 tsp. baking soda
1 tsp. baking powder
1 c buttermilk
1 recipe Cream Cheese Frosting for Pioneer Woman’s Italian Cream Cake
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease and flour three 9-inch round cake pans. Beat egg whites until stiff. Set aside.
In a large bowl, cream together butter, oil, and sugar until light and fluffy. Mix in the egg yolks, vanilla, and coconut.
In a separate bowl, mix flour, baking soda, and baking powder.
Alternate adding buttermilk and dry ingredients to wet ingredients. Mix until just combined, and then fold in egg whites.
Pour evenly into the three prepare pans, and then sprinkle the top of each pan with 1 Tbsp. sugar.
Bake in preheated oven to 20-25 minutes, or until toothpick comes out clean.
Remove from oven and allow to cool for 15 minutes, then turn the cakes out onto cooling racks and allow to cool completely.
Related Recipes: Cream Cheese Frosting for Italian Cream Cake

Ingredients:
½ c unsalted butter, at room temperature
1 c vegetable oil
1 c + 3 Tbsp. sugar
5 eggs, separated
3 tsp. vanilla
1 c sweetened, flaked coconut
2 c all-purpose flour
1 tsp. baking soda
1 tsp. baking powder
¼ tsp. salt
1 c buttermilk
1 recipe Frosting and Filling for Italian Cream Cake – Billie’s
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350. Spray three 9” round cake pans with baking spray or grease and flour.
Beat egg whites in a clean mixing bowl, increasing speed from low to medium high until you reach stiff peaks. Set aside.
In a large mixing bowl, cream together butter, oil, and 1 c sugar until light and fluffy, about two minutes. Mix in the egg yolks and vanilla and mix until well incorporated. Add in the coconut and mix to combine. In a separate bowl, combine flour, baking soda, baking powder and salt and whisk together.
Alternate adding flour mixture in three additions and buttermilk in two additions to the batter, beating until just incorporated after each addition. Once complete, fold in egg white. Fold in 1/3 of the egg whites first to lighten the batter, and then gently fold in the remaining egg whites.
Divide batter evenly among pans; sprinkle the tops with one Tbsp. of sugar each. Bake approximately 20 minutes until a toothpick inserted in the center of each comes out clean. Tops should be golden brown. Cool in pans about 10 minutes, run a knife around the edge to loosen and invert onto a rack covered with wax or parchment paper. Flip right side up and let cool completely.
Related Recipes: Frosting and Filling for Italian Cream Cake

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Betty Crocker
Ingredients:
3 eggs
1 c sugar
1/3 c water
1 t vanilla
¾ c plain flour
1 t baking powder
Instructions: Beat eggs for 5 minutes. Gradually beat in sugar. Blend in water and vanilla. Gradually add dry ingredients. Bake in parchment paper lined jellyroll pan at 350 for 15 minutes. While hot, roll cake up in confectioner’s sugar coated towel. Cool, unroll, spread with jelly, roll up and sprinkle with confectioner’s sugar.

Ingredients:
3 c flour
1 tsp. baking powder
1 ½ c butter
2 ¾ c sugar
1 tsp. vanilla
6 large eggs
¼ c milk
1 20 oz. can crushed pineapple in syrup
1 ½ c confectioners’ sugar
Instructions:
Cream butter and sugar. Beat in eggs, 1 at a time, add vanilla. Stir in flour mixture alternately with milk and ¾ c undrained pineapple. Bake in greased and floured pan at 325 degrees for about 1 hour and 25 minutes.
Blend confectioners’ sugar and ¼ c soft butter. Stir in remaining pineapple, well drained. Spoon this mixture over hot cake.
Use a lemon cake mix in place of the yellow cake. Add the juice (approximately 1/4 c) and zest of 2 lemons to the cream cheese filling. Proceed: as directed above.

Ingredients:
3 sticks butter
2 ½ c sugar
4 eggs
3 ½ c flour
2 tbsp. lemon extract
1 c buttermilk
½ tsp. soda in 1 tbsp. hot water (add to buttermilk)
1 recipe Glaze for Lemon Pound Cake – Rose Marie Luke
Instructions:
Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs, one at a time. Add extract then dry ingredients alternately with buttermilk.
Bake at 325 degrees for about 1 ½ hours.

Ingredients:
Duncan Hines Butter Recipe any flavor plus 2 oz
1/2 c sugar
4 eggs
2/3 c oil
1/4 c water
8 ounces sour cream
Vanilla
Instructions: Mix and bake at 350 for an hour.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Rosalie French
Ingredients:
¾ c butter
2 c sugar
3 whole eggs or 6 whites
2 tsp. baking powder
3 c flour
1 c milk
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions:
Cream shortening and sugar, add eggs and vanilla.
Sift together dry ingredients and add alternately with milk. (If using egg whites, beat and fold in last.)

Ingredients:
1 pkg yellow cake mix
½ c chunk-style peanut butter
1 16 ½ oz. can chocolate frosting
1/3 c chunk-style peanut butter
Instructions:
Prepare cake mix according to directions adding the ½ cup peanut butter.
When ready to frost, blend frosting with the ½ cup peanut butter.

Ingredients:
1 pkg yellow cake mix plus 2 oz
1 pkg instant pistachio pudding
4 eggs
1 c sour cream
¾ c water
¼ c oil
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions:
Add all ingredients and mix according to directions on box.
1 c pecans
¾ c sugar
2 tbsp. cinnamon
Mix and spread through batter.
Bake according to directions on box.

Follow the original recipe, adding a 15-ounce, can of pumpkin pie filling and an extra egg to the cream cheese filling. Bake as usual, remove from oven, and allow to cool. Cut into squares and top each square with a pecan half. Serve with a dollop of fresh whipped cream.

Ingredients:
3 eggs
1 c white sugar
2/3 c canned pumpkin
1 tsp. lemon juice
¾ c all-purpose flour
2 tsp. ground cinnamon
1 tsp. baking powder
½ tsp. salt
¼ tsp. ground nutmeg
1 c chopped walnuts
6 ounces cream cheese, softened
1 c confectioners’ sugar
¼ c butter, softened
½ tsp. vanilla extract
Instructions: Preheat oven to 375 degrees. In a mixing bowl, beat eggs on high for 5 minutes. Gradually beat in white sugar until thick and lemon-colored. Add pumpkin and lemon juice. In another bowl combine flour, cinnamon, baking powder, salt, and nutmeg; fold into the pumpkin mixture. Grease a 15x10x1 inch baking pan; line with waxed paper. Grease and flour the paper. Spread batter into pan; sprinkle with walnuts. Bake at 375 degrees for 15 minutes or until cake springs back when lightly touched. Immediately turn out onto a linen towel dusted with confectioners’ sugar. Peel off paper and roll cake up in the towel, starting with the short end. Cool. Meanwhile, in a mixing bowl, beat cream cheese, 1 c confectioners’ sugar, butter, and vanilla until fluffy. Carefully unroll the cake. Spread filling over cake to within 1 inch of edges. Roll up again. Cover and chill until serving. Dust with additional confectioners’ sugar, if desired.

Ingredients:
Duncan Hines Dark Chocolate Fudge Cake Mix plus 2 oz
Small fudge chocolate instant pudding
8 ounces sour cream
4 eggs
1/2 c warm water
1/2 c cooking oil
12 ounces Ghirardelli 60% chocolate chips
1 tub dark chocolate fudge icing
Vegetable spray
Instructions: Mix cake mix, pudding mix, sour cream, eggs, warm water and cooking oil. Beat for four minutes. Fold in chocolate chips. Bake 1 hour at 325 degrees in tube pan which has been sprayed with vegetable oil. Melt icing in microwave and drizzle over cake.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Sue Luke Lewis
Ingredients:
1/2 c shortening
2 eggs
2 Tbsp. cocoa
1 tsp. soda
1 Tbsp. vinegar
1 1/2 c sugar
2 ounces red food coloring
2 1/2 c flour
1 c buttermilk
1 tsp. vanilla
1 recipe Cream Cheese Frosting for Red Velvet Cake
Instructions: Cream sugar, shortening and eggs. Make paste with food coloring and cocoa and add to mixture. Add flour alternately with buttermilk and vanilla. Add soda and vinegar, just blend.
Related Recipes: Cream Cheese Frosting for Red Velvet Cake

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Bell’s Best
Ingredients:
1 stick butter
3 eggs
1pkg yellow cake mix plus 2 oz
1 c pecans, chopped
8 oz. pkg cream cheese
1 lb. box powdered sugar
1 tbsp. vanilla extract
Instructions:
Dump cake mix, softened butter and 1 egg in bowl. Mix well and pour in 9 X 13 pan. Press in chopped pecans.
Mix cream cheese, powdered sugar and 2 eggs with mixer until creamy. Add vanilla and pour over cake mixture. Bake at 325 degrees for about 35 minutes.

Ingredients:
2 sticks butter
3 c sugar
3 c flour
¼ tsp. soda
1 8 oz. carton sour cream
2 tsp. vanilla
6 eggs
Instructions:
Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs one at a time. Blend in flour and soda.
Add sour cream and vanilla; mix well.
Bake at 325 degrees for about 1 ½ hours.

Ingredients:
½ pound (2 sticks) butter, softened
½ c granulated sugar
¾ c brown sugar
½ tsp. baking soda
¼ tsp. salt
2 eggs
2 c flour (plus more for pressing out the layers)
½ c mini chocolate chips (plus more for garnish)
4 c heavy cream, divided
4 Tbsp. powdered sugar, divided
1 c Tia Maria coffee liqueur
2 tsp. instant coffee
12 mocha beans for garnish
Instructions:
Arrange two racks in the middle third of the oven, and preheat oven to 350 degrees. Grease and line four 8-inch cake pans with parchment paper, then lightly spray with pan coating.
Cream butter, granulated sugar, brown sugar, baking soda and salt until light. Add one egg and continue beating on high speed until fluffy. Repeat with second egg. Add vanilla, flour and chocolate chips, mixing until just incorporated.
Divide dough among the four pans using an ice cream scoop for even distribution. Have a small bowl of flour nearby to generously coat fingers and press out each layer, using more flour as needed.
Bake layers until golden brown, 30-35 minutes. Do not overtake. Let cakes cool in the pans before turning out onto a lightly greased wire rack to finish cooling. Peel off and discard parchment.
Whip 2 c of the cream with 2 Tbsp. of the powdered sugar until stiff.
Brush the bottom side of one cake layer with coffee liqueur until moistened. Flip onto a serving plate and brush top with more liqueur. Evenly spread one third of the whipped cream across the top of the layer. Repeat with the second and third layers. Flip the fourth layer on top of the third and brush with liqueur.
Wrap the top and sides of the torte with plastic wrap and refrigerate 8 hours or overnight.
Whip the remaining 2 c of cream, remaining 2 Tbsp. powdered sugar and instant coffee until stiff. Spread onto top and sides of torte. Press chocolate chips onto sides. With a knife, score the torte into quarters and each quarter into thirds. Place the remaining cream in a piping bag fitted with a star tip and pipe a rosette onto each of the 12 portions. If desired, top each rosette with a mocha bean.

Source: Rose Marie Luke, Mammy Lehmann’s from Fayette’s Favorite Recipes
Ingredients:
1 c butter
2 c sugar
3 c flour
1 c milk
2 tsp. baking powder
1 tsp. almond or vanilla
8 egg whites
Instructions:
Cream butter and sugar. Sift together dry ingredients. Add alternately with milk. Add flavoring and fold in beaten egg whites.
Bake in greased layer pans in moderate oven.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Better Homes and Gardens
Ingredients:
¾ c shortening
1 1/3 c sugar
1 ½ tsp. vanilla
2 ½ c sifted cake flour
3 tsp. baking powder
1 c milk
5 egg whites
1/3 c sugar
Cream shortening and sugar, add vanilla.
Instructions:
Sift flour and baking powder. Add alternately with milk
Beat egg whites, add 1/3 c sugar gradually, stir into batter.
Bake in layers.

Ingredients:
1 1/2 c graham cracker crumbs
3 tbsp sugar
1/3 c butter, melted
4 pkg (8 oz) of cream cheese, softened
1 c sugar
1 tsp vanilla
4 eggs
Instructions:
Heat oven to 325. Mix graham crumbs, 3 tbsp sugar and butter; press onto bottom of 9-inch- springform pan. Beat cream cheese, 1 cup sugar and vanilla with mixer until blended. Add eggs, 1 at a time, mixing on low speed after each just until blended. Pour over crust. Bake 55 minutes or until center is almost set. Loosen cake from rim of pan; cool before removing rim. Refrigerate 4 hours.
Note: Reduce oven temperature to 300 if using a dark springform pan.

Source: All Day I Dream About Food
Ingredients:
Crust:
1 1/4 c almond flour
1/4 c cocoa powder
1/4 c Swerve Sweetener
3 tbsp butter, melted
Filling:
6 oz dark chocolate, chopped
1 tbsp butter
24 oz cream cheese, softened
1/2 c Swerve Sweetener
1/2 c powdered Swerve Sweetener
1 tsp vanilla
3 large eggs, room temperature
1/4 c cocoa powder
1/3 c heavy cream, room temperature
2 tsp melted butter for brushing sides of pan
Topping:
3/4 c whipping cream
1/3 c powdered Swerve Sweetener
3 oz dark chocolate, finely chopped
1/2 tsp vanilla
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 325. In a medium bowl, whisk together the almond flour, cocoa powder, and sweetener. Stir in melted butter until well combined. Press mixture firmly into the bottom of a 9-inch springform pan. Bake 10 to 12 minutes, and then remove and reduce oven temperature to 300.
In a medium saucepan over low heat, melt dark chocolate with butter, stirring until smooth. Set aside to let cool. In a large bowl, beat cream cheese until smooth. Beat in sweeteners and vanilla extract, then beat in eggs one at a time, scraping down beaters and sides of bowl as needed. Beat in cocoa powder and heavy cream until well combined, then beat in melted chocolate until mixture is completely smooth. Brush sides of springform pan with melted butter, taking care not to disturb the crust. Pour filling into pan and gently shake from side to side to even out. Bake 55 to 60 minutes, or until filling is mostly set but still jiggles just a little in the center. Remove and let cool 15 minutes, then run a sharp knife around the inside of the pan to loosen, Let cool completely. Once cool, remove sides, cover tightly in plastic wrap and refrigerate at least 3 hours.
In a medium saucepan over medium heat, combine ream and sweetener. Bring to just a simmer, then remove from heat and add chopped chocolate and vanilla. Let sit 5 minutes and then whisk until smooth. Pour over the top of the chilled cheesecake, allowing some to drip down the sides. Child until set.

Ingredients:
Crust:
1 1/2 c almond flour
1 tbsp granulated sweetener
3 tbsp butter, melted
Filling:
16 oz cream cheese, room temperature
1/2 c + 2 tbsp granulated Swerve
1/4 c heavy whipping cream
1/2 tsp vanilla
1 tsp orange zest
Zest of 1 small lemon
3 eggs, room temperature
Top Layer:
1/2 c sour cream or Greek yogurt
2 tsp granulated Swerve
Instructions:
Grease an 8 inch push pan inside and place a round of parchment in the bottom and grease that too. Put the rack in the bottom of the instant pot and add 1 1/2 c water to the bottom of the pot.
Mix together the crust ingredients and press evenly in the bottom of the pan and slightly up the sides of the pan.
Blend the cream cheese, Swerve, cream, vanilla, orange and lemon zest together until smooth. Add eggs, one at a time only until blended, Overmixed eggs makes a funky cheesecake texture. Literally seconds is all it takes. Pour the batter into the pan and over the crust. Lay a paper towel over the top of the pan and a piece of aluminum foil the size of the towel over the towel and gently crimp the foil around the pan to hold the paper towel the rack out and set the pan on the rack and lower the whole thing in. Seal the lid and set the Manual control for 35 minutes.
After the cook time, let it natural release for 18 minutes and then release the remaining steam. The foil on top of the cheesecake will be moist. Be careful to keep the water out of the cheesecake as you lift that rack out of the pot. The cheesecake will have a slight jiggle. Pour the sour cream topping mixture over the top of the cake. cover with a fresh, dry paper towel. You can reuse the foil if you dry it off good. Refrigerate the covered cheesecake 8 hours or overnight. Run a butter knife around the inside of the pan between the sides and the cheesecake. Push up from the bottom. You can use a spatula gently slide between the crust and parchment to move the cheesecake to a serving platter, or just leave the parchment and bottom of the cheesecake as is to serve.

Ingredients:
1 1/4 c finely crushed coconut bar cookies
1/4 c butter, melted
3 tbsp sugar
2 pkgs (8 oz each) cream cheese, softened
1 can (14 oz) sweetened condensed milk
1/2 tsp grated lime peel
1/3 c lime juice
few drops green food coloring (optional)
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Mix crumbs, butter and sugar; press firmly onto bottom and up side of 9-inch pie plat. Bake 10 minutes. cool.
Beat cream cheese and sweetened condensed milk in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Add peel, juice and food coloring; mix well. Pour into crust.
Refrigerate at least 8 hours or overnight. Store leftover pie in refrigerator.

Ingredients:
1 1/2 c graham cracker crumbs
1/3 c sugar
1/3 c butter, melted
3 pkg (8 oz) cream cheese, softened
1 c sugar
1 c sour cream
3 eggs
grated peel and juice from 1 lemon
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 325 degrees if using a silver 9-inch springform pan (or to 300 degrees if using a dark nonstick 9-inch springform pan. Mix graham crumbs, 1/3 cup sugar and butter. Reserve 1/2 cup crumb mixture; press remaining crumb mixture firmly onto bottom of pan. Set aside.
Beat cream cheese and 1 cup sugar in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Add sour cream; mix well. Add eggs, 1 at a time, mixing on low speed after each addition just until blended. Stir in lemon peel and juice. Pour over crust; sprinkle with reserved crumb mixture.
Bake 45 to 50 minutes or until center is almost set. Run knife or metal spatula around rim of pan to loosen cake; cool before removing rim. Refrigerate at least 4 hours or overnight. Store leftover cheesecake in refrigerator.

Notes: 16 servings - with crust fat 19, carb 5, protein 6; without crust fat 11, carb 2, protein 3
Ingredients:
Crust
1 1/2 c almond meal
1/2 stick melted butter
1 tsp vanilla
1/4 c stevia
Filling
3 8 oz packages cream cheese, room temperature
3 tsp pumpkin pie spice
1/2 tsp salt
1 1/2 c stevia
1 can (15 oz) pumpkin
1 tbsp vanilla
5 eggs, room temperature
1/2 c heavy cream
Instructions:
Heat oven to 325. Combine ingredients for crust and press into a springform pan. Bake 8 minutes.
Beat cream cheese until fluffy. Scrape sides of bowl and beaters. This step will be repeated several times and is important. The mixture will gradually become lighter, and the denser stuff has a tendency to cling to the bowl. You won't be able to incorporate it as well later, so keep scraping. Add spices and sweetener. Beat again, scrape again. Add pumpkin and vanilla. Beat well, scrape. Add 3 eggs. Beat well (about a minute), scrape. Add the other 2 eggs and cream and beat another minute. Pour mixture into pan over crust.
Bake until cake is firm to touch but slightly soft in the center, or the center reaches 150 to 155, remove from oven. Approximately 60 minutes. Run knife around pan and loosen sides. Wait about 5 or 10 minutes. Removes sides from pan. Let the cheesecake cool to room temperature, or up to 3 hours. Cover and chill, ideally for another 3 to 4 hours.

Notes: Prep time 5 minutes; Total time 5 minutes; Serves 12
Ingredients:
2 8 oz packages cream cheese
1 15 oz can pure pumpkin puree (not pumpkin pie filling)
2 c heavy cream
pinch salt
2 tsp pumpkin pie spice (we use Alton Brown's)
1-2 tsp vanilla liquid stevia or to taste (I used 1 1/2 tsp liquid splenda)
1 tsp vanilla extract
Optional toppings: cacao nibs or Sukrim Gold Brown Sugar Substitute
Instructions: In a stand mixer blend cream cheese and pumpkin until smooth. Add the rest of the ingredients and blend until whipped and fluffy, about 5 minutes. Taste and adjust sweetener to your liking, if needed. Pipe into serving glasses and top with cacao nibs or brown sugar sub like Sukrin, if desired. Keep refrigerated until ready to serve.

Ingredients:
2 c graham cracker crumbs
1/2 c butter, melted
2 tbsp sugar
4 pkgs (8 oz) cream cheese, softened
1 1/3 c sugar
2 tbsp corn starch
1 tbsp vanilla
3 eggs
2 c sour cream
Instructions: In small bowl toss together graham crumbs, margarine or butter and 2 tablespoons sugar. Reserve 2 tablespoons of crumb mixture for garnish. Press remaining crumb mixture onto bottom and 2 1/4-inches up sides of 9-inch springform pan. Chill in freezer while preparing filling. In large mixing bowl beat cream cheese on medium speed of electric mixer until fluffy. Add 1 1/3 cups sugar, cornstarch and vanilla. Beat until combined. Add eggs, one at a time, beating until just combined after each addition. Stir in sour cream. Pour into crust. Bake at 325°F about 1 1/4 hours or until center is almost set. Cool on wire rack for 15 minutes. Loosen sides of pan. Continue cooling on wire rack for 45 minutes more. Sprinkle top with reserved crumbs. Refrigerate at least 3 hours. Garnish as desired. Store in refrigerator.

Recipe Name: Philadelphia Cream Cheese Tips for the Perfect Cheesecake
Instructions:
Preheating the oven: The baking time indicated in a recipe is based on using a preheated oven. Turn the oven on when you start to mix the cheesecake ingredients. This should allow enough time for the oven to heat to the correct temperature for when you are ready to place the cheesecake in the oven to bake. Unless otherwise indicated, always bake cheesecakes in the center of the middle oven rack.
How to soften cream cheese: Place unwrapped package of cream cheese in microwaveable bowl. Microwave on high 15 seconds or just softened. Add 15 seconds for each additional package of cheese.
Beating the batter: While adding ingredients, do not overbeat the cheesecake batter. Too much air beaten into the batter will result in a cheesecake that sinks in the center when cooled.
Baking cheesecakes: Overbaked cheesecakes tend to crack. Remove the cheesecake from the oven when the center is almost set (i.e. center of cheesecake still wiggles when the pan is gently shaken from side-to-side). Although the cheesecake appears underbaked, the residual heat in the cheesecake will be enough to finish baking the center. After chilling, the cheesecake will have a perfectly smooth consistency.
Cooling cheesecakes: Cool cheesecakes completely before refrigerating. Placing a warm cheesecake in the refrigerator will cause condensation to form on the cake, resulting in a soggy cheesecake.
How to bake in a springform pan: Preheat oven to 325 degrees if using a silver 9-inch springform pan (or to 300 degrees if using a dark nonstick 9-inch springform pan). Prepare crust as directed; press firmly onto bottom of pan. Prepare filling as directed; pour over crust. Bake 1 hour or until center is almost set. Run small knife or small spatula around rim of pan to loosen cake; cool before removing rim of pan. Refrigerate 4 hours or overnight.
Cutting cheesecakes: Cut cheesecakes when they are cold rather than warm. Use a sharp knife with a clean, thin blade. to make clean cuts, dip the knife in hot water after each cut and wipe the blade clean.

Ingredients:
2 c graham cracker crumbs
½ c finely ground pecans
2 Tbsp. light brown sugar
2 tsp. ground cinnamon
8 Tbsp. salted butter, melted
3 (8-ounce) packages of cream cheese, at room temperature
2 c cooked, mashed sweet potatoes
3 eggs plus 1 egg yolk
¼ c sour cream
1 ½ c sugar
1 tsp. ground cinnamon
2 Tbsp. all-purpose flour
1 tsp. vanilla extract
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
Instructions:
Combine graham cracker crumbs, pecans, brown sugar and cinnamon. Add melted butter. Press crust into bottom and sides of spring form pan.
Beat cream cheese until smooth. Add sweet potatoes, eggs, egg yolk, sour cream, sugar, cinnamon, flour and vanilla. Beat together until well combined.
Pour cream cheese mixture into crust. Spread out evenly and place in oven for 1 hour. Remove from oven and let sit for 15 minutes. Cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate for 4 hours.

Ingredients:
2 packages (8-ounce) cream cheese, softened
½ c sugar
½ tsp. vanilla
2 eggs
3 squares (1 ounce each) Baker’s Premium White Baking Chocolate, melted
1 prepared chocolate flavor crumb crust (6-ounce or 9-inch)
3 Tbsp. red raspberry preserves
Instructions:
Mix cream cheese, sugar and vanilla with electric mixer on medium speed until well blended. Add eggs; mix until blended. Stir in white chocolate.
Pour into crust. Microwave preserves in small microwavable bowl on high 15 seconds or until melted. Dot top of cheesecake with small spoonful of preserves.
Cut through top of batter with knife several times to marbleize.
Bake at 350 degrees for 35 to 40 minutes or until center is almost set. Cool. Refrigerate 3 hours or overnight. Store leftover cheesecake in refrigerator. Makes 8 servings.

Ingredients:
1 c butter
2/3 c sugar
1 egg
2 tsp vanilla
1/2 tsp salt
2 1/4 c all purpose flour
Heat oven to 400°F.
Instructions:
Combine all ingredients except flour in bowl. Beat at medium speed until creamy. Add flour; beat at low speed until well mixed. Prepare dough as directed by variations below, if desired. (If dough is too soft, cover and refrigerate, 30-45 minutes.)
Place dough into cookie press fitted with template. Form desired shapes, 1 inch apart, onto ungreased cookie sheets.
Bake 6-8 minutes or until edges are lightly browned.
Variations:
Chocolate Chip Spritz: Add 1/4 cup coarsely grated semi-sweet chocolate.
Eggnog-Glazed Spritz: Add 1 teaspoon ground nutmeg. Glaze: Stir together 1 cup powdered sugar, 1/4 cup butter, softened, 2 tablespoons water and 1/4 teaspoon rum extract in small bowl until smooth. Drizzle over warm cookies.
Lebkuchen Spice Spritz: Add 1 teaspoon ground cinnamon, 1 teaspoon ground nutmeg, 1/2 teaspoon ground allspice and 1/4 teaspoon ground cloves. Glaze: Stir together 1 cup powdered sugar, 2 tablespoons milk and 1/2 teaspoon vanilla in small bowl until smooth. Drizzle over warm cookies.
Mint Kisses: Add 1/4 teaspoon peppermint extract. Immediately after removing cookies from oven place 1 chocolate candy kiss on each cookie.

Ingredients:
1 c butter, softened
½ c powdered sugar
½ c sugar
1 egg
½ tsp. vanilla
2 ¼ c all-purpose flour
½ tsp. cream of tartar
½ tsp. baking soda
Instructions:
Heat oven to 375 degrees. Combine butter, powdered sugar and ½ c sugar in large bowl. Beat at medium speed, scraping bowl often, until creamy. Add egg and vanilla; continue beating until well mixed. Reduce speed to low; add flour, cream of tartar and baking soda. Beat until dough forms a ball.
Shape dough into 1-inch balls. Place 2 inches apart onto ungreased cookie sheets. Flatten to about 1 ½ inches with bottom of glass dipped in sugar, sprinkle with decorator sugars, if desired. Bake for 7 to 9 minutes or until edges are lightly browned. Let stand 1 minute; remove from cookie sheets.

Ingredients:
2 1/2 c all-purpose flour
3/4 tsp ground cardamom
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
1/2 tsp kosher salt
1 1/2 c powdered sugar, divided
1 c pecans
1 c unsalted butter, room temperature
1 tbsp vanilla extract
Instructions:
Arrange racks in lower and upper thirds of oven; preheat to 350 degrees. Line 2 baking sheets with parchment paper. Whisk first 4 ingredients in a medium bowl. Combine 1/2 c sugar and pecans in a food processor; pulse until coarse meal forms. Using an electric mixer, beat butter and vanilla in a medium bowl until creamy 2-3 minutes. Add nut mixture; beat to blend. Add dry ingredients; blend well (dough will be moist but still crumbly). Transfer to a work surface; knead to form a ball, about 4 turns.
Measure 1 rounded tablespoon of dough; form into a ball, then roll into a 1 1/2-inch log. Gently bend into a crescent shape, pinching ends to taper (cookies may crack slightly). Repeat with remaining dough, spacing about 1-inch apart on prepared sheets.
Bake, rotating sheets halfway through baking, until bottoms are golden, 12-15 minutes. Sift remaining 1 cup powdered sugar into a shallow wide bowl. Working in batches of about 8 cookies each, roll warm cookies gently in powdered sugar to coat. Transfer to a wire rack to let cool. Roll or dust cooled cookies with powdered sugar.

Notes: Fragrant with the classic flavors of chai tea, these buttery, crumbly cookies are perfect for dunking in a c of tea or coffee. Makes about 4 dozen cookies.
Ingredients:
1 c butter, softened
1/2 c powdered sugar
2 c all-purpose flour
1 ½ tsp ground cardamom
1 ½ tsp ground ground allspice
1 tsp ground cinnamon
1 tsp ground nutmeg
1/2 tsp ground ginger
1/2 tsp ground cloves
1/2 tsp salt
4 tsp vanilla
2 egg yolks
Coating
1 1/2 c powdered sugar
1/2 tsp ground cardamom
1/2 tsp ground cinnamon
Instructions:
Heat oven to 350 degrees. In large bowl, beat butter and 1/2 cup powdered sugar with electric mixer on low speed until blended. Stir in remaining cookie ingredients.
Shape dough by tablespoonfuls into balls. On ungreased cookie sheets, place balls 1 1/2 inches apart.
Bake 12 to 15 minutes or until very lightly browned. Remove from cookie sheets to cooling rack; cool 5 minutes.
In medium bowl, mix coating ingredients. Working in batches, gently roll warm cookies in coating mixture. Cool on cooling rack 5 minutes. Roll in mixture again.

Ingredients:
1 package Devil’s Food Cake Mix
¾ c crunchy peanut butter
2 large eggs
2 Tbsp. milk
1 c candy-coated peanut butter pieces
Instructions: Preheat oven to 350. Grease cookie sheets. Combine cake mix, peanut butter, eggs and milk in large mixing bowl. Beat at low speed with electric mixer until blended. Stir in peanut butter pieces. Drop dough by slightly rounded Tbsp. onto prepared cookie sheets. Bake 7 to 9 minutes or until lightly browned. Cool 2 minutes on cookie sheets. Remove to cooling racks.

Ingredients:
1 cup creamy peanut butter
¾ cup sugar
1 large egg (lightly beaten)
½ tsp. baking soda
¼ tsp. salt
¾ cup semisweet chocolate chips
½ cup roasted salted peanuts
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees with racks in upper and lower thirds. In a large bowl, stir together first 5 ingredients until well combined. Stir in chocolate chips and peanuts (if desired).
