With moistened hands, roll dough, about 1 heaping tbsp. at a time into balls. Please 2 inches apart on two baking sheets.
Bake until cookies are golden and puffed, 12 to 14 minutes, rotating sheets from top to bottom and front to back halfway through. Cool 5 minutes on sheets; transfer cookies to racks to cool completely.

Ingredients:
Nonstick vegetable oil spray
1 c bittersweet chocolate chips (do not exceed 61% cacao; about 6 oz), divided
1 1/2 c powdered sugar, divided
1/3 c unsweetened cocoa powder
2 tsp cornstarch
2 large egg whites, room temperature
1/2 tsp vanilla extract
1/8 tsp cream of tartar
Instructions:
Position 1 rack in top third and 1 rack in bottom third of oven and preheat to 350 degrees. Coat 2 large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper or cooking spray. Place 1/2 cup chocolate chips in small microwave-safe bowl. Cook in 15-second intervals until chocolate softens; stir until melted and smooth. Cool chocolate to lukewarm, about 10 minutes. Whisk 1/2 c sugar, cocoa, and cornstarch in small bowl to blend.
Using electric mixer, beat room-temperature egg whites, vanilla, 1/2 teaspoon salt, and cream of tartar in medium bowl until soft peaks form. Add remaining 1 cup sugar in 4 additions, beating just to blend after each addition. Continue to beat until meringue is thick and glossy like marshmallow creme, about 2 minutes longer. Beat in cocoa mixture. Fold in melted chocolate, then 1/2 cup chips.
Drop batter by rounded tablespoonfuls onto prepare sheets, spacing 3 inches apart. Bake cookies 7 minutes. Reverse sheets and bake until dry looking and cracked, about 6 minutes. Cool cookies on sheets 5 minutes. Transfer cookies to racks and cool completely.

Ingredients:
3/4 c shortening
1 c brown sugar
1 egg
1/4 c molasses
2 1/4 c flour
2 tsp. baking soda
1 tsp. cinnamon
1 tsp. ginger
1/2 tsp. cloves
1/4 tsp. salt
Granulated sugar
Instructions: Cream shortening, brown sugar, egg and molasses. Mix in remaining ingredients except granulated sugar. Cover and chill 1 hour. Heat oven to 375. Shape dough by rounded teaspoonful into balls. Dip tops in granulated sugar. Place balls sugared side up 3” apart on lightly greased baking sheet. Bake 10 to 12 minutes or just until set. Immediately remove from baking sheet.

Ingredients:
1 c plain fat-free yogurt
½ c brown sugar substitute, such as Splenda Brown Sugar Blend
2 Tbsp. butter spread, such as Smart Balance Original, melted
2 egg whites (she uses whole egg)
1 ½ c quick-cooking oats
½ c dried fruit, such as cranberries or blueberries
½ tsp. ground cinnamon (Eric would like more)
½ c chopped pecans, or almonds (she uses ½ c)
Instructions: Preheat oven to 350. Line a cookie sheet with parchment paper. Combine yogurt, brown sugar substitute and buttery spread in a bowl; mix until light and fluffy. Beat egg whites in a small bowl until soft peaks form. Fold into yogurt mixture. Stir in oats, dried fruit, cinnamon and pecans just until blended. Drop by heaping teaspoonful onto prepared cookie sheet. Press together and flatten with a fork for uniform baking. Bake 20 minutes. Cookies have a moist cake-like texture.

Ingredients:
2 1/4 c all-purpose flour
2 tsp baking soda
1 tsp ground cinnamon
3/4 tsp ground ginger
1/2 tsp ground allspice
1/2 tsp ground cloves
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 c vegetable shortening at room temperature
1/2 stick unsalted butter, softened
1 c packed brown sugar
1 large egg
1/2 c molasses (not robust or blackstrap)
about 1/3 c sanding or granulated sugar for tops of cookies
Instructions:
Whisk together flour, baking soda, cinnamon, ginger, allspice, cloves, and salt in a bowl until combined.
Beat together shortening, butter, and brown sugar in a large bowl with an electric mixer at medium-high speed until pale and fluffy, about 3 minutes in a stand mixer (preferably fitted with paddle attachment) or 6 minutes with a handheld. Add egg and molasses, beating until combined. Reduce speed to low, then mix in flour mixture until combined.
Put oven racks in upper and lower third of oven and preheat to 375 degrees.
Roll 1 heaping teaspoon of dough into a 1-inch ball with wet hands, then dip 1 end of ball in sanding sugar. Make more cookies in same manner, arranging them, sugared side up, 2 inches apart on 2 ungreased baking sheets.
Bake cookies, switching position of sheets halfway through baking, until undersides are golden brown, 10-12 minutes total, then cool on sheets 1 minute. Transfer to racks to cool completely. Make more cookies with remaining dough on cooled baking sheets.

Ingredients:
2 c white sugar
½ c cocoa
1 stick butter
½ c milk
1 tsp vanilla
3 c quick-cooking oatmeal
½ c peanut butter
Instructions:
Combine sugar, cocoa, butter and milk in a saucepan. On medium heat, bring to a boil for one full minute.
Remove from heat. Stir in peanut butter (optional), vanilla and oatmeal.
Drop by spoonful onto wax paper. Let cool for at least 30 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 c shortening
1 c brown sugar
1 c sugar
2 eggs
1 tsp. vanilla
1 ½ c flour
1 tsp. salt
1 tsp. baking soda
3 c Old Fashioned Quaker Oats
½ c chopped pecans
Instructions:
Combine shortening and sugars; mix to combine. In a separate bowl beat eggs and stir in vanilla; add to shortening/sugar mixture and mix well. In a separate bowl combine flour, salt and baking soda. Add dry mixture to wet mixture and blend. Add oats and chopped pecans and mix well.
Using a sheet of wax paper, roll ½ of the dough into a log; continue with other ½ of dough. Chill or freeze until ready to use. When chilled, remove wax paper and slice about ½ inch thick. Place on ungreased cookie sheet and bake in 350 oven for 10 minutes.

Ingredients:
2 c all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp baking powder
1 tsp kosher salt
1 c unsalted butter, softened
1 c sugar
1 c brown sugar, firmly packed
2 large eggs
2 tsp vanilla
Then stir in
3 c old-fashioned oats
1 1/2 c raisins
Instructions: Preheat oven to 350 degrees. Whisk dry ingredients; set aside. Combine wet ingredients with a hand mixer on low. To cream, increase speed to high and beat until fluffy and the color lightens. Stir the flour mixture into the creamed mixture until no flour is visible. (Over mixing develops the gluten, making a tough cookie.) Now add the oats and raisins; stir to incorporate. Fill a cookie scoop and press against side of bowl, pulling up to level dough (to measure 2 tbsp of dough). Drop 2 inches apart onto baking sheet lined with parchment or sprayed with cooking spray. Bake 11-13 minutes (on center rack), until golden, but still moist beneath cracks on top. Remove from oven; let cookies sit on baking for 2 minutes before transferring to a wire rack to cool.

Recipe Name: Original Toll House Chocolate Chip Cookies
Ingredients:
2 ¼ c all-purpose flour
1 tsp. baking soda
1 tsp. salt
1 c (2 sticks) butter, softened
¾ c granulated sugar
¾ c packed brown sugar
1 tsp. vanilla extract
2 large eggs
2 c (12 ounces) Nestle Toll House Semi-Sweet Chocolate Morsels
1 c chopped nuts
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 375. Combine flour, baking soda and salt in small bowl. Beat butter, granulated sugar, brown sugar and vanilla extract in large mixer bowl until creamy. Add eggs, one at a time, beating well after each addition. Gradually beat in flour mixture. Stir in morsels and nuts. Drop by rounded Tbsp. onto ungreased baking sheets. Bake for 9 to 11 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on baking sheets for 2 minutes; remove to wire racks to cool completely.
Pan Cookie Variation: Preheat oven to 350. Grease 15 x 10-inch jelly roll pan. Prepare dough as above. Spread into prepared pan. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes or until golden brown. Cool in pan on wire rack. Makes 4 dozen bars.
Slice and Bake Cookie Variation: Prepare dough as above. Divide in half; wrap in waxed paper. Refrigerate for 1 hour or until firm. Shape each half into 15-inch log; wrap in wax paper. Refrigerate for 30 minutes.* Preheat oven to 375. Cut into 1/2-inch thick-slices; place on ungreased baking sheets. Bake for 8 to 10 minutes or until golden brown. Cool on baking sheets for 2 minutes, remove to wire racks to cool completely. 
*May be stored in refrigerator for up to 1 week or in freezer for up to 8 weeks.

Ingredients:
1/4 c sugar
1 tbsp ground cinnamon
1 1/2 c sugar
1/2 c shortening
1/2 c butter, softened
2 eggs
2 3/4 c all-purpose flour
2 tsp cream of tartar
1 tsp baking soda
1/4 tsp salt
Instructions:
Heat oven to 400 degrees. Mix 1/4 c sugar and the cinnamon; set aside. Beat 1 1/2 c sugar, the shortening, butter and eggs in large bowl with electric mixer on medium speed, or mix with spoon. Stir in flour, cream of tartar, baking soda and salt.
Shape dough into 1 1/4-inch balls. Roll in sugar-cinnamon mixture. Place about 2 inches apart on ungreased cookie sheet. Bake about 10 minutes or until centers are almost set. Remove from cookie sheet to wire rack. Cool completely.

Ingredients:
1/2 c shortening or butter
1 c sugar
1 egg
1 tsp. vanilla
3 1/4 c sifted flour
1 tsp. baking soda
1/2 c sour cream
Instructions: Cream together the first four ingredients. Add sour cream. Add flour mixture. Blend. Chill (1 hour or so) Roll out to 1/4 inch thickness. Cut, sprinkle with sugar. Put on greased cookie sheets. Bake at 325 for about 12 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 c all-purpose flour
1/2 tsp baking soda
1/2 tsp cinnamon
1/4 tsp salt
10 tbsp unsalted butter, room temperature
1/2 c sugar
1/2 packed brown sugar
1 large egg
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 c old-fashioned oats
1/2 c semisweet chocolate chips
1/2 c milk chocolate chips
1/2 c white chocolate chips
1/2 c coarsely chopped fresh or frozen cranberries
2 oz milk or white chocolate, chopped (for drizzling)
Instructions:
Position rack in center of oven and preheat to 350 degrees. Line 2 large rimmed baking sheets with parchment paper. Whisk flour, baking soda, cinnamon, and salt in medium bowl to blend. Using electric mixer, beat butter and both sugars in bowl until smooth. Beat in egg and vanilla. Add flour mixture and oats and stir until blended. Stir in all chocolate chips and cranberries.
Drop batter by rounded tablespoonfuls onto prepared sheets, 2 inches apart. Bake cookies, 1 sheet at a time, until edges are light brown, about 16 minutes. Cool on sheets 5 minutes. Transfer to rack; cool completely.
Stir chopped milk or white chocolate in top of double boiler until melted and smooth. Using small spoon, drizzle melted chocolate over cookies in zigzag pattern. Let stand until chocolate sets, about 1 hour. (Can be made 2 days ahead. Store in airtight container at room temperature.)

Ingredients:
1 bag (11.5-ounce) Ghirardelli 60% Cacao Bittersweet Chocolate chips
6 Tbsp. (3/4 stick) unsalted butter
3 eggs
1 c sugar
1/3 c all-purpose flour
½ tsp. baking powder
1 bag (12-ounce) Ghirardelli Semi-Sweet Chocolate chips
1 c (4 ounces) chopped walnuts
Instructions:
In double boiler over hot water, melt the bittersweet chocolate chips and butter. In large bowl with electric mixer, beat eggs and sugar until thick; stir in chocolate mixture. In small bowl, stir together flour and baking powder; stir into chocolate mixture. Gently mix in semi-sweet chocolate chips and walnuts.
Using a sheet of plastic wrap, form dough into two logs, each 2 inches in diameter and about 8 inches long. As dough will be very soft, use plastic wrap to hold dough in log shape. Wrap tightly. Refrigerate at least 1 hour or until firm.
Preheat oven to 375. Unwrap dough; with sharp knife, cut into ¾-inch slices. Place slices 1 ½ inches apart on greased or parchment-lined cookie sheet. Bake 12 to 14 minutes or until shiny crust forms on top but interior is still soft.
Cool on baking sheet. Enjoy the moment of timeless pleasure.

Ingredients:
1/2 c + 6 Tbsp. butter
3/4 c firmly packed brown sugar
1/2 c granulated sugar
2 eggs
1 tsp. vanilla
1 1/2 c all-purpose flour
1 tsp baking soda
1 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp salt
3 c Quaker Oats (quick or old fashioned, uncooked)
1 c raisins
Instructions:
Heat oven to 350°F. In large bowl, beat butter and sugars on medium speed of electric mixer until creamy. Add eggs and vanilla; beat well. Add combined flour, baking soda, cinnamon and salt; mix well. Add oats and raisins; mix well. Drop dough by rounded tablespoonfuls onto ungreased cookie sheets. Bake 8 to 10 minutes or until light golden brown. Cool 1 minute on cookie sheets; remove to wire rack. Cool completely. Store tightly covered.
Serving Tips: Bar Cookies: Press dough onto bottom of ungreased 13 x 9-inch baking pan. Bake 30 to 35 minutes or until light golden brown. Cool completely in pan on wire rack. Cut into bars. Store tightly covered. 24 BARS. VARIATIONS: Stir in 1 cup chopped nuts. Substitute 1 cup semisweet chocolate chips or candy-coated chocolate pieces for raisins; omit cinnamon. Substitute 1 cup diced dried mixed fruit.

Ingredients:
¼ c butter
2/3 c dark brown sugar
3 ½ Tbsp. rum
1 ½ tsp. vanilla
½ tsp. ground cinnamon
3 bananas, peeled and sliced lengthwise and crosswise
¼ c coarsely chopped walnuts
1 pint vanilla ice cream
Instructions: In a large, deep skillet over medium heat, melt butter. Stir in sugar, rum, vanilla and cinnamon. When mixture begins to bubble, place bananas and walnuts in pan. Cook until bananas are hot, 1 to 2 minutes. Serve at once over vanilla ice cream.

Ingredients:
¼ c orange juice
1 Tbsp. lemon juice
One-half stick butter
One-half tsp. nutmeg
1 tsp. cinnamon
4 Tbsp. brown sugar
1/3 c light rum
4 bananas-quarter
4 scoops ice cream
Instructions:
In a medium size sauté pan combine orange juice, lemon juice, butter, nutmeg, cinnamon and brown sugar.
Stir over medium heat until butter is melted and sugar is dissolved.
Add rum, being careful as it may flame up.
Simmer for 1 minute. Add bananas and simmer for additional 2 minutes.
Serve over vanilla ice cream.

Ingredients:
8 sheets phyllo dough
1/2 c prepared lemon curd
1 package (8-ounce) nondairy whipped topping
Heat oven to 350.
Instructions:
Lightly coat eight 3” muffin tins with butter flavored cooking spray.
Cut each phyllo sheet into 4” squares to fit muffin tins. Layer phyllo dough squares into tins, spraying each layer with butter-flavored cooking spray. Coat top layer with butter-flavored cooking spray.
Bake 5 min or until golden.
Meanwhile, in bowl, combine prepared lemon curd and nondairy whipped topping.
Spoon 1/4 c of lemon curd mixture into each phyllo cup. Garnish, if desired, with fresh blueberries, raspberries or assorted fresh fruit.

Recipe Name: Keto Lemon Curd Tart in Low Carb Shortbread Crust
Ingredients:
Shortbread Crust
3/4 c coconut flour
1/4 c sweetener
1/8 tsp salt
1/2 c butter (1 stick, cold, cubed)
1 large egg white
Lemon Curd
1 c sweetener
lemon zest (4 lemons)
3/4 c lemon juice
4 large eggs
2 large egg yolks
1/2 c butter
1/8 tsp salt
1 tsp vanilla
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 325. In the bowl of a food processor, combine the coconut flour, sweetener and salt. Add the butter to the food processor and pulse to combine, until it reaches a sandy consistency. Add the egg white and pulse to combine until the dough is fully hydrated and forms pea-sized pieces. Press the shortbread dough firmly into a 9-inch tart pan with a removable bottom, using the bottom of a glass or your fingers. Use a piece of plastic wrap to prevent sticking while pressing the dough if necessary. Bake at 325 for 10-12 minutes, until the outer edges of the shortbread have just begun to brown (you want it to be underdone at this stage).
Prepare a mesh strainer by setting it over a medium heat proof bowl. Set the strainer and bowl aside near your stove. Fill a medium saucepan with 3 - 4 inches of water and bring it to a simmer over medium heat. While the water is heating, combine the sweetener, lemon juice, and lemon zest in a large, heat proof bowl and whisk to combine. Add the eggs and egg yolks and immediately whisk to combine. Place the heat proof bowl over the simmering water to form a double boiler. Continue whisking the egg mixture constantly. The sweetener will gradually dissolve and then the mixture will begin to thicken. The curd is done cooking when it has reached 160 degrees and is the consistency of sour cream or full fat yogurt.
Turn off the burner and remove the bowl from the double boiler, being careful not to burn yourself on the escaping steam. Immediately strain the hot lemon curd through the prepared mesh strainer and into the bowl. Tap the strainer firmly against your hand, and use a rubber spatula to help the curd through without damaging your strainer. Add the butter, vanilla and salt to the hot curd. Whisk the curd occasionally until the butter has melted completely and is fully incorporated.
Pour the curd into the parbaked low carb shortbread crust. Gently shake the pan to even out the curd or spread it with an offset spatula. Bake the tart at 325 for 15 minutes to set the curd filling. For best results, allow the tart to cool and then chill for 1-2 hours, or overnight. Store in the refrigerator.

Ingredients:
4 servings
3/4 c oats
3/4 c flour
2/3 c light brown sugar, packed
1 tsp ground cinnamon
1/2 tsp ground ginger
pinch kosher salt
1 stick cold unsalted butter, diced
4 Braeburn apples (Fuji will substitute)
4 tsp honey
Instructions:
In a bowl combine all the dry ingredients and diced butter. Rub mixture briskly between finger tips until it forms small moist clumps in a loose sandy mixture. Refrigerate while preparing the apples.
Cut a small layer off the bottom of each apple to create a flat, stable bottom surface. With a small paring knife, cut a cylindrical cone out of the top of the apple, moving about 1-inch outside of the core, similar to removing the top of a pumpkin when carving a Jack O'Lantern. Remove the top and discard. With a melon baller or a teaspoon, remove the remaining core and seeds taking care not to puncture the base of the apple.
Place apples on a baking sheet or pie dish and fill each center with a teaspoon of honey. Spoon in mixture, packing lightly until heaped and overflowing over sides of the apples.
Bake in oven at 350 degrees on the top or middle rack for 40 minutes or until filling is golden brown and the tip of a paring knife can be inserted into the side of the apple with little or no resistance. Let apples stand for 10 minutes before serving.

Ingredients:
3 cups sugar
¾ c butter
2/3 cup (5 1/ oz. can) evaporated milk
1 12 oz. pkg semi-sweet chocolate chips
1 7 oz. jar marshmallow crème
1 cup chopped pecans
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions:
Combine sugar, butter and milk in heavy saucepan; bring to a full rolling boil; stirring constantly. Continue boiling for 5 minutes over medium heat; stirring constantly. Remove from heat; stir in chocolate chips until melted. Add marshmallow crème, nuts and vanilla; beat until well blended. Pour into greased 13x9 inch pan. Cool at room temperature. Cut into squares. Makes 3 lbs.
Jello Flesh Toned Qwiggle Brain Mold
RB made Halloween 2009 and it made about 1 ½ c too much.
Cleaning the Mold:
Wash the entire inside cavity of the plastic brain mold using a soft sponge, dish soap, and warm water before each use. Use care to avoid puncturing a hole in the thin areas of the plastic mold while washing and drying it. Dry thoroughly before use.
3 large, 6-ounce boxes of peach or watermelon flavored gelatin. (Using any other flavored gelatin will not result in a flesh toned appearance.
1 12-ounce can of lite evaporated skimmed milk (99.5% fat free). No other milk will work!
Cooking spray (to lubricate the plastic mold)
2 tsp. of vegetable oil (to lubricate the plastic mold)
Green food coloring
3 ½ c of water (2 ½ c boiled, 1 c cold)
Spray a small amount of vegetable oil inside the cavity of the plastic mold. Wipe off any excess oil.
Place gelatin from the boxes into a large bowl. Add 2 ½ c boiling water. Stir gelatin with a whisk until it is completely dissolved, about 3 minutes. Stir in one c of cold water. Add skimmed milk and stir for two minutes. (If a clear brain is desired, leave out the skimmed milk and add an additional 1 ½ c of cold water) Add a few drops of green food coloring to produce the flesh tone color, re-stir. Pour gelatin mixture into the plastic brain mold. Leave approximately one inch of space at top of mold.
Wrap a towel around the bottom of the mold. The towel will help to keep the mold upright. Set the towel and mold in the refrigerator overnight.
Extracting the hardened gelatin from the mold:
Carefully shake the mold until the gelatin is loosened from the plastic mold. Put on a cooking glove, and place the open palm of your hand on the top of the gelatin, and turn the mold over. Shake the mold again. Continue to carefully shake the mold until the brain plops out onto your gloved hand. Place mold onto a plate or a platter. Cover the mold with plastic food wrap and return the mold to the refrigerator until you are ready to put it on display and serve it.

Ingredients:
Makes 2 to 3 dozen, depending on size
Butter for greasing pans
1 pound light brown sugar
1 cup heavy (whipping) cream, plus 1-3 teaspoons for thinning batter if needed
2 tablespoons light corn syrup
4 tablespoons unsalted butter, cut in pieces
1-1/2 cups toasted chopped pecans
1 tablespoon vanilla extract
Instructions:
Line two baking sheets with aluminum foil. Butter the foil.
Stir together the sugar, cream and corn syrup in an eight-cup glass measure or deep microwaveable bowl, stirring until the sugar lumps are dissolved. Microwave on high for 13 minutes. Carefully remove from the oven. The bowl will be extremely hot.
Stir in the 4 tablespoons butter, pecans and vanilla. Beat by hand for 1 minute, until the mixture is noticeably less glossy.
Working quickly and using two tablespoons, drop the mixture by tablespoonfuls onto the prepared pans.
If the mixture thickens as it cools, add 1 to 3 additional teaspoons of cream and stir.
Let pralines cool to room temperature, about 20 minutes. Serve as soon as possible. Pralines are best the day they are made, but they can be stored in airtight containers up to four days.

Ingredients:
1/2 c (1 stick) butter
2/3 c HERSHEY'S Cocoa
3 c powdered sugar
1/3 c milk
1 tsp vanilla
Instructions:
Melt butter. Stir in cocoa. Alternately add powdered sugar and milk, beating to spreading consistency.
Add small amount additional milk, if needed. Stir in vanilla. About 2 cups frosting.

Ingredients:
6 ounces (1 ½ sticks) unsalted butter
2 ounces (4 Tbsp.) margarine
2 ounces (4 Tbsp.) solid vegetable shortening
1 pound confectioners’ sugar (6x or 10x)
1 Tbsp. heavy cream
Pinch salt
1 tsp. vanilla
½ tsp. lemon juice, optional
Instructions: Using a mixer with the paddle attachment, cream the butter and margarine until smooth. Add vegetable shortening and beat until white and fluffy. Sift confectioners’ sugar if lumpy. Add half of the confectioners’ sugar and beat until thoroughly incorporated into the butter mixture. Repeat with the remaining sugar. Beat in cream, salt, vanilla and optional lemon juice. Mix on medium speed for 7-10 minutes, until perfectly smooth.

Ingredients:
½ cup Crisco
½ cup butter
1 tsp vanilla
4 cups powdered sugar
1-2 tbsp milk
Instructions: Cream butter and Crisco together. Gradually add sugar one cup at a time beating well on medium. When all sugar has been mixed, add 2 tbsp of milk (one at a time) until smooth and creamy. You can add more milk to make it thinner, or less milk to make it thicker.

Ingredients:
1 cup sugar
1 1/2 teaspoons baking soda
1/2 cup buttermilk
1/2 cup butter or margarine
1 tablespoon light corn syrup
1 teaspoon vanilla extract
Instructions: Bring first 5 ingredients to a boil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Boil, stirring often, 4 minutes. Remove from heat, and stir in vanilla.

Ingredients:
½ c solid vegetable shortening
½ (1 stick) butter, softened
¾ c cocoa or three 1 oz. unsweetened chocolate squares, melted
1 tsp. vanilla
4 c sifted confectioners’ sugar (approximately 1 lb.)
3-4 tbsp. milk
Instructions:
Step 1
Instructions:
Stiff consistency: In large bowl, cream shortening and butter with electric mixer. Add cocoa and vanilla. Gradually add sugar, one cup at a time, beating well on medium speed. Scrape sides and bottom of bowl often. When all sugar has been mixed in, icing will appear dry. Add milk and beat at medium speed until light and fluffy. Keep bowl covered with a damp cloth until ready to use. For best results, keep icing bowl in refrigerator when not in use. Refrigerated in an airtight container, this icing can be stored 2 weeks. Rewhip before using.
Step 2
Chocolate Mocha Icing: Substitute cold brewed strong coffee for milk in Chocolate Buttercream recipe.
Darker Chocolate Icing: Add an additional ¼ cup cocoa (or one additional 1 oz. square unsweetened chocolate, melted) and 1 additional tablespoon milk to Chocolate Buttercream Icing.
For Thin (Spreading) Consistency Icing: Add 3-4 tablespoons light corn syrup, water or milk.

Recipe Name: Chocolate Frosting for Beatty’s Chocolate Cake
Ingredients:
6 ounces good semisweet chocolate
½ pound (2 sticks) unsalted butter, at room temperature
1 extra-large egg yolk, at room temperature
1 tsp. pure vanilla extract
1 ½ c sifted confectioners’ sugar
1 Tbsp. instant coffee powder
Instructions:
Chop the chocolate and place it in a heat-proof bowl set over a pan of simmering water. Stir until just melted and set aside until cooled to room temperature.
In the bowl of an electric mixer fitted with a paddle attachment, beat the butter on medium-high speed until light yellow and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add the egg yolk and vanilla and continue beating for 3 minutes. Turn the mixer to low, gradually add the confectioners’ sugar, then beat at medium speed, scraping down the bowl as necessary, until smooth and creamy. Dissolve the coffee powder in 2 tsp. of the hottest tap water. On low speed, add the chocolate and coffee to the butter mixture and mix until blended. Don’t whip! Spread immediately on the cooled cake.

Ingredients:
1/2 c butter
6 Tbsp. Coca Cola
3 Tbsp. cocoa
1 box confectioner’s sugar
1 c chopped pecans
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions: Heat to boiling point, 1/2 c butter, 6 Tbsp. coke and 3 Tbsp. cocoa. Pour over 1 box powdered sugar. Beat until smooth and creamy. Add 1 c finely chopped nuts and 1 tsp. vanilla. Spread on hot cake. If icing is too thick add a few drops of hot water.

Recipe Name: Coconut Pecan Frosting for German Chocolate Cake
Ingredients:
1 c evaporated milk
1 c sugar
3 egg yolks
1 stick butter
1 t vanilla
1 1/3 c coconut
Instructions: Combine first five ingredients. Cook and stir over medium heat until thickened, about 12 minutes. Add coconut and pecans. Stir until smooth.

Recipe Name: Cream Cheese Frosting for Italian Cream Cake
Ingredients:
2 packages cream cheese
1 stick butter
2 tsp. vanilla
1 package 2pound powder sugar
1 c chopped walnuts
1 c sweetened, flaked coconut
Instructions: In a medium bowl, combine cream cheese, butter, vanilla, and powdered sugar. Beat until light and fluffy. Stir in chopped walnuts and sugared coconut. Spread between layers and serve. Cream cheese frosting will soften at room temperature, so refrigerate if you won’t use it right away.

Recipe Name: Cream Cheese Frosting for Carrot Cake
Ingredients:
3/4 cup butter or margarine, softened
1 (8-ounce) package cream cheese, softened
1 (3-ounce) package cream cheese, softened
3 cups sifted powdered sugar
1 1/2 teaspoons vanilla extract
Instructions: Beat butter and cream cheese at medium speed with an electric mixer until creamy. Add powdered sugar and vanilla; beat until smooth.

Recipe Name: Cream Cheese Frosting for Red Velvet Cake
Source: Rose Marie Luke from Sue Luke Lewis
Ingredients:
1 8-ounce package cream cheese
1 stick butter
1 box confectioner’s sugar
1 tsp. vanilla
1 c chopped pecans
Instructions: Cream butter and cream cheese. Gradually add sifted sugar. Stir in vanilla and pecans. Frost Red Velvet Cake.

Ingredients:
2 boxes powdered sugar
1/3 c Crisco
1 tsp. cream of tarter
1/2 tsp. salt
2 c ice water
Instructions: Box and a half sugar, Crisco, cream of tartar, salt, 1/2 c ice water. Add more sugar and ice water as needed for consistency.

Recipe Name: Frosting and Filling for Italian Cream Cake
Ingredients:
2 8-ounce packages of cream cheese, at room temperature
½ c unsalted butter, at room temperature
2 tsp. vanilla extract
2 pounds powdered sugar
¾ c sweetened, flaked coconut
Approximately 2 c chopped pecans
Instructions: Combine cream cheese and butter in mixing bowl and beat until light and fluffy, about 3 minutes. Add vanilla extract and beat until well incorporated. Gradually add in powdered sugar, mix on low speed until the frosting reaches the desired consistency. Remove approximately 1/3 of the frosting to a separate bowl to make filling. Add coconut and ¾ c chopped pecans to the filling and mix well. Set aside remaining frosting to ice the cake.

Ingredients:
½ c butter, cubed
1/3 c heavy whipping cream
¼ c baking cocoa
1 ½ tsp ground cinnamon
3 c confectioners’ sugar
1 tsp vanilla extract
1 c finely chopped walnuts (optional)
Instructions: Combine the butter, cream, cocoa and cinnamon in a saucepan. Cook and stir over medium heat until butter is melted and mixture is heated through. Remove from the heat; beat in sugar and vanilla until smooth. Stir in walnuts, if using. Carefully spread over warm cake. Cool completely. Yield: 24-30 servings.

Ingredients:
3 to 4 cups confectioners’ sugar
1 stick unsalted butter, at room temperature
3 to 4 tbsp. Baileys
Instructions:
Whip the butter in the bowl of an electric mixer, or with a hand mixer, for several minutes. You want to get it very light and fluffy. Slowly add the powdered sugar, a few tablespoons at a time.
When the frosting looks thick enough to spread, drizzle in the Bailey’s and whip it until combined. If this has made the frosting too thin beat in another spoonful or two of powdered sugar.

Ingredients:
8 oz. bittersweet chocolate
2/3 c heavy cream
2 tbsp. butter, room temperature
1 to 2 tsp. Irish whiskey
Instructions: Chop the chocolate and transfer it to a heatproof bowl. Heat the cream until simmering and pour it over the chocolate. Let it sit for one minute and then stir until smooth. (If this has not sufficiently melted the chocolate, you can return it to a double-boiler to gently melt what remains. 20 seconds in the microwave, watching carefully, will also work.) Add the butter and whiskey and stir until combined.

Ingredients:
Juice of 1 lemon
½ c sugar
2 tbsp. water
Instructions: Mix and let come to a boil. Punch holes in cake and pour glaze over it.

Ingredients:
1 c sugar
2 1/2 tsp. flour
1 egg
2 lemons, juiced and zested
1 Tbsp. butter
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions: Mix sugar and flour together. Beat in a beaten egg. Add the lemon zest and the juice. Add butter and vanilla. Cook over low heat until boiling and it thickens.

Ingredients:
1 c sugar
1 c boiling water
2 2/3 t cornstarch
1 egg yolk, well-beaten
1 T butter
1/4 c lemon juice
Instructions: Cook your cake of choice and split into six layers or cook in six pans. Combine all ingredients except lemon juice. Cook until thick, smooth and transparent. Add lemon juice. Cook for 5 minutes.

Ingredients:
7 ounces semisweet chocolate, chopped (see note)
2 Tbsp. unsalted butter
2 Tbsp. white rum
1 c heavy cream
4 Tbsp. confectioners’ sugar
Instructions:
In a double boiler, melt the chocolate with the butter and rum. Stir and remove from heat.
Whisk in the cream, then the sugar. Cool mixture in the refrigerator or freezer, stirring occasionally, until the chocolate mixture thickens to a dense frosting consistency.
Note: The better the quality of the chocolate, the better the final result. While it is preferable to measure the chocolate by weight and chop it into chip-sized pieces, 1 c of semisweet chocolate chips may be substituted.

Ingredients:
For the crust:
1 ¼ c all-purpose flour, plus more for dusting
6 tbsp cold unsalted butter, cut into small pieces
2 tbsp cold vegetable shortening
1 tbsp granulated sugar
½ tsp salt
1 tbsp apple cider vinegar
For the filling:
2 ¼ c pecans
6 tbsp unsalted butter
2 large eggs
2/3 c packed light brown sugar
½ c molasses or sorghum
2 tbsp bourbon
½ tsp salt
Instructions:
Make the crust: pulse the flour, 2 tbsp butter, the shortening, granulated sugar and salt in a food processor until it looks like fine meal. Add the remaining 4 tbsp butter and pulse until it is in pea-size pieces. Drizzle in the vinegar and 2 tbsp cold water and pulse until the dough just comes together. Turn out onto a piece of plastic wrap; use the plastic to help form the dough into a disk. Wrap tightly and refrigerate until firm, at least 1 hour or overnight. (The dough can be frozen for up to 2 month; thaw at room temperature.)
Roll out the dough into a 12-inch round on a lightly floured surface. Ease into a 9-inch pie place, fold the overhanging dough under itself and crimp the edge with your fingers. Chill at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, make the filling: Position a rack in the lower third of the oven; preheat to 425 degrees. Spread the pecans on a baking sheet and toast until darkened, 10 to 12 minutes. Transfer to pa plate and return the empty baking sheet to the lower oven rack. Melt the butter in a small skillet over medium heat and cook, stirring with a rubber spatula, until brown flecks appear, about 8 minutes; let cool slightly. Whisk the browned butter, eggs, brown sugar, molasses, bourbon and salt in a bowl. Scatter the toasted pecans in the prepared crust; pour in the brown sugar mixture.
Put the pie directly on the hot baking sheet in the oven and reduce the temperature to 325 degrees. Bake until the crust is golden brown and the filling is set, 45 to 55 minutes. (Cover the edge with foil if it is browning too quickly.) Transfer to a rack and let cool at least 1 hour before serving.

Ingredients:
1½ cup sugar
4 egg yolks
3 tbsp. flour
½ stick butter
1 ½ cups milk
1 ½ tsp. vanilla
4 egg whites
8 tbsp. sugar
Instructions:
Brown ½ cup sugar in an iron skillet until it is a medium golden brown.
Mix one cup sugar and flour. Add beaten egg yolks, butter and milk. Cook until almost thick and add quickly to the caramelized sugar and stir until very smooth. Cook more I not thick enough. Add vanilla and mix well. Pour into baked pie shell.
Beat egg whites and add 8 tbsp. of sugar. Place on pie and brown.

Ingredients:
1 stick butter, melted
1 ½ cup sugar
3 eggs
1 tsp. vanilla
Unbaked pie shell
Instructions: Beat eggs slightly. Add other ingredients. Pour into pie shell. Bake at 425 degrees for 10 minutes. Reduce to 350 and bake for about 30 minutes longer.

Ingredients:
1 (9 inch) pie shell, unbaked
1 ½ cups sugar
3 tbsp. cocoa
¼ cup butter, melted
2 eggs, slightly beaten
1 (5 oz.) can evaporated milk
1 tsp. vanilla
½ to 3/34 cup chopped pecans (optional)
Instructions: Mix sugar, cocoa and butter. Stir well. Add eggs and beat with electric mixer for 2 ½ minutes. Add milk and vanilla. Stir in pecans, if desired. Pour into pie shell. Bake at 325 degrees to 35-45 minutes.
Related Recipes: Chocolate Chess Pie — Angus Barn, Raleigh

Ingredients:
3 eggs, slightly beaten
1 c Karo, light or dark corn syrup
1/3 c sugar
1 tsp. vanilla
1 1/2 c pecan halves
2 Tbsp. butter
4 ounces semisweet chocolate chips
1 unbaked 9” pie shell
Instructions: Stir together first 4 ingredients until blended. Melt chocolate chips and butter in microwave in 30 second intervals until melted. Combine butter chocolate mixture and nuts with wet ingredients. Pour into pastry shell. Bake in 350 oven 50 to 60 minutes or until knife inserted halfway between center and edge comes out clean. Cool.

Ingredients:
3 tbsp. cocoa
1 ½ cups sugar
4 heaping tbsp. all-purpose flour
2 cups milk
3 egg yolks
1 tsp. vanilla
3 tbsp. butter
Instructions:
Beat egg yolks and add milk. Sift dry ingredients. Gradually add milk mixture. Cook over low heat until thickened. Take off heat and add butter and vanilla.
Beat eggs whites. Beat in 6 tbsp. sugar
Pour chocolate mixture into baked pie crust. Cover with egg whites and brown.

Ingredients:
8 servings
1 c Karo Light or Dark Corn Syrup
3 eggs
1 c sugar
2 tbsp butter, melted
1 tsp vanilla
1 1/2 c pecans (6 oz)
1 9-inch frozen* or unbaked deep-dish pie crust
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350. Mix syrup, eggs, sugar, butter, and vanilla using a spoon. Stir in pecans. Pour filling into the pie crust. Bake on center rack of oven for 60 to 70 minutes. (see tips for doneness, below) Cool on wire rack for 2 hours before serving. *To use prepared frozen pie crust: Place cookie sheet in oven and preheat oven as directed. Pour filling into frozen crust and bake on preheated cookie sheet.
RECIPE TIPS: Pie is done when center reaches 200°F. Tap center surface of pie lightly - it should spring back when done. For easy clean up, spray pie pan with cooking spray before placing pie crust in pan. If pie crust is overbrowning, cover edges with foil.

Ingredients:
2 cups sugar
2 tsp flour
6 eggs
1 stick melted oleo (butter)
1 can coconut
1/3 cup cream
Raw Pie Crust
Instructions: Mix sugar and flour together, beat in eggs, oleo until blended, add coconut and cream. Pour into crust and bake at 350 degrees until done.

Ingredients:
1 small package sugar free lime Jell-O
1/2 c boiling water
8 ounces cream cheese, softened
1 Tbsp. lime juice
Lime peel, finely grated
1/2 c heavy cream
Instructions: Dissolve the Jell-O in the boiling water. Beat cream cheese in a medium bowl with an electric mixer until smooth. Gradually beat the Jell-O into the cream cheese. Add the lime juice and rind. Add cream and whip until fluffy. Pour into a pie plate that has been sprayed with nonstick spray. Chill until set.

Ingredients:
1 4 oz. pkg Jell-O lemon pudding (not instant)
1 3 oz. pkg Jell-O lime gelatin
1/3 cup sugar
2 ½ cups water
2 eggs, slightly beaten
½ cup Bacardi light rum
2 cups Cool Whip
1 9 inch pie crust - crumb
Instructions: Mix pudding, gelatin and sugar in a saucepan. Stir in ½ cup water and eggs, blend well. Add remaining water. Stir over medium heat until full boil. Remove from heat. Stir in rum, chill. Blend topping into chilled mixture, spoon into crust. Chill until firm, about 2 hours.

Ingredients:
3/4 c granulated sugar
1 tsp cinnamon
1/2 tsp salt
1/2 tsp ground ginger
1/4 tsp ground cloves
2 large eggs
1 can (15 oz) 100% Pure Pumpkin
1 can (12 oz) evaporated milk
1 unbaked 9-inch (4 cup volume) deep-dish pie shell
Whipped Cream (optional)
Instructions: Mix sugar, cinnamon, salt, ginger and cloves in a small bowl. Beat eggs in a large bowl. Stir in pumpkin and sugar-spice mixture. Gradually stir in evaporated milk. Pour into pie shell. Bake in preheated 425 oven for 15 minutes. Reduce temperature to 350 and bake for 40 to 50 minutes or until knife inserted near center comes out clean. Cool on wire rack for 2 hours. Serve immediately or refrigerate. Top with whipped cream before serving.

Ingredients:
½ c peanut butter
8 oz. cream cheese, softened
½ c milk
1 c powdered sugar
9 oz. Cool Whip, thawed
9 inch graham cracker pie crust
Instructions: Whip cheese until soft and fluffy. Beat in peanut butter and sugar. Slowly add milk, blending thoroughly into mixture. Fold in Cool Whip. Pour into prepared crust. Freeze until firm. We have always used smooth peanut butter, but original recipe suggested using crunchy and garnishing with chopped peanuts.

Ingredients:
one:
1 cup flour
1 stick butter
1 cup chopped pecans
two:
1 8 oz. pkg cream cheese
1 cup confectioner’s sugar
1 cup Cool Whip from large container
three:
2 small pkgs instant lemon or chocolate pudding
3 cups milk
Instructions:
One - Melt butter, add flour, mix, and add pecans. Pat in 9x13 inch dish. Bake at 350 for 15 minutes or until light brown – cool.
Two - Mix. Spread on pastry.
Three - Mix. Spread on cheese layer.
Four - Spread remaining Cool Whip from large container over pudding layer.
Refrigerate. Cut into squares.

Ingredients:
1 cup sugar
4 tbsp. butter, melted
4 eggs
Juice of 1 lemon
8 inch pie shell
Instructions: Best eggs slightly, add remaining ingredients. Bake at 350 degrees until lightly golden brown, about 25-30 minutes.

Ingredients:
3 or 4 eggs
1 cup sugar
1 tbsp. flour
½ cup hot water
¼ cup butter
Juice of 1 lemon
Instructions: Mix well and pour into an unbaked crust and bake at 350 until set.

Recipe Name: Lemon or Lime Ice Box Pie
Ingredients:
1 Vanilla Wafer Crust
1/3 – ½ c Lemon or Lime Juice (tartness desire)
2 egg yolks
1 can condensed milk (if using deep crust, double milk)
2 egg whites
4 Tbsp. sugar
Instructions: Beat egg yolks; add lemon or lime juice; add condensed milk; pour into crust. Whip whites and add sugar; whip to peaks. Bake at 350 about 10 minutes until meringue is desired color.

Ingredients:
1 (15-ounce) can pumpkin
1 (14-ounce) can condensed milk
2 eggs
1 tsp. ground cinnamon
½ tsp. each: ground ginger, nutmeg and salt
1 (9-inch) unbaked pie crust
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 425 degrees. With mixer, beat pumpkin, condensed milk, eggs, spices and salt in large bowl. Pour into crust. Bake 15 minutes.
Reduce oven temperature to 350 degrees; bake 35 to 40 minutes longer or until set in center. Cool. Refrigerate within 2 hours. Store leftovers covered in refrigerator.

Ingredients:
3 eggs
2 c whole milk
¼ c sugar
1 tsp. vanilla
Instructions: Whisk eggs. Stir in remaining ingredients. Pour into glass dishes or Corning ware. Yitts always used small single-serving bowls. Place dishes in bain marie. Cook at 350 for about 30 minutes, until knife inserted into pudding comes out clean.

Ingredients:
3 eggs
2/3 c sugar
1 Tbsp. vanilla
Approximately 8 ounces white bread 1/4 inch cubes
1 12-ounce can evaporated milk, rinsed with half can of regular milk
1/2 stick butter
3/4 c raisins (cover with half hot water, half bourbon and microwave 90 seconds, drain)
1/4 tsp. salt
Instructions: Beat eggs, add the sugar, milk, vanilla and melted butter. Fold in bread cubes, let sit 30 minutes. Mixture should be fluid, but not so thin that raisins will sink to the bottom. Add more regular milk, if necessary. Fold in raisins. Pour into 9X9 sprayed pan. Bake in water bath at 350 for 50-60 minutes until set. Omit water bath if you like crunchy corner pieces.

Source: Café 14 via Noelle Carter, Los Angeles Times
Ingredients:
¼ pound (1 stick) unsalted butter
2 c lightly packed (13 ounces) brown sugar
2 c heavy cream
1 tsp. vanilla
Salt
3 bananas (overripe is preferred), peeled and coarsely mashed with a fork
6 c diced croissants, from about 3 croissants diced into ½-inch cubes
3 eggs
Instructions:
In a large, heavy-bottom saucepan, melt the butter over medium heat. Stir in the brown sugar and cook, stirring to dissolve. Stir in the cream, vanilla and a pinch of salt and gently bring the mixture to a boil, stirring frequently. When the mixture has come to a boil, remove from heat and set aside to cool.
While the caramel cream cools, toss the bananas and croissants in a large mixing bowl.
Whisk the eggs into the caramel cream to form a custard base, strain and then pour the custard base over the croissants and gently toss. Place the mixture in a greased 9-inch round cake pan or an 8-inch square baking dish. Set the mixture aside to soak for 1 hour. Meanwhile, heat the oven to 350 degrees.
Place the cake pan into a larger baking dish; fill the larger baking dish with enough boiling water to come up the side of the cake pan halfway to form a water bath.
Bake the bread pudding until puffed and set, about 45 minutes. Remove from the oven and water bath, and cool slightly before serving. For a crisp topping, place the cake pan under the broiler just until it begins to brown and crisp on top, about 1 to 3 minutes, depending on the broiler.

Recipe Name: Chunky Chocolate Walnut Bread Pudding with Kahlua Cream Sauce
Ingredients:
Non-stick pan spray
16 ounces cubed day old French bread (6 c)
1 c granulated sugar
4 large eggs
1 Tbsp. vanilla extract
3 c milk
1 ¼ c chocolate chips
1 c chopped walnuts
Kahlua Cream Sauce:
1 c heavy cream
¼ c sugar
½ c Kahlua
2 Tbsp. cornstarch
1 Tbsp. water
Instructions:
Spray medium-sized baking dish (8x10x2) with pan spray and line with cubed French bread.
Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
In medium mixing bowl, beat together sugar and eggs approximately 3-4 minutes, until light and fluffy.
Add milk and vanilla and beat until combined. Pour over cubed bread and soak for 30 minutes.
Stir in chocolate chips and walnut pieces until evenly dispensed throughout the bread pudding.
Bake for 1 hour. Bread pudding should be puffy and brown.
Sauce:
In a small saucepot, heat heavy cream, sugar and Kahlua over a medium heat.
Combine cornstarch and water and mix until cornstarch is dissolved. Whip into heated cream mixture and stir until thickened.
Top individual servings of bread pudding with sauce.

Ingredients:
3 lemons
1 ½ c sugar
¼ lb unsalted butter, room temperature
4 extra-large eggs
½ c lemon juice (3 to 4 lemons)
1/8 tsp kosher salt
Instructions:
Zest 3 lemons, being careful to avoid the white pith. Put the zest in a food processor fitted with a steel blade. Add the sugar and pulse until the zest is very finely minced into the sugar.
Cream the butter and beat in the sugar and lemon mixture. Add the eggs, 1 at a time, and then add the lemon juice and salt. Mix until combined.
Pour the mixture into a 2 quart saucepan and cook over low heat until thickened (about 10 minutes), stirring constantly. The lemon curd will thicken at about 170 degrees, or just below simmer. Remove from the heat and cool or refrigerate.

Ingredients:
makes about 5 ramekins
12 oz semi-sweet chocolate chips
4 eggs, room temperature
2 tsp vanilla or liqueur
pinch of salt
8 oz coffee
Whipped cream
Instructions: Put chocolate chips, eggs, vanilla and salt in blender. Pulse a few times to break up chips. Heat coffee to just under boiling. With blender running, slowly pour coffee into through hole at top. Pour into serving dishes, cover with plastic wrap and refrigerate until set. Top with whipped cream and serve. Note: do not double recipe, it will burn your blender up! Found out the hard way.

Ingredients:
1 package (8-ounce) cream cheese
1 can (14-ounce) sweetened condensed milk
2 c. milk
1 package (6-ounce) instant vanilla pudding mix
1 carton (8-ounce) nondairy whipping topping
4 bananas, sliced
1 box (12-ounce) vanilla wafers
Jigger each of rum and bourbon
Instructions:
Lay vanilla wafers out on a cookie sheet. I use the whole box. Mix the liquors together and sprinkle over the vanilla wafers – not enough to make them mushy, you have to be able to get them in the bowl.
Cream the cheese until smooth. Stir in sweetened condensed milk, milk and pudding mix. Fold in half of the whipped topping. Layer pudding, cookies and bananas in dish. Top with remaining topping. Chill.

Ingredients:
3 c pound cake cubes (1/2 inch)
2 envelopes Amaretto Cappuccino, divided
2 c cold milk, divided
1 package (8-ounce) cream cheese, softened
1 package (4 serving size) Vanilla Instant Pudding
2 c thawed Whipped Topping
Instructions: Cover bottom of 8-inch square dish with cake. Dissolve 1 envelope of the cappuccino in 1/2 c of the milk; sprinkle over cake. Beat Cream cheese in large bowl with electric mixer until smooth. Gradually beat in remaining 1 1/2 c milk until smooth. Add pudding mix and remaining envelope of cappuccino. Beat on low speed until blended. Stir in whipped topping. Spoon over cake. Refrigerate 3 hours.

Ingredients:
1/2 can evaporated milk
1 stick butter
1 c sugar
1 egg yolk, beaten
1/2 c bourbon
Instructions: Cook in double boiler for 10-13 minutes until thickened.

Ingredients:
2 cans condensed milk
1 large can crushed pineapple
5 12 oz. cans orange soda
1 12 oz. can Sprite
Instructions: Mix together all ingredients in freezer bucket. Freeze according to ice cream freezer directions.

Ingredients:
3 cups sugar
8 eggs
3 quarts milk
1 tbsp. flour
1 tbsp. vanilla
1 tbsp. lemon flavoring
Instructions:
Beat eggs well. Add flour and sugar. Add milk. Cook slowly, stirring constantly until mixture coast spoon. Remove from heat and add flavoring
Chill and freeze according to freezer directions.
Amount of milk used should be adjusted to size of freezer.

Ingredients:
3 1/2 c sifted flour
3 tsp. baking powder
1 tsp. baking soda
2 Tbsp. lemon juice
2 c mashed bananas (4 to 6)
3/4 c butter
1 1/2 c sugar
3 eggs
3/4 c milk
3/4 c chopped nuts
Instructions: Cream butter and sugar. Add eggs one at a time. Add sifted dry ingredients alternately with milk. Mash bananas and stir in lemon juice. Fold bananas and pecans into batter. Bake at 350 for 50 to 55 minutes.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Good Housekeeping July 1982
Ingredients:
1 c sugar
1 ¾ c flour
2 tsp. baking powder
1 tsp. vanilla flavoring
4 eggs
1 pint blueberries
Pecan Topping
½ c butter, melted
1 c flour
½ c chopped pecans
¼ c sugar
1 tsp. cinnamon
Instructions: Cream butter and sugar. Add flour, baking powder, vanilla and eggs, blend then beat until smooth. Spread batter in 13/9 baking dish; top with blueberries. Pinch pecan topping into small pieces; sprinkle on cake. Bake at 325 45 minutes – 1 hour.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Betty Crocker
Ingredients:
1 egg
½ c milk
¼ c cooking oil
1 ½ c flour
½ c sugar
2 tsp. baking powder
1 c blueberries
Instructions:
Beat egg; stir in milk and oil. Mix in remaining ingredients just until flour is moistened. Batter should be lumpy.
Fill greased muffin cups 2/3 full. Bake for 20 to 25 minutes at 400 degrees.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Seacliffe Subdivision
Ingredients:
1 box Duncan Hines Butter Recipe Golden
¾ c sugar
¾ c Crisco oil
4 eggs
1 tbsp. vanilla
8 oz. sour cream
Mix in separate bowl:
2 tbsp. sugar
2 tbsp. cinnamon
Instructions: Mix all ingredients except sugar and cinnamon. Beat until smooth and creamy. Grease and flour 2 loaf pans. Put a thin layer of batter in loaf pans; spread a thin layer of cinnamon and sugar mixture over batter. Repeat until both pans are half full. Bake at 325 degrees for about 45 minutes to an hour.

Ingredients:
3 c powdered sugar
3 Tbsp. butter, melted
2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
1/4 tsp. vanilla extract
2 to 4 Tbsp. milk
Instructions: Stir together first 4 ingredients. Stir in 2 Tbsp. milk, adding additional milk until spreading consistency.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Linda L. and Betsy
Ingredients:
Yellow cake mix
5 oz. vanilla instant pudding
5 eggs
1 c water
¾ c oil
2 tsp. vanilla
Combine and mix for 10 minutes.
½ c sugar
½ c nuts
4 tsp. cinnamon
Combine and add to batter.
Bake as directed on cake mix box.
Sunshine Power Muffins - Relish
Cooking spray
1 c all-purpose flour
½ c old-fashioned oats
½ c flaxseed meal
¼ c sugar
2 tsp. baking powder
½ tsp. salt
½ c honey
2 eggs, lightly beaten
1 c plain yogurt
½ c orange juice
1 tsp. vanilla extract
2 tsp. finely grated orange rind
1 c chopped dried apricots
1/3 c granola
Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
Coat 12 muffin cups with cooking spray.
Instructions:
Combine flour and next five ingredients (flour through salt) in a mixing bowl.
Combine honey and next five ingredients (honey through orange rind) in a separate bowl. Whisk well.
Make a well in the center of dry ingredients. Pour wet ingredients into the well and stir until just combined. Gently fold in apricots.
Fill muffin cups two-thirds full. Sprinkle with granola.
Bake 18 to 20 minutes, until centers spring back when touched.

Ingredients:
1 (16-ounce) container sour cream
1/3 c sugar
1/4 c butter
1 tsp. salt
2 (1/4-ounce) envelopes active dry yeast
1/2 c warm water (100 to 110)
1 Tbsp. sugar
2 large eggs, lightly beaten
6 to 6 1/2 c bread flour
1/3 c butter, softened
1/2 c sugar
1 1/2 tsp. ground cinnamon
Creamy Glaze
Purple, green and gold sugar sprinkles
Instructions:
Cook sour cream, sugar, butter and salt in a medium saucepan over low heat, stirring often, until butter melts. Set aside and cool mixture to 100 to 110.
Stir together yeast, 1/2 c warm water and 1 Tbsp. sugar in a 1 c glass measuring cup; let stand 5 minutes.
Beat sour cream mixture, yeast mixture, eggs and 2 c flour at medium speed with a heavy-duty electric stand mixer until smooth. Reduce speed to low, and gradually add enough remaining flour (4 to 4 1/2 c) until soft dough forms.
Using dough hook, knead until smooth and elastic. Place in a well-greased bowl, turning to grease top. Cover and let rise in a warm place (85 degrees), free from drafts, 1 hour or until dough is doubled in bulk.
Punch down dough, and divide in half. Roll each portion into a 22 x 12 inch rectangle. Spread 1/3 c softened butter evenly on each rectangle, leaving a 1-inch border.
Stir together 1/2 c sugar and cinnamon, and sprinkle evenly over butter on each rectangle. Roll up each dough rectangle, jell-roll fashion, starting at 1 long side.
Place one dough roll, seam side down, on a lightly greased baking sheet. Bring ends of roll together to form an oval ring, moistening and pinching edges together to seal. Repeat with second dough roll.
Cover and let rise in a warm place, free from drafts, 20 to 30 minutes or until doubled in bulk.
Bake at 375 for 14 to 16 minutes or until golden. Slightly cool cakes on pans on wire racks (about 10 minutes).
Drizzle Creamy Glaze evenly over warm cakes; sprinkle with color sugars, alternating colors and form bands. Let cool completely.

Ingredients:
1 1/4 c finely chopped fresh cranberries
1/2 c finely chopped walnuts
1/4 c granulated sugar
1 package Duncan Hines Lemon Supreme Cake Mix
3/4 c milk
3 ounces cream cheese
4 large eggs
Instructions: Preheat oven to 350. Grease and flour two loaf pans. Stir together cranberries, walnuts and granulated sugar in large bowl; set aside. Combine cake mix, milk and cream cheese in large bowl. Beat at medium speed with electric mixer for 2 minutes. Add eggs, 1 at a time, beating for 2 minutes. Fold in cranberry mixture. Pour into prepared pans. Bake 45 to 50 minutes or until toothpick inserted in centers comes out clean. Cool in pans 15 minutes. Loosen loaves from pans. Invert into cooling rack. Turn right side up. Cool completely. Dust with confectioners' sugar.

Ingredients:
1/2 c sugar
1 tsp. cinnamon
2 cans (16.3-ounce) Grands Home-style refrigerated buttermilk biscuits
1/2 c chopped walnuts, if desired
1/2 c raisins, if desired
1 c firmly packed brown sugar
3/4 c butter or margarine, melted
Instructions: Heat oven to 350. Lightly greased 12-cup fluted tube pan. In large plastic food-storage bag, mix sugar and cinnamon. Separate dough into 16 biscuits; cut each into quarters. Shake in bag to coat. Arrange in pan, adding walnuts and raisins among the biscuit pieces. Mix brown sugar and butter, pour over biscuit pieces. Bake 28 to 32 minutes or until golden brown and no longer doughy in center. Cool in pan 10 minutes. Turn upside down onto serving plate; pull apart to serve. Serve warm.

Ingredients:
2 c sifted flour
¼ c sugar
4 tsp. baking powder
4 tbsp. butter
3 eggs
1 c milk
Instructions:
Sift dry ingredients into a large bowl. Cut in butter until mixture is crumbly.
Separate eggs – beat whites.
Beat yolks slightly; stir in milk. Pour all at once over flour mixture; stir lightly. Fold in egg whites.
Spread in a butter 9 inch pie pan.
Bake in hot oven for about 30 minutes.

Notes:
Prep time: 30 minutes; Cook time: 12 minutes; 6 servings
The trick to this dish is to reduce the tomato mixture to a thick sauce that won't break and liquefy when added to the eggs. If you've never made scrambled eggs this way before, you're in for a treat. Steady, slow cooking turns them incredibly creamy.
Total carbohydrates per serving: 7 grams (count only 6 digestible carbs when doing Atkins); protein: 19 grams; fat: 30 grams; calories: 371
Ingredients:
3 tbsp olive oil
1 medium onion, sliced
6 garlic cloves, chopped
2 medium green or red bell peppers, roasted
2 plum tomatoes, chopped
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
salt and pepper
12 eggs
3/4 stick (6 tbsp) butter
1/3 c thinly sliced fresh basil
6 ounces thinly sliced ham
Instructions:
Heat oil in large heavy skillet over medium heat. Saute onion 5 minutes, until softened; add garlic and cook 1 minute more. Add roasted peppers, tomatoes and cayenne. Cover and cook 10 minutes, until vegetables are very soft, stirring occasionally. Uncover and simmer over medium heat until sauce is thick, about 10 minutes, stirring often. Season to taste with salt and pepper. (Mixture can be made up to 2 days ahead and reheated.)
In large bowl, beat eggs until blended. In large non-stick skillet, melt butter over low heat. Add eggs and basil. Cook, stirring constantly with rubber spatula, 12 minutes, until soft curds form and eggs are barely set. Add pepper mixture and ham; stir just until mixed.

Notes:
Prep time: 20 minutes; Cook time: 5 minutes; 4 servings
These tasty burritos are perfect for brunch. For variety, add 1/2 cup chopped cooked ham or smoked turkey to the filling.
Total carbohydrates per serving: 16 grams (count only 6 digestible carbs when doing Atkins); protein: 21 grams; fat: 21 grams; calories: 317
Ingredients:
4 controlled carb flour tortillas
1 tbsp peanut or canola oil
3 green onions, chopped
1 can (4 ounces) green chiles, drained, patted dry and chopped
1 small tomato, diced
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp black pepper
8 eggs, beaten
pinch cayenne pepper
2 tbsp chopped cilantro
1/4 c green salsa
1/2 c grated cheddar cheese
Instructions:
Heat oven to 325 degrees. Wrap tortillas in foil and heat in oven 5 to 10 minutes.
In a medium nonstick skillet, heat oil over medium-high heat. Add green onions, chiles, tomato, salt and pepper; saute 3 minutes. Push mixture to side of pan. Add eggs and cayenne to skillet. Cook 1 to 2 minutes, stirring occasionally with rubber spatula, until soft, cream curds form. Stir in vegetable mixture into eggs. Divide mixture among warm tortillas, sprinkle with cilantro, a tablespoon of salsa and 2 tablespoons of cheese. Roll up tortillas.

Notes:
Prep and cook time: 20 minutes; Bake time: 1 hour 15 minutes; 8 servings. So tasty, you'll never miss the crust
Total carbohydrates per serving: 6.5 grams (count only 4 digestible carbs when doing Atkins); protein; 17 grams; fat: 27 grams; calories: 329
Ingredients:
4 ounces nitrate-free bacon
1/2 onion, thinly sliced
6 eggs
3/4 c heavy cream
2 boxes (10 oz each) frozen chopped broccoli or spinach, thawed and squeezed dry
1/2 lb Gruyere cheese, shredded
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
Instructions: Heat oven to 350. Butter a 10-inch tart pan or 9-inch deep pie plate. In a large skillet over medium heat, cook bacon until crisp. Drain on paper towels; coarsely chop. Remove all but 1 tbsp bacon drippings from skillet. Add onion and cook 5 minutes, until softened but not brown. In a large bowl, combine eggs, cream, broccoli, cheese, salt and pepper. Stir in bacon and onion. Pour mixture into prepared pan. Bake 1 hour and 15 minutes (tenting with foil, if necessary, to prevent over browning) or until a knife inserted in center comes out clean. Cool 5 minutes before cutting into wedges.

Ingredients:
Yield: 4 servings
8 slices Canadian bacon
4 English muffins, split
2 tsp. white vinegar
8 eggs
Salt and pepper, to taste
Hollandaise sauce (Recipe under Sauces)
Fresh chopped parsley, for garnish
Instructions:
Brown the bacon in a medium skillet and toast the English muffins, cut sides up, on a baking sheet under the broiler.
Fill a 10-inch nonstick skillet half full of water. Add white vinegar to the cooking water. This will make the egg white cook faster so it does not spread. Bring to a slow boil. Gently break 1 of the eggs into the water taking care not to break it. Repeat with remaining eggs. Reduce the heat to a gentle simmer. Cook 3 ½ minutes until the egg white is set and yolk remains soft. Remove with a slotted spoon, allowing the egg to drain. To assemble: Lay a slice of Canadian bacon on top of each muffin half, followed by a poached egg. Season with salt and pepper. Spoon hollandaise sauce over the eggs. Garnish with chopped parsley.

Notes: 12 Servings, 265 Calories, 1 g Carb, 16 g Protein, 21 g Fat, 11 g Saturated Fat, 161 mg Cholesterol, 713 mg Sodium, 181 mg Potassium, 595 IU Vitamin A, 4 mg Vitamin C, 162 mg Calcium, 0.9 mg Iron
Ingredients:
1/2 green pepper, chopped
1 lb Black Forest or smoked deli ham, chopped
2 tbsp butter
7 eggs
3/4 c heavy whipping cream
2 c Cheddar cheese
1/2 tsp salt
1/4 tsp pepper
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 350 degrees. In a skillet, saute green peppers and chopped deli ham until the peppers begin to soften and the ham is browned. Grease a 9X13 casserole dish. Pour in the green peppers and ham. In a bowl, beat the eggs, add the heavy whipping cream, salt, and pepper and whisk until all the ingredients are thoroughly combined. Pour egg mixture over the ham and green peppers. Use a fork to stir the ham and green peppers into the egg mixture. Sprinkle cheese on top.
Bake for 20 - 23 minutes depending on how done you like your eggs.

Notes:
Prep time: 15 minutes; Cook time: 10 minutes; 2 servings. 
White mushrooms are fine in this recipe, but more exotic varieties, such as morel, shiitake or cremini, would be even tastier.
Total carbohydrates per serving: 19 grams (count only 14.5 digestible carbs when doing Atkins); protein: 39 grams; fat: 42 grams; calories: 611
Ingredients:
2 tbsp butter, divided
4 oz mushrooms, sliced
1/4 c finely chopped red onion
2 cloves garlic, chopped
1/2 tsp dried thyme
1/2 tsp grainy Dijon mustard
salt and pepper
6 eggs
1/4 c chopped fresh parsley
3/4 c shredded Swiss cheese
Instructions: Melt 1 tbsp butter in a small nonstick skillet over medium-high heat; saute mushrooms, onion, garlic and thyme 4 minutes, until tender. Stir in mustard and season with salt and pepper; transfer to a bowl. In medium bowl, whisk eggs, parsley and 1/4 c cheese until mixed. In skillet, melt 1/2 tbsp butter over medium-high heat and add half the egg mixture. Tilt skillet or spread eggs to cover bottom. Cook until set, lifting omelette with rubber spatula to allow uncooked egg to flow underneath. Top omelette with 1/4 c shredded cheese and half the mushroom mixture. Fold up filled portion over center of omelette and roll it out onto serving plate. Repeat with remaining butter, egg mixture, cheese and mushroom mixture.

Ingredients:
1 prebaked deep dish pie shell
6 large eggs
2/3 c heavy cream or crème fraiche
1 c milk
8 ounces gruyere, emmenthal, or other Swiss-type cheese
1/4 tsp. freshly ground nutmeg
Instructions:
In a medium-sized bowl, whisk together the eggs, cream, and the milk until thoroughly blended. Season with the salt and pepper, then add the cheese and stir until it is blended,
Turn the mixture into the pre-baked pastry, and spread out the cheese evenly over the bottom of the pastry. Sprinkle the top with nutmeg if you've used a Swiss-type cheese and bake in the center of the oven until the filling is golden and puffed, and is completely baked through, about 30 minutes.
To test for doneness, shake the quiche - if it is solid without a pool of uncooked filling in the center, it is done. You may also stick a sharp knife blade into the center of the filling and if it comes out clean, the quiche is baked through.
Remove the quiche from the oven and serve immediately.

Ingredients:
9 inch pie crust
6 slices bacon
1 onion, sliced
3 eggs, beaten
1 1/2 c milk
1/4 tsp. salt
1 1/2 c shredded Swiss cheese
1 Tbsp. all-purpose flour
Instructions:
Prepare pie crust.
Preheat oven to 325.
In a skillet, cook bacon until crisp. Drain and reserve 2 Tbsp. of drippings. Crumble the bacon and set aside. Cook onion in skillet with reserved drippings; cook until onion is tender and then drain.
In a large bowl, mix together milk, salt and eggs. Stir in bacon and onion.
In a separate bowl, toss cheese and flour together, then add to egg mixture. Be sure to mix well.
Pour egg mixture into pie crust. Bake in preheated oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until knife inserted in center of quiche comes out clean.
If necessary, cover edge of crust with foil while baking to prevent burning or over browning. Let stand for 10 minutes before serving.

Source: WRAL 2013 NC State Fair Egg Association
Ingredients:
1/2 bag tater tots, thawed
6 Eggs
1 tbsp oil
1 small onion chopped
10 oz can rotel tomatoes and chilies, original
3/4 c shredded Mexican blend cheese
1/4 tsp cumin
1/2 pickled jalapeno pepper, finely chopped
1/2 c cream
1/2 can green chilies, chopped
salt and pepper
1 tbsp cilantro or parsley, chopped
1 large tomato, thinly sliced
Instructions:
Thaw tater tots and mash onto bottom of sprayed and heated waffle iron. Close lid and cook for 5 minutes until crust is crispy and brown.
Saute onion in oil just until soft. Beat the eggs vigorously and add the sauteed vegetables, rotel tomatoes and chilies, cheese, cumin, jalapeno pepper and cream. Salt and pepper to taste.
Place potato crust in frying pan and pour egg mixture over crust. Arrange slices of tomato evenly on top of frittata and sprinkle with chopped cilantro. Cook 2 minutes on stovetop on medium heat until edges are set.
Transfer skillet to oven and bake at 375 degrees for 20-25 minutes or until a knife inserted into the center comes out clean.
Let cool before cutting into wedges and serving.

Ingredients:
1 3/4 cups water
1 cup Old Fashioned Oats, or Quick Cooking Oats
1/8 teaspoon salt
1/4 cup dried cherries
1/2 teaspoon vanilla extract
2 tablespoons cherry jam, or to taste
1/4 cup 1 percent low-fat milk, plus more to taste
Instructions: Put water, oats, salt and cherries into a medium sized saucepan. Bring to a boil, reduce the heat and simmer for about 5 minutes for Old Fashioned Oats, or 1 minute for Quick Cooking Oats. Remove from heat. Stir in the vanilla extract and cherry jam. Put into serving bowls and pour 2 tablespoons of milk over each bowl.

Ingredients:
1 (14-ounce) can low-sodium fat-free chicken broth
1 c fat-free milk
½ tsp. salt
1 c uncooked quick-cooking grits
¼ c (3 ounces) shredded 2 % reduced-fat sharp Cheddar cheese
¼ c freshly grated Parmesan cheese
½ tsp. hot sauce
¼ tsp. ground white pepper
Instructions: Bring first 3 ingredients and 1⅓c water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat; gradually whisk in grits. Reduce heat to low, and simmer, stirring occasionally, 10 minutes or until thickened. Stir in Cheddar cheese and next 3 ingredients.

Ingredients:
4 c water
1 tsp. salt
2 garlic cloves, pressed
1 c uncooked regular grits
1 (12-oz) block sharp Cheddar cheese, shredded
½ c butter
1 tsp. seasoned pepper
1 tsp. Worcestershire sauce
¼ tsp. hot sauce
3 large eggs, lightly beaten
Paprika
Instructions:
Bring first 3 ingredients to a boil in a large saucepan; gradually stir in grits. Return to a boil; reduce heat, and simmer, stirring occasionally, 15 minutes or until thickened.
Add Cheddar cheese and next 4 ingredients, stirring until cheese melts. Remove from heat; let stand 10 minutes.
Stir in eggs, and pour into a lightly greased 11- x 7-inch baking dish or 2-quart baking dish. Sprinkle with paprika.
Bake grits at 350 degrees for 1 hour or until set.

Recipe Name: Tasso-Parmesan Grit Cake for Rabbit Tenderloin
Ingredients:
1 Tbsp. unsalted butter
1/4 c ground Tasso
5 c water
1 tsp. salt
1 1/2 c white or yellow grits
2 c grated Parmesan cheese
3 Tbsp. olive oil
Instructions:
Heat the butter in a saucepan over medium-high heat. Add the Tasso and cook, stirring constantly for 1 minute. Add the water and salt and bring to a boil. Slowly add the grits, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to very low and cook until the grits are done, 5-7 minutes. The grits should be stiff and not runny. Remove from the heat and add the cheese, stirring until thoroughly incorporated.
Brush a baking dish with olive oil and transfer the grits mixture to the dish. Refrigerate until thoroughly chilled, about 1 hour. Cut the grits cakes into 3 inch square or diamond shapes.
To cook the grits cakes, heat a flat-top griddle or non-stick skillet over medium heat. Brush both sides of each grits cake with a little bit of olive oil. Cook the grits cakes for 3-4 minutes on each side until brown and crispy.

Ingredients:
1 pound sweet Italian sausage
¾ pound lean ground beef
½ c minced onion
2 cloves garlic, crushed
1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes
2 (6-ounce) cans tomato paste
2 (6.5-ounce) cans canned tomato sauce
½ c water
2 Tbsp. white sugar
1 ½ tsp. dried basil leaves
½ tsp. fennel seeds
1 tsp. Italian seasoning
1 Tbsp. salt
¼ tsp. ground black pepper
4 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley
12 lasagna noodles
16 ounces ricotta cheese
1 egg
½ tsp. salt
¾ pound mozzarella cheese, sliced
¾ c grated Parmesan cheese
Instructions:
In a Dutch oven, cook sausage, ground beef, onion, and garlic over medium heat until well browned. Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, tomato sauce, and water. Season with sugar, basil, fennel seeds, Italian seasoning, 1 Tbsp. salt, pepper and 2 Tbsp. parsley. Simmer, covered, for about 1 ½ hours, stirring occasionally.
Bring a large pot of lightly salted water to a boil. Cook lasagna noodles in boiling water for 8 to 10 minutes. Drain noodles, and rinse with cold water.
In a mixing bowl, combine ricotta cheese with egg, remaining parsley, and ½ tsp. salt.
Preheat oven to 375 degrees.
To assemble, spread 1 ½ c of meat sauce in the bottom of a 9X13 inch baking dish. Arrange 6 noodles lengthwise over meat sauce. Spread with one half of the ricotta cheese mixture. Top with a third of mozzarella cheese slices. Spoon 1 ½ c meat sauce over mozzarella and sprinkle with ¼ c Parmesan cheese. Repeat layers, and top with remaining mozzarella and Parmesan cheese. Cover with foil; to prevent sticking, either spray foil with cooking spray, or make sure the foil does not touch the cheese.
Bake in a preheated oven for 25 minutes. Remove foil and bake an additional 25 minutes. Cool for 15 minutes before serving.

Ingredients:
9 to 10 Lasagna noodles, uncooked
¾ c water or vegetable broth
1 onion, chopped
1 large clove garlic, minced or 1 tsp. garlic powder
1 (28-ounce) can crushed tomatoes
1 pound zucchini, halved lengthwise and sliced
1 (10-ounce) package mushrooms, sliced (4 c)
½ tsp. oregano (2 stems stripped & chopped)
½ tsp. sugar
1 (15-ounce) container part-skim ricotta cheese
1 (4-ounce) c shredded skim milk mozzarella cheese, divided
¼ c grated Parm/Reg cheese, divided
¼ c shopped parsley or 2 Tbsp. parsley flakes
1 egg, beaten
½ tsp. salt
¼ tsp. pepper
Preheat oven to 350.
Instructions:
Heat water in large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic, stirring frequently. Cook 3 minutes or until tender. Add tomatoes, zucchini, mushrooms, oregano and sugar. Reduce heat, cover and simmer 10 minutes.
Combine ricotta, ¾ c mozzarella, 2 Tbsp. Parm, parsley, egg, salt and pepper in a medium bowl.
Layer 1 c vegetable sauce, 1/3 of the uncooked lasagna and half of the cheese mixture in a 13x9-inch baking dish. Repeat layers once. Top with remaining lasagna, vegetable sauce, ¼ c mozzarella and 2 Tbsp. Parm.
Cover tightly and bake 40 minutes. Uncover. Bake 10 minutes longer. Let stand 10 minutes before serving.

Recipe Name: Curried Lentils with Sweet Potatoes and Swiss Chard
Ingredients:
2 tbsp. extra virgin olive oil
1 medium onion, chopped
4 garlic cloves, minced
1 1-inch piece fresh ginger root, peeled and grated (or 1 tsp. ground ginger)
1 ½ tsp. garam masala
1 ½ tsp. curry powder
1 jalapeno pepper, seeded if desired, then minced
4 to 5 c vegetable broth as needed (or chicken broth)
2 lbs. orange-fleshed sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into ½-inch cubes (about 4 c)
1 ½ c dried lentils
1 bay leaf
1 lb. Swiss chard, center ribs removed, leaves thinly sliced (we don’t use this)
1 tsp. kosher salt, more to taste
½ tsp. ground black pepper
1/3 c chopped fresh cilantro
Finely grated zest of 1 lime
Juice of ½ lime
1/3 c finely chopped tamari almost, for garnish (we don’t use this)
¼ c chopped scallions, for garnish
Instructions:
In large saucepan, heat oil over medium heat. Add onion and sauté until translucent, 5 to 7 minutes. Add garlic, ginger, garam masala, curry powder and jalapeno. Cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
Stir in 4 cups broth, sweet potatoes, lentils and bay leaf. Increase heat to high and bring to a boil; reduce heat to medium, partially cover, and simmer for 25 minutes (If lentils seem dry add up to 1 cup stock, as needed.) Stir in chard and salt and pepper, and continue cooking until lentils are tender and chard is cooked, about 30 to 45 minutes total.
Just before serving, stir in cilantro, lime zest and juice. Spoon into a large shallow serving dish. Garnish with almost if desired and scallions.

Ingredients:
Kosher salt
8 c broccoli florets (4 heads)
1/2 pound farfalle (bow tie) pasta
2 Tbsp. unsalted butter
2 Tbsp. good olive oil
1 tsp. minced garlic
1 lemon, zested
1/2 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
1 Tbsp. freshly squeezed lemon juice
1/4 c toasted pignoli (pine) nuts
Freshly grated Parmesan, optional
Instructions:
Cook the broccoli for 3 minutes in a large pot of boiling salted water. Remove the broccoli from the water with a slotted spoon or sieve. Place in a large bowl and set aside.
In the same water, cook the bow-tie pasta according to the package directions, about 12 minutes. Drain well and add to the broccoli.
Meanwhile, in a small sauté pan, heat the butter and oil and cook the garlic and lemon zest over medium-low heat for 1 minute. Off the heat, add 2 tsp. salt, the pepper, and lemon juice and pour this over the broccoli and pasta. Toss well. Season to taste, sprinkle with the pignolis and cheese, if using, and serve.
To toast pignolis, place them in a dry sauté pan over medium-low heat and cook, tossing often, for about 5 minutes, until light brown.

Ingredients:
1/2 c butter or margarine
1/2 c all-purpose flour
1/2 tsp. salt
1/2 tsp. ground black pepper
1/4 tsp. ground red pepper
1/4 tsp. granulated garlic
2 c half-and-half
2 c milk
4 c shredded sharp Cheddar, divided
2 c shredded extra-sharp Cheddar
1 (16-ounce) package macaroni, cooked
Instructions: Melt butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Gradually whisk in flour until smooth; cook, whisking constantly, 2 minutes. Stir in salt and next 3 ingredients. Gradually whisk in half-and-half and milk, cook whisking constantly, 8 to 10 minutes or until thickened. Stir in 2 c sharp Cheddar. Stir in extra- sharp Cheddar until smooth. Remove from heat. Combine pasta and cheese mixture, and pour into a lightly greased 13 x 9 inch baking dish. Sprinkle with remaining sharp Cheddar cheese. Bake at 350 to 20 minutes (bake 15 minutes longer for a crusty top).

Ingredients:
2 servings
3 c udon noodles, cooked
Cornstarch
Salt
2-3 drops, sesame oil
4 tbsp teriyaki sauce
2 tbsp rice wine vinegar
2 tbsp vegetable oil
1 tbsp fresh ginger, minced
2 cloves garlic, minced
1 tsp red chili flakes
1 c shitake mushrooms, sliced
1 c Asian sprouts
2 c broccoli florets
½ c carrots, sliced
1 c cilantro leaves, chopped
Instructions: Heat oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add the carrots, mushrooms, and broccoli. Stir fry 2-3 minutes. Remove. Add oil, then sauté the ginger & garlic. Add the red chili flakes, sesame oil, teriyaki sauce and rice wine vinegar. Add noodles and let caramelize. Add all the veggies and toss to coat well.

Recipe Name: Orecchiette and Chicken Sausage with Sundried Tomato and Spinach
Ingredients:
12 ounces dried orecchiette pasta, boiled and drained
1 Tbsp. Canola oil
1 onion, chopped
2 large cloves garlic, minced
1/4 tsp. chili flakes (we used 1/2 of a large, fresh jalapeno, finely diced)
1 12-ounce package sundried tomato chicken sausage, cut into small pieces
1 pound firm, ripe tomatoes, rinsed, cored and chopped
8 ounces baby spinach leaves
2 ounces crumbled feta
Instructions: While pasta is cooking, heat skillet on medium-high heat and add Canola oil. Add onion and stir often until lightly browned around the edges, 8 to 10 minutes. Lower heat to medium and add garlic, chili flakes (jalapeno) and sausage. Stir until meat begins to brown, 5 to 10 minutes; if garlic begins to scorch, lower heat. Add tomatoes and spinach; stir until spinach is wilted, about 2 minutes. Combine pasta and sausage mixture and top with feta.

Ingredients:
1 1/2 c (12-ounces) orzo
1 large garlic clove, minced
2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
1 tsp. dried Greek oregano
1/4 c extra-virgin olive oil
2 c fresh or frozen edamame beans, cooked and drained
2 medium vine-ripened tomatoes, cored and diced
6 scallions, trimmed and thinly sliced
1 c packed fresh flat-leafed parsley leaves, chopped
2 tsp. salt
1/2 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
Cook the orzo in a large pan of boiling, salted water until al dente.
While the orzo cooks, make the dressing by whisking together the garlic, lemon juice, oregano and olive oil in a large bowl.
Drain the orzo, add it to the dressing and stir to coat. Let the orzo cool for 5 minutes and then stir in the cooked beans, tomatoes, scallions and parsley.
Season to taste with salt and pepper and serve warm or at room temperature.

Ingredients:
1 pound bacon or pancetta
1 pound linguine pasta
1 whole large onion
8 cloves to 12 cloves garlic
1 c white wine or stock
1 c chicken stock
4 whole eggs
1 ½ c parmesan cheese
1 bunch parsley
½ stick butter
1 to 2 Tbsp. black pepper
Instructions:
Slice bacon into pieces about one inch wide and cook until browned and crisp.
While the bacon’s cooking, boil water for pasta. Use about 2 Tbsp. salt per gallon of water. Boil linguine until al dente.
Chop onion. When bacon is crisp remove to a plate and reserve bacon grease to cook onions. Let them cook down over a medium-high heat for a couple of minutes. In the meantime, thinly slice the garlic and add to the onions; cook a couple of minutes more and then remove onions and garlic with a slotted spoon. Discard the grease.
Place the pan back on high heat until it starts to smoke a little. As soon as the pan begins to smoke, pour in the white wine. Whisk until pan is thoroughly deglazed and all the brown bits have come off of the bottom of the pan. Add 1 cup of chicken stock. Return bacon, onion and garlic back to the pan. Let simmer over medium heat.
Crack four eggs into a large bowl. Add most of the Parmesan cheese, and rough chop the parsley, adding it into the eggs as well. Leave a little Parmesan and parsley out for garnish. Mix well with a fork.
Drain the water from your pasta. Be sure the pasta is very hot. Add the pasta to the egg mixture and then add the bacon/onion mixture. Finally throw ½ stick of butter in. Mix it all together and pepper to taste. Garnish with parmesan and parsley.

Notes: This sauce is named for ladies of the night. They would place pots of it in their windows to tempt men into the bordellos. I like it because it's spicy, fast and easy (...no disrespect to the ladies.)
Ingredients:
2 Tbsp. (2 turns around the pan) extra-virgin olive oil
4 to 6 cloves garlic, chopped
1 tin flat anchovy fillets, drained
1 /2 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
20 oil-cured black olives, cracked away from pit and coarsely chopped
3 Tbsp. capers
1 (32-ounce) can chunky style crushed tomatoes
1 (14.5-ounce) can diced tomatoes, drained
A few grinds black pepper
1/4 c (a couple of handfuls) flat leaf parsley, chopped
1 pound spaghetti, cooked to al dente (with a bite)
Crusty bread, for mopping
Grated Parmigianino Reggiano or Romano, for passing, optional
Instructions:
Heat a large skillet over medium heat and add oil, garlic, anchovies, and crushed pepper. Sauté mixture until anchovies melt into oil and completely dissolve and garlic is tender, about 3 minutes: your kitchen never smelled so good! Add olives, capers, tomatoes, black pepper, and parsley. Bring sauce to a bubble, reduce heat, and simmer 8 to 10 minutes.
Toss sauce with cooked pasta. Pass bread and cheese at the table and serve with a simple salad of mixed bitter greens dressed with oil and vinegar, salt, and pepper.

Ingredients:
¾ pound penne pasta
1 pound shrimp
3 Tbsp. butter
3 Tbsp. olive oil
1 whole onion (small)
2 cloves garlic
½ c white wine
1 8-ounce can tomato sauce
1 c heavy cream
