5 tbsp butter, divided
1/2 c chicken broth
1 c heavy cream
2 tbsp balsamic vinegar
8 oz baby bella mushrooms, halved
1/2 c Parmesan cheese
salt and pepper to taste
Instructions: Lightly season the chicken breasts with salt and pepper on both sides. In a large saute pan over low-medium heat, add 2 tbsp butter, onions, salt and pepper. Cook until onions are caramelized, about 20 minutes. Remove from the heat. In a separate pan, over medium-high heat, pan-sear the chicken breasts in remaining 3 tbsp of butter. Brown on both sides. Remove from pan and set aside. Chicken will not be fully cooked. De-glaze the pan with chicken broth and scrape up the bits. Let simmer 5 minutes. Reduce the heat to low, add heavy cream, balsamic vinegar, mushrooms and a little salt and pepper. Let simmer 10 minutes. Add chicken breasts back to the sauce pan, simmer until the chicken is cooked all the way through, about 10 to 15 minutes. Remove the chicken breasts and plate. Add the Parmesan cheese and caramelized onion mixture to the sauce and stir until the cheese melts. Pour sauce on chicken.

Recipe Name: Peruvian-Style Roast Chicken with Green Sauce
Notes: Serves 4; Cook time 1 hour 30 minutes; total time 2 hours, plus at least 6 hours marinating time
Source: Once Upon a Chef and Andrea Lynn
Ingredients:
For the chicken: 
3 tbsp extra-virgin olive oil
1/4 c lime juice - 2 limes
4 large cloves garlic
1 tbsp kosher salt
2 tsp paprika
1 tsp black pepper
1 tbsp cumin
1 tsp dried oregano
2 tsp sugar
4 lb whole chicken

For the Green Sauce: Andrea Lynn
2 fresh jalapenos, including seeds and ribs, roughly chopped
1 tbsp aji Amarillo paste (hot yellow pepper paste from Peru, can be omitted)
1 c freshly picked cilantro leaves and small stems
2 tbsp grated cotija cheese or Parmesan cheese
1 medium clove garlic, minced (about 1 tsp)
1 tbsp vegetable oil
1 tsp white vinegar
1/2 tsp juice from 1 lime
1/2 c mayonnaise
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
Instructions:
For the Chicken
Combine all ingredients except the chicken in a blender or mini food processor, and blend until smooth. Remove the giblets from the inside of the chicken and pat the outside of the chicken dry with paper towels; place in a bowl, breast side up with the legs facing you. Using the handle of a wooden spoon or your fingers, loosen the skin from the flesh over the breasts and legs, being careful not to teat the skin or push all the way through (you want the marinade to stay inside the bird). Spoon about 2/3 of the marinade evenly underneath the skin, and spread the remaining 1/3 evenly over the skin. Marinate the chicken in the refrigerator for at least 6 hours or overnight.
Adjust the oven rack to the lower-middle position, and preheat the oven to 425 degrees. Line a roasting pan with aluminum foil for easy clean-up. Spray a rack (preferably a v-shape) with non-stick cooking spray and place the chicken on top. Tie the legs together with kitchen string. Roast for 20 minutes, until the skin is golden. Turn the heat down to 375 degrees, and continue to roast for about an hour and ten minutes more, or until the juices run clear when you cut between the leg and thigh. (Keep an eye on it -- if it's browning too quickly, cover it loosely with foil.) Ten the chicken with foil and let rest for about 20 minutes. Tilt the chicken over the roasting pan to release the juices, then transfer to a cutting board. Carve the chicken and serve with green sauce.

For the Green Sauce
Combine jalapenos, aji amarillo, cilantro, cotija garlic, oil, vinegar, and lime juice in a blender. Blend on high speed until smooth paste forms. Add mayonnaise and blend until homogenous. Transfer to a bowl and season to taste with salt and pepper. Serve with grilled chicken. Unused sauce can be stored in a covered container for up to a week.

Ingredients:
6 chicken breasts, bone-in, skin on (brined or not)
Fresh herbs, chopped
Butter, softened
Salt
Pepper
Instructions: Rinse and dry the chicken breasts. Stick your hand between the skin and the meat to separate the skin from the meat all around without removing it. Mash the butter with chopped herbs and a little salt and pepper and using your hand spread this mixture between the meat and skin. Salt and pepper the outside of the chicken, place in a roasting pan and bake at 425 for 40 minutes.

Recipe Name: Sautéed Chicken Cutlets with Optional Pan Sauces
Notes: Serves 4. The chicken breasts will be easier to slice in half if you freeze them for 15 minutes. To slice in half, place one hand on top of the breast to secure it, hold a chef's knife parallel to the cutting board, and slice through the middle of the breast hori­zontally. Serve with any of the sauces that follow, or Brandy, Cream, and Chive Pan Sauce, available free at www.cooksillustrated.com/october.
Ingredients:
4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts (6 to 8 ounces each), tenderloins removed, trimmed of excess fat, halved horizontally, and pounded 1/4 inch thick (see note above)
Table salt and ground black pepper
4 tsp. vegetable oil
Instructions: Adjust oven rack to middle position and heat oven to 200 degrees. Season both sides of each cutlet with salt and pepper. Heat 2 tsp. oil in 12-inch skillet over medium-high heat until smoking. Place 4 cutlets in skillet and cook without mov­ing them until browned, about 2 minutes. Flip cutlets and continue to cook until second sides are opaque, 15 to 20 seconds. Transfer to large oven safe plate. Add remaining 2 tsp. oil to now-empty skillet and repeat to cook remaining cutlets. Cover plate loosely with foil and transfer to oven to keep warm while making pan sauce.

Ingredients:
1 whole large chicken
cool water to cover
1 yellow onion peeled and split
1 bay leaf
3 sprigs of thyme
kosher salt
freshly ground black pepper
2 c white rice
3 tbsp butter
Instructions:
Put chicken, onion, bay leaf, thyme in a heavy-bottomed pot with cool water to barely cover the bird; add 2 tbsp kosher salt and 2 tsp black pepper. Cover and bring to a simmer and cook for at least an hour or until the bird "falling to pieces." A young chicken should take no more than an hour and a half and a laying hen will take up to 5 hours and have a better broth.
Once the bird is "falling to pieces," turn the heat off and let the bird rest for 30 minutes in the broth. Remove and reserve the bird and discard the onion, bay leaf and thyme. Tear the chicken into bite-size pieces and add it back to the pot. Bring the broth to a simmer and add the rice. Cook about 12 minutes. You want the rice just cooked through. Turn off the heat, test for seasonings, add more salt and pepper if needed.

Ingredients:
4 boneless, skinless chicken breasts
1 ¼ tsp. cumin
1 tbsp. chili powder
½ tsp. coriander
¼ tsp. paprika
1 ½ tsp. salt
½ tsp. black pepper
1/3 cup chicken broth
1/3 cup (packed) cilantro leaves, chopped 2 times
1 large onion, chopped
1 jalapeno, seeded and minced
4 garlic cloves, minced
1 tbsp. olive oil
Instructions:
Heat the oil in a medium sized sauté pan. Add the onion and sauté for 5 minutes. Add the cumin, chili powder, coriander, paprika and garlic and sauté two more minutes. Deglaze the pan with the chicken broth making sure to scrape up any browned bits. Turn off and set aside.
Sprinkle the chicken with salt and pepper. Add to crock pot. Squeeze the lime on top, add the cilantro, jalapeno, and pour the onion and garlic mixture over top.
Cook on low for 3-5 hours, or until cooked through (different crock-pots cook at varying heats) and tender. Remove chicken breasts from the crock pot, shred and place in a bowl. Scoop out the onions and add to the chicken. Depending on the amount of juices produced, you may want to add some or all of the juice back to the shredded chicken.
For fast and easy soft tacos, add this chicken to taco sized flour tortillas with shredded lettuce, avocado, tomato, cilantro, red onion, and cheese (I like Cheddar, pepper jack, or feta best). Add a dollop of sour cream and salsa, too, if you like.

Recipe Name: Sicilian Style Chicken with Roasted Lemon
Ingredients:
Roasted Lemons:
12 Thin lemon slices
Olive Oil
Chicken:
4 large, skinless, boneless chicken breast halves
all purpose flour
5 tbsp olive oil
1/2 c green Sicilian olives, pitted and sliced
3 tbsp drained capers
1 1/2 c chicken stock
3/4 c butter, cut in 4 pieces
3 tbsp chopped fresh parsley
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 325. Line baking sheet with parchment paper. Arrange lemon slices in single layer on prepared sheet. Brush lemon slices with olive oil and sprinkle lightly with salt. Roast until slightly dry and beginning to brown around edges, about 25 minutes. Sprinkle chicken with salt and pepper. Dredge chicken in flour to coat both sides, shake off excess. Heat 5 tbsp oil in heavy large skillet over high heat. Add chicken and cook until golden brown, about 3 minutes per side. Stir in olives and capers.
Add stock and bring to boil, scraping up browned bits from bottom of skillet. Boil until liquid is reduced to syrup consistency, turning chicken over at 3 minutes, about 5 minutes total. Add butter, roasted lemon slices, and 2 tbsp parsley, simmer until butter melts and chicken is cooked through, about 2 minutes. Season with salt and pepper. Transfer to platter. Sprinkle with remaining 1 tbsp parsley.

Ingredients:
2 teaspoons fresh thyme leaves
1 teaspoon whole fennel seeds
Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
1/3 cup good olive oil
1 lemon, halved and sliced ¼ inch thick
1 yellow onion, halved and sliced ¼ inch thick
2 large garlic cloves, thinly sliced
1 (4-pound) chicken, backbone removed and butterflied
½ cup dry white wine, such as Pinot Grigio
Juice of 1 lemon
Preheat the oven to 450 degrees.
Instructions:
Place the thyme, fennel seeds, 1 tablespoon salt, and 1 teaspoon pepper in a mini food processor and process until ground. Pour the olive oil into a small glass measuring cup, stir in the herb mixture, and set aside.
Distribute the lemon slices in a 12-inch cast-iron skillet and distribute the onion and garlic on top. Place the chicken, skin side down, on top of the onion and brush with about half the oil and herb mixture. Turn the chicken skin side up, pat it dry with paper towels (very important!), and brush it all over with the rest of the oil and herb mixture.
Roast the chicken for 30 minutes. Pour the wine into the pan (not on the chicken!) and roast for another 10 to 15 minutes, until a meat thermometer inserted into the thickest part of the breast registers 155 to 160 degrees.
Remove the chicken from the oven, sprinkle it with the lemon juice, cover the skillet tightly with aluminum foil, and allow to rest for 10 to 15 minutes. Cut the chicken into quarters or eighths, sprinkle with salt, and serve hot with the pan juices, cooked lemon, and onion.

Ingredients:
4 c cooked chicken, shredded or chopped
9 oz package frozen spinach, thawed and well drained
14 oz artichoke hearts (plain), chopped
8 oz cream cheese, at room temperature
1 c Parmesan cheese, grated
1 1/2 c Mozzarella cheese, shredded
2/3 c sour cream
1/2 c heavy cream
1/4 c mayonnaise
3 cloves garlic, minced
salt and pepper to taste
Instructions: Preheat oven to 375. Mix cream cheese, sour cream, heavy cream, mayonnaise, garlic, salt and pepper in a large mixing bowl until combined, then add everything else, except the mozzarella. Pour into a 9X13 baking dish, sprayed with cooking spray. Top with mozzarella and bake for 20 minutes. Increase the oven temp to 400 and bake for another 5 minutes until the cheese is golden and bubbly.

Recipe Name: Thai Red Curry with Chicken Thighs and Sweet Potatoes
Ingredients:
olive oil
8 chicken thighs
1 medium onion, cut into 1" slices
8 scallions, thinly sliced, white parts separated from green parts
2 14 ounce cans of coconut milk
Thai red curry paste (3 teaspoons)
1/4 cup organic or brown sugar
handful Thai Basil
a 2" piece of ginger, peeled and cut into 2 large chunks
4 sweet potatoes, peeled and cut into large yet bite-sized chunks (can also use regular potatoes)
extra basil for garnishing, cut into slivers
Instructions:
Heat a large dutch oven (or large cast iron skillet) over high heat and cover the bottom lightly with olive oil or coconut oil. Pat the chicken pieces dry with paper towels and carefully place them - skin side down - in the hot oil to brown. Don't crowd the pan - you might have to do this in 2 batches. Cook for about 2 minutes or until they release easily, the turn and cook one more minute. Remove the chicken breasts to a plate and set aside.
Turn the heat to medium and add the onions and the white parts of the scallions. Cook for about 4 minutes or until they begin to become translucent and then turn the heat to high. Allow the onions to brown very lightly. Add the curry paste and heat until it becomes fragrant. Deglaze the pan with two cans of coconut milk and the water. Stir to be sure the curry paste melts into the coconut milk. Add the sugar, basil and ginger. Stir everything together and allow it to simmer for a minute or two. Taste this mixture for heat level. It should be a little spicier than you'd like the end result to be. Add the sweet potatoes.
Simmer over medium heat for 10 minutes, and then add the chicken. Simmer for another 10-15 minutes, or until the chicken is cooked and the sweet potatoes are soft. Serve over hot rice, garnished with the extra basil and the green parts of the scallions. To allow for different heat preferences, serve with sriracha.

Ingredients:
2 1/2 gallons cold water
2 c kosher salt
1 c sugar
2 bay leaves, torn into pieces
1 bunch fresh thyme or 4 Tbsp. dried
1 whole head of garlic, cloves separated and peeled
5 whole allspice berries, crushed
4 juniper berries, smashed
Instructions: Place the water in a large non-reactive pot that can easily hold the liquid and the turkey. Add all the ingredients and stir for a minute or two until the sugar and salt dissolve. Put the turkey into the brine and refrigerate for 24 hours. If the turkey floats to the top, cover it with plastic wrap and weight it down with a plate and cans to keep it completely submerged in the brine. Note: You may halve or double the recipe. The important thing is to prepare enough brine to cover the turkey completely. To roast: Remove the bird from the brine and drain well. Pat dry. Follow the accompanying Best Way Brined Turkey instructions for roasting.

Ingredients:
Equal parts butter and flour
3 1/2 c turkey stock or chicken broth
Instructions: Pan juices from a brined bird may be saltier than from an unbrined one, so you may not want to use all of them. Strain the pan drippings from the turkey roasting pan into a freezer-proof container. Cool the drippings then freeze them so the fat will rise to the top and harden. Cook roux mixture over medium heat, stirring, until it begins to look grainy, about 3-4 minutes. Heat turkey stock or chicken broth or equal amounts of water and stock in a sauce pot. Whisk in a bit of the roux and bring to a simmer to thicken. Add more roux, whisking, until the gravy thickens as desired.

Ingredients:
2 Tbsp. softened butter
4 1/2 tsp. kosher salt
1 1/2 tsp. pepper
1/2 c chicken stock
Instructions:
Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Spread butter over the skin and sprinkle kosher salt and pepper over the skin and in the cavity. Tuck the wing tips under, truss the legs and place the turkey on a v-shaped rack in a roasting pan. Tent the breast with foil and place the turkey in the oven.
After about 1 hour, remove the foil and baste the turkey with chicken stock. Re-baste it with pan drippings and more stock, if desired, every 20 minutes. Roast the turkey until the internal thigh temperature reaches 165 degrees, 1 3/4-2 1/4 hours.

Ingredients:
5 ½ pounds apples-peeled, cored and finely chopped
4 c white sugar
2 tsp. ground cinnamon
¼ tsp. ground cloves
¼ tsp. salt
Instructions:
Place the apples in a slow cooker. In a medium bowl, mix the sugar, cinnamon, cloves and salt. Pour the mixture over the apples in the slow cooker and mix well.
Cover and cook on high 1 hour.
Reduce heat to low and cook 9-11 hours, stirring occasionally, until the mixture is thickened and dark brown.
Uncover and continue cooking on low 1 hour. Stir with a whisk, if desired to increase smoothness.
Spoon the mixture into sterile containers, cover and refrigerate or freeze.

Ingredients:
1 (10-oz.) jar seedless raspberry fruit spread (or any other fruit)
2 tbsp. orange liqueur
½ tsp. vanilla
Instructions: Microwave fruit spread in microwave-safe glass bowl at high 45 seconds or until warm and easy to stir. Stir in liqueur and vanilla. Let stand 10 minutes or until slightly thickened but still smooth. Use as sauce for pound cake or other dessert.

Ingredients:
4 c coarsely chopped peeled Bartlett pears (2 ½ pounds)
1 c sugar
1 tsp. ground cardamom
2 tsp. minced peeled fresh ginger
2 tsp. fresh lemon juice
¼ tsp. salt
Instructions:
Process pears in a food process until smooth. Combine pear puree and remaining ingredients in a medium sauté pan over medium heat. Bring to a simmer, stirring occasionally. Cook for 3 hours or until mixture is thick.
Spoon butter into hot canning jar, filling only ¼ inch from top. Immediately wipe rim, using towel dipped into hot water. Place lid on jar; seal tightly. Repeat with remaining jars.
Arrange jars in large pot. Add boiling water to pot so that at least 1 inch of water covers tops of jars. Cover pot and boil rapidly 15 minutes. Remove jars from pot. Cool to room temperature. Press center of each lid. If lid stays down, jar is sealed (if lid pops up, store butter in refrigerator.)
Store in a cool dry place up to 1 year. Refrigerate after opening.

Ingredients:
4 pounds medium pears, quartered and cored
2 c sugar
2 tsp. grated orange zest
¼ tsp. ground nutmeg
¼ c orange juice
Instructions:
Place pears into a large pot over medium heat, and add just enough water to cover the bottom of the pot and keep them from sticking, about ½ c. Cook until the pears are soft, about 30 minutes. Press pears through a sieve or food mill, and measure out 2 quarts of pulp.
Pour the pear pulp and sugar into a large saucepan and stir to dissolve sugar. Stir in the orange zest, nutmeg and orange juice. Cook over medium heat until the mixture is thick enough to mount in a spoon. When the mixture begins to thicken, stir frequently to prevent scorching on the bottom. This will take about 1 hour.
Ladle the pear butter into hot sterile jars, leaving ¼ inch of headspace. Remove air bubbles by sliding a metal spatula around where the pear butter touches the glass. Wipe jar rims clean, and seal with lids and rings. Process for 20 minutes in a boiling water bath. The water should cover the jars by 1 inch.

Ingredients:
For every gallon of pulp add:
6 c. sugar
1/2 tsp. salt
2 tsp. cinnamon
Instructions:
Cook ripe pears in very little water until soft. Run pear mix through food mill, will look like applesauce.
Place pulp into large kettle. Add ingredients.
Being very careful not to burn, cook pulp until boiling and boil until thick enough to spread. Use a cold plate to determine if thick enough. Process in jars.

Ingredients:
8 c peeled, cored and chopped pears
1 c unsweetened pineapple juice
8 c white sugar
Instructions:
Place chopped pears into a large pot, and pour pineapple juice over them to prevent them from browning. Stir in sugar, and bring to a boil over medium-high heat. Stir frequently to prevent scorching. When the pears are at a full boil, reduce heat to medium, and cook until the mixture is the color and texture of honey. The longer you cook it, the thick it gets. Cooking time is usually 2 to 3 hours.
Ladle into hot sterile jars, filling to within ¼ inch of the top. Wipe rims with a clean damp cloth, and seal jars with lids and rings. Process in a boiling water canner for 10 minutes, or the amount of time recommended by your local extension for your area.

Notes: I always double this recipe, I use them so much.
Ingredients:
2 lemons, preferably organic
1/3 c Coarse Sea Salt
1/2 c freshly squeezed lemon juice
About 1/2 c extra virgin olive oil
Instructions:
Scrub the lemons and dry them well. Cut each lemon lengthwise into 8 wedges. In a bowl, toss the lemon wedges, salt and lemon juice to coat the fruit. Transfer to a 2-c glass container with a non-metal lid. Close the container tightly and let the lemons ripen at room temperature for 7 days. Shake the container daily to evenly distribute the salt and juices. To store, add olive oil to cover and keep in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.
'Goop' ... for me, when I do the preserved lemons, the lemon slices stay relatively whole. By the goop, I mean everything else, the liquid/oil that is quite thick that surrounds the lemon slices. That is what I slide under the skin and then slather all over the outside.
I use the 'goop' all the time... in marinades (currently, I am marinating boneless chicken breasts in the goop, fresh thyme and rosemary, garlic, salt and pepper and a little olive oil to grill out tomorrow); in salad dressings, in place of lemon juice; you can make a nice lemon garlic mayo if you mix the goop, minced garlic, salt and pepper with Hellman’s (or make your own aioli with it) a€" use it in basically any savory dish that calls for lemon juice.
My favorite way to use the actual lemons themselves is in a vegetable couscous I make. Make your couscous (I usually use chicken broth for flavor, and add seasoning to the pot before it cooks, such as cumin or herbs de Province) then cool the couscous. In the meantime, chop a couple of slices of the lemon fine; also chop additional veggies: carrots, yellow squash, zucchini, cherry tomatoes halved, or any additional vegetables you like. I like them raw but you could steam them if you like. Toss everything together with a little olive oil and additional seasonings: salt and pepper for sure, perhaps paprika or others. You can also make a quick vinaigrette with olive oil, red or white wine vinegar, a little course ground mustard and the seasonings for a vinaigrette with which to toss it all. It is fresh, good for you and tastes great.

Ingredients:
4 pounds Bartlett pears (unpeeled), cored, cut into 1-inch chunks
½ dry white wine
2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
1 ½ c sugar
4 orange slices
1 lemon slice
4 whole cloves
1 vanilla bean, split lengthwise
1 cinnamon stick
½ tsp. ground cardamom
Pinch of salt
Instructions:
Combine pears, wine and lemon juice in heavy large saucepan. Cover and simmer until pears are soft, pushing unsubmerged pears into liquid occasionally, about 25 minutes.
Force through food mill or coarse sieve to remove pear peel. Transfer to processor and puree.
Return puree to heavy large sauce pan. Add remaining ingredients. Stir over low heat until sugar dissolves. Increase heat to medium and boil gently until mixture thickens and mounds slightly on spoon stirring often, about 50 minutes.
Discard fruit slices, cloves, vanilla and cinnamon. Spoon butter into hot canning jar, filling only ¼ inch from top. Immediately wipe rim, using towel dipped into hot water. Place lid on jar; seal tightly. Repeat with remaining jars.
Arrange jars in large pot. Add boiling water to pot so that at least 1 inch of water covers tops of jars. Cover pot and boil rapidly 15 minutes. Remove jars from pot. Cool to room temperature. Press center of each lid. If lid stays down, jar is sealed (if lid pops up, store butter in refrigerator.)
Store in a cool dry place up to 1 year. Refrigerate after opening.

Ingredients:
10 cups squash
4 cups onions
3 or 4 bell peppers
1/3 cup salt
Combine and let sit overnight. Drain.
Add:
2 tsp cornstarch
4 ½ cups sugar
2 tsp celery seed
2 ½ cups vinegar
1 tsp dry mustard
1 tsp black pepper
2 tbsp prepared mustard
1 tsp turmeric
½ tsp cayenne
Instructions: Mix thoroughly. Boil for 30 minutes. Pour into prepared jars and seal.

Ingredients:
¼ c firmly packed brown sugar
¼ c sweet paprika
3 Tbsp. black pepper
3 Tbsp. coarse salt
1 Tbsp. hickory-smoked salt or more coarse salt
2 tsp. garlic powder
2 tsp. onion powder
2 tsp. celery seeds
1 tsp. cayenne pepper
Instructions: Combine all the ingredients in a mixing bowl and stir to mix. Use your fingers to break up any lump of brown sugar. Store the rub in an airtight jar away from heat or light; it will keep for at least 6 months.

Ingredients:
2 1/2 Tbsp. paprika
2 Tbsp. salt (sometimes I halve the salt)
2 Tbsp. garlic powder
1 Tbsp. black pepper
1 Tbsp. onion powder
1 Tbsp. cayenne pepper
1 Tbsp. dried leaf oregano
1 Tbsp. dried thyme
Instructions: Combine and store.

Ingredients:
3/4 c chili powder
2 tbsp ground cumin
2 tbsp dried oregano
2 tbsp dried minced onion
2 tbsp seasoned salt
2 tbsp sugar
2 tsp dried minced garlic

Recipe Name: Dry Rub for Fall Off the Bone Oven Baked Ribs
Ingredients:
1 teaspoon garlic salt
1 teaspoon cumin
20 turns of the pepper grinder
1 teaspoon paprika
1 teaspoon chili powder
1 teaspoon oregano
1/4 teaspoon of white pepper
1/4 cup of brown sugar

1/4 tsp cumin
1/2 tsp onion
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
1 tbsp cornstarch
2 tsp Chili powder
1 tsp salt
1 tsp sugar
1/4 tsp garlic powder
1/4 tsp crushed chicken bouillon cube
1 tsp paprika

3 tbsp parsley
3 tbsp basil
1 tbsp garlic powder
1 tsp rosemary
1/4 tsp black pepper
1 tsp onion powder
1/2 tsp red pepper flakes
2 1/2 tsp oregano
1 tsp thyme

1 tbsp garlic powder
2 to 3 tsp cayenne pepper
2 tsp onion powder
2 tsp dried thyme
2 tsp dried parsley
2 tsp sugar
2 tsp salt
1 tsp paprika
1 tsp ground allspice
1/2 tsp black pepper
1/2 tsp dried crushed red pepper
1/2 tsp ground nutmeg
1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
Instructions: Combine all ingredients; store in an airtight container for up to 3 months.

Ingredients:
1 tsp ground bay leaf
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1 tsp black pepper
3 tbsp kosher salt
1 tsp smoked paprika
1 tsp dry mustard
1/4 tsp ginger
1/4 tsp allspice
1/4 tsp cardamom
1/4 tsp cinnamon
1 tsp cayenne pepper
1 tsp celery seed

2 tsp rosemary
1 tsp sage
2 tsp garlic salt
1 tsp black pepper
2 tsp thyme

1/4 tsp black pepper
1/2 tsp basil
1 tbsp garlic powder
1/4 c dried parsley
1 tbsp dill
1 tbsp onion powder

2 Tbsp. chili powder
2 Tbsp. sweet paprika
1 Tbsp. ground coriander
1 Tbsp. garlic powder
1 Tbsp. crushed dried oregano
2 tsp. ground cumin
1 tsp. cayenne pepper
1 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
1 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
Instructions: Sprinkle generously on chicken.

Ingredients:
1 Tbsp. chili powder
1/4 tsp. garlic powder
1/4 tsp. onion powder
1/4 tsp. crushed red pepper flakes
1/4 tsp. dried oregano
1/2 tsp. paprika
1.5 tsp. ground cumin
1/2 to 1 tsp. sea salt (more or less to taste)
1 tsp. black pepper
Instructions:
In small bowl, mix all ingredients and store in airtight container. (Or you can mix it together in the container you are going to store it in–just give it a shake!)
Add 2 to 3 tablespoons of this mixture plus 1/2 to 3/4 cup of water to one pound of cooked meat (of your choice). Simmer over medium heat, stirring frequently until there is very little liquid left in the pan.
Serve with your favorite taco topping! I recommend lots of sour cream, fresh salsa, guacamole, lettuce, cheese and tomato.

Ingredients:
1 tbsp ground cinnamon
2 tsp ground ginger
3 dry allspice berries
1 whole star anise pod
1/2 tsp fresh ground nutmeg
Instructions: Purchase quality ground cinnamon and dry ginger. (Alton gets his from TheSpiceHouse.com) Grind the allspice berries along with the star anise pod in a pepper mill or spice grinder. Grate a nutmeg on a micro grater until you have the desired amount. Combine in a small jar with a tight fitting lid. Use within 1 month.

Ingredients:
½ c butter
4 egg yolks
2-3 Tbsp. fresh squeezed lemon juice
¼ tsp. salt
Dash of pepper
Instructions: Heat butter until bubbly. Meanwhile, place egg yolks, fresh lemon juice, salt and pepper in blender. Turn blender on and off quickly. Then turn to high speed and slowly add bubbly butter in a very thin but steady stream. Serve immediately over broccoli, asparagus, or grilled tomatoes. Also good on fish or for Eggs Benedict.

Ingredients:
1 large garlic clove, chopped
2 Tbsp. chopped fresh flat-leaf parsley
1 ½ tsp. anchovy paste
1 tsp. Dijon mustard
1 ½ Tbsp. freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ c good mayonnaise
Instructions: Place the garlic and parsley in the bowl of a food processor fitted with a steel blade and process until minced. Add the anchovy paste, mustard, lemon juice and mayonnaise and process again to make a smooth dressing. (Refrigerate the Caesar dressing if not using it immediately.)

Ingredients:
1 c mayonnaise
1/4 c chili sauce
1/4 c ketchup
1 tsp. mustard
1/2 c vegetable oil
1 tsp. Worcestershire
1 tsp. black pepper
1 Tbsp. minced onion
1 Tbsp. minced garlic
Juice of 1 lemon
Instructions: I don’t usually add the oil. Shake well in jar. Let set overnight before using.

Ingredients:
1 Tbsp. olive oil
½ pound country ham cut into small pieces
¼ c diced onions
1 Tbsp. butter
2 ¼ c cream or milk or half and half
½ t pepper
1 Tbsp. roux
Instructions: Heat oil in stock pot. Add country ham to stock pot and sauté until hot throughout. Do not overcook. Remove ham, leaving the fat. Add onions to the stock pot and sauté until tender. When onions are tender, add butter to melt and then add cream, ham and pepper. Bring to a simmer, stirring constantly. Add roux, a spoonful at a time, stirring in. Add only enough roux to slightly thicken. Cook 8 – 10 minutes.

Recipe Name: Cranberries in J. Lohr Seven Oaks Cabernet Sauvignon
Ingredients:
12 oz package of fresh cranberries
1 1/2 c granulated sugar
1 c J. Lohr Estate Seven Oaks Cabernet Sauvignon
1 Cinnamon Stick
1 piece of orange rind (about 7 inches long)
Instructions: Pick over the cranberries, removing stems and any shriveled berries. Rinse under cold running water; drain. Combine sugar and J. Lohr Estate Seven Oaks Cabernet Sauvignon in a non-corrosive heavy medium-sized pot; bring to a boil, stirring constantly, over moderate heat. Stir in cranberries, cinnamon stick and orange rind. Increase heat to high and return mixture to a boil, stirring constantly. Reduce heat to medium -low; simmer partially covered, stirring occasionally until the cranberries burst, 10 to 15 minutes. Remove from heat and discard the cinnamon stick. Carefully lift out the piece of orange rind; let cool slightly. Julienne the rind, then stir back into cranberry mixture. (R.B. requested that I just discard the rind and I did) Let cool to room temperature. Enjoy immediately, or spoon into sterilized jars, seal and store in the refrigerator for up to two months.

Ingredients:
1 tbsp. olive oil
2 tsp. creole seasoing
2 tsp. minced shallots
1 tbsp. minced garlic
1 pound crawfish tail
2 c heavy whipping cream
1 tbsp hot saue
1 tbsp Worcestershire sauce
Salt and pepper
1 tbsp. unsalted butter
¼ c chopped green onion
Instructions: Heat olive oil in pan over medium heat. Saute shallots and garlic for 30 seconds. Add crawfish and creole seasoning. Saute an additional 1 to 2 minutes. Add cream, hot sauce and Worcestershire sauce. Bring liquid to a boil, then reduce to simmer. Simmer the cream until it thickens and reduces by half, about 5 minutes. Add butter, salt, pepper to taste. Fold in green onions and serve over already cooked fillets.

Recipe Name: Creole Mustard Sauce for Rabbit Tenderloin
Ingredients:
¾ cup heavy whipping cream
¾ cup Creole mustard
¼ cup sour cream
Instructions: In a small saucepan, bring the cream to a low-boil over medium heat. Whisk in the mustard and sour cream and simmer for 5 minutes. Hold warm.

Notes:
This is a brown sauce. It’s a combination of a dark brown roux, tomato paste, browned veggies (mirepoix), herbs and rich meat stock.
Espagnole sauce is the basis for Bordelaise sauce (with red wine, shallot, bay leaf, and thyme), sauce Robert (with white wine and onion sautéed in butter), and Chasseur sauce, aka Hunter’s sauce (with mushroom, shallot, white wine, and tomato).
Ingredients:
1 small carrot, coarsely chopped
1 medium onion, coarsely chopped
½ stick (1/4 cup) unsalted butter
¼ c all-purpose flour
4 c hot beef stock or reconstituted beef-veal demi-glace concentrate*
¼ c canned tomato puree
2 large garlic cloves, coarsely chopped
1 celery rib, coarsely chopped
½ tsp. whole black peppercorns
1 Turkish or ½ California bay leaf
Instructions:
Cook carrot and onion in butter in a 3-quart heavy saucepan over moderate heat, stirring occasionally, until golden, 7 to 8 minutes. Add flour and cook roux over moderately low heat, stirring constantly, until medium brown, 6 to 10 minutes. Add hot stock in a fast stream, whisking constantly to prevent lumps, then add tomato puree, garlic, celery, peppercorns, and bay leave and bring to a boil, stirring. Reduce heat and cook at a bare simmer, uncovered, stirring occasionally, until reduced to about 3 cups, about 45 minutes.
Pour sauce through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding solids.
*Available at some specialty foods shops and cooking.com (stock requires a dilution ration of 1:16; ¼ c concentrate to 4 cups water).

Ingredients:
1/4 c lime juice
1/3 c water
2 Tbsp. olive oil
4 cloves garlic, crushed
2 tsp. soy sauce
1 tsp. salt
1/2 tsp. liquid smoke flavoring
1/2 tsp. cayenne pepper
1/2 tsp. ground black pepper
Instructions: In a large resealable plastic bag, mix together the lime juice, water, olive oil, garlic, soy sauce, salt and liquid smoke flavoring. Stir in cayenne and black pepper. Place desired meat in the marinade, and refrigerate at least 2 hours, or overnight. Cook as desired.

Recipe Name: Five French Mother Sauces - White or Béchamel Sauce
Notes:
Yield: Makes about 1 cup
This is a classic white sauce commonly referred to as cream sauce. Use as a base for many different sauces including cheese sauce.
Ingredients:
2 tbsp. butter
2 tbsp. flour
1 ¼ cups milk, heated
Salt
Freshly ground pepper
Instructions:
Melt the butter in a heavy-bottomed saucepan. Stir in the flour and cook, stirring constantly, until the paste cooks and bubbles a bit, but don’t let it brown, about 2 minutes. Add the hot milk, continuing to stir as the sauce thickens. Bring it to a boil. Add salt and pepper to taste, lower the heat, and cook, stirring for 2 to 3 minutes more. Remove from the heat. To cool this sauce for later use, cover it with wax paper or pour a film of milk over it to prevent a skin from forming.
Cheese Sauce
Stir in ½ cup grated Cheddar cheese during the last 2 minutes of cooking, along with a pinch of cayenne pepper.
How hot should the milk be?
Warm the milk on low heat just until little bubbles begin to form at the edges. Then remove from heat.

Ingredients:
3 Tbsp. butter
1/3 c thinly sliced green onions
1/4 c chopped cilantro
2 Tbsp. finely chopped peeled fresh ginger
4 garlic cloves, chopped
8 ounces fresh shiitake mushrooms, stemmed, caps sliced
6 Tbsp. soy sauce
1 1/2 c whipping cream
3 Tbsp. fresh lime juice
Lime wedges
Fresh cilantro sprigs
Instructions: Combine butter, sliced green onions, cilantro, ginger and chopped garlic to skillet and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Mix in mushrooms and soy sauce and simmer 30 seconds. Add whipping cream and simmer until sauce lightly coats back of spoon, about 3 minutes. Stir in lime juice.

Notes:
Yield: 1 cup
Hollandaise is a rich, buttery yellow sauce that’s probably best known for its starring role atop eggs benedict.
Ingredients:
4 egg yolks
1 tbsp. freshly squeezed lemon juice
½ c unsalted butter, melted (1 stick)
Pinch cayenne
Pinch salt
Instructions: Vigorously whisk the egg yolks and lemon juice together in a stainless steel bowl and until the mixture is thickened and doubled in volume. Place the bowl over a saucepan containing barely simmering water (or use a double boiler,) the water should not touch the bottom of the bowl. Continue to whisk rapidly. Be careful not to let the eggs get too hot or they will scramble. Slowly drizzle in the melted butter and continue to whisk until the sauce is thickened and doubled in volume. Remove from heat, whisk in cayenne and salt. Cover and place in a warm spot until ready to use for the eggs benedict. If the sauce gets too thick, whisk in a few drops of warm water before serving.

Source: The Keto Diet Blog - Keto Diet App
Ingredients:
1/4 c heavy whipping cream
2 tbsp butter
1/4 c cream cheese or soft goat cheese
1/2 c grated Cheddar or hard goat cheese
pinch salt, if needed
1-2 tbsp water or more cream if you need to thin it down
Optional seasonings
1/4 tsp cayenne pepper
1/2 tsp garlic powder
1/2 tsp onion powder
1/2 tsp paprika
2-4 tbsp freshly chopped herbs of choice
Instructions: Gently heat the cream and butter in a small sauce pan. Once heated, add the cream cheese. Stir until melted and bring to a simmer. Once it bubbles remove from heat. Add cheddar cheese and mix until smooth and creamy. If you prefer a thicker sauce, cook for 3-5 more minutes while stirring. If too thick, add a splash of water or cream. Serve over steamed vegetables, fish and meat. If you can use the whole receipt, only make one serving. The sauce should be eaten immediately or kept at room temperature before serving.

Ingredients:
1/2 cup freshly squeezed lemon or orange juice
1/2 cup thin soy sauce
1/4 cup honey
1/4 cup extra virgin olive oil
1 tbsp your favorite hot sauce
8 cloves garlic, minced
1/2 cup chopped fresh sage, basil, cilantro sprigs, or mint (R.B. has used a basil/mint combo)
Instructions: Combine all ingredients and use half on or in meat of choice and then baste with the rest.

Recipe Name: Lemon, Caper, and Parsley Pan Sauce
Ingredients:
2 tsp. vegetable oil
1 medium shallot, minced (about 3 Tbsp.)
1 tsp. all-purpose flour
3/4 c low-sodium chicken broth
2 tsp. juice from 1 lemon
1 Tbsp. capers, rinsed and chopped
1 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley leaves
1 Tbsp. cold unsalted butter
Table salt and ground black pepper
Instructions: Add oil to empty skillet used to cook chicken and return pan to low heat. Add shallot and cook, stirring often, until softened, 1 to 1 ½ minutes. Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, seconds. Add broth, increase heat to medium high, and bring to simmer, scraping pan bottom to loosen browned bits. Simmer rapidly until reduced to 1/2 c, 2 to 3 minutes. Stir in accumulated chicken juices; return to simmer and cook 30 seconds. Off heat, whisk in lemon juice, capers, parsley, and butter; season with salt and pepper. Spoon over cutlets and serve immediately.

Recipe Name: Onion Blue Cheese Sauce for Ribeye
Ingredients:
4 tbsp. butter
1 whole very large yellow onion, sliced
1 c heavy cream
½ c crumbled blue cheese
Instructions: Sauté onions in 4 tablespoons butter over high heat. Cook for 5 to 7 minutes, or until dark and caramelized. Reduce heat to simmer and pour in cream. Cook for 3 to 5 minutes, or until reduced by half. Stir in blue cheese until melted.

Ingredients:
1/4 cup fresh lemon juice
3/4 cup vegetable oil
1/2 cup chopped onion
1/2 cup chopped green onions
1/4 cup chopped celery
2 tablespoons chopped garlic
2 tablespoons prepared horseradish
3 tablespoons Creole whole-grain mustard
3 tablespoons ketchup
3 tablespoons chopped parsley
1 teaspoon salt
1/4 teaspoon cayenne pepper
1/8 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper
Instructions: Combine all ingredients in the bowl of a food processor and process for 30 seconds. Refrigerate for at least an hour. Will keep for several days in an airtight container in the refrigerator.

Ingredients:
1 part oil or butter
1 part flour
Instructions: Heat oil or butter in pot; add flour, stirring constantly over medium heat until desired brown is reached, approximately 10 minutes.

Recipe Name: Sherry, Red Pepper and Toasted Garlic Pan Sauce
Ingredients:
2 tsp. vegetable oil
3 medium garlic cloves, minced or garlic press (about 1 Tbsp.)
1 tsp. all-purpose flour
1/4 tsp. paprika
3/4 c low-sodium chicken broth
1/2 c plus 1 tsp. dry sherry
1/4 c jarred roasted red peppers, patted dry and cut into1/4 inch dice
1 tsp. chopped fresh thyme leaves
1 Tbsp. cold unsalted butter
Table salt and ground black pepper
Instructions: Add oil to empty skillet used to cook chicken and return pan to low heat. Add garlic and cook, stirring constantly, until lightly browned, 30 to 60 seconds. Add flour and paprika; cook, stir­ring constantly, 30 seconds. Add broth and ½ c sherry; increase heat to medium-high and bring to simmer, scraping pan bottom to loosen browned bits. Simmer rapidly until reduced to 3/4 c, 3 to 5 minutes. Stir in any accumulated chicken juices; return to simmer and cook 30 seconds. Off heat, whisk in peppers, thyme, butter, and remaining tsp. sherry; season with salt and pepper. Spoon over cutlets and serve immediately.

Ingredients:
1 c sugar
2 ounces dry mustard
1 c vinegar
3 eggs
Instructions: Beat 3 eggs well. In a saucepan over medium heat, combine sugar, mustard, vinegar and eggs. Whisk continuously to keep eggs from solidifying. Cook approximately 15 minutes or until thickened.

Ingredients:
1 c mayonnaise
1/3 c green olives
1/4 c onion
1/3 c pickle relish
1 Tbsp. capers
Instructions: Mince onion and chop olives and capers. Combine with pickle relish and drain well. Stir into mayonnaise. Refrigerate at least 2 hours.

Ingredients: Yield: 4 cups
Instructions:
Use it on pizza, pasta, meat or chicken. Dress it up or down with ground sausage, mushrooms, olives, or any manner of veggies.
¼ c extra-virgin olive oil
1 Spanish onion, ¼-inch dice
4 garlic cloves, peeled and thinly sliced
3 tbsp. chopped fresh thyme leaves, or 1 tbsp. dried
½ medium carrot, finely grated
2 (28-oz) cans peel whole tomatoes, crushed by hand and juices reserved
Salt spaghetti, cooked al dente
Whole basil leaves, for garnish
Grated Parmesan
In a 3-quart saucepan, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the onion and garlic, and cook until and light golden brown, about 8 to 10 minutes. Add the thyme and carrot, and cook 5 minutes more, until the carrot is quite soft. Add the tomatoes and juice and bring to a boil, stirring often. Lower the heat and simmer for 30 minutes until as thick as hot cereal. Season with salt and serve. This sauce holds 1 week in the refrigerator or up to 6 months in the freezer.
When ready to use, the cooked pasta should be added to a saucepan with the appropriate amount of sauce. Garnish with basil leaves and cheese.

Ingredients:
½ c Canola Oil
1 c chicken broth
3 Tbsp. Worcestershire sauce
1 Tbsp. garlic powder
1 Tbsp. onion powder
1 tsp. ground thyme
1 Tbsp. Black Pepper
1 Tbsp. White Pepper
1 Tbsp. Tabasco
1 Tbsp. Cayenne
Instructions: Combine all ingredients in a saucepan and simmer for a few minutes while stirring. You want to make sure the mixture is well mixed and that any herb is small enough to fit through your needle.

Notes:
Yield 2 cups
Veloute is a white sauce that’s made just like a Béchamel, except it’s with chicken, veal, or fish stock instead of milk.
Use Veloute as the basis for a piquant white wine sauce, add in tarragon, shallots, and chervil for Venetian Sauce, or make Sauce Albufera by adding in a little meat glaze (reduced brown sauce).
Ingredients:
3 tbsp. butter
3 tbsp. flour
2 c chicken stock
Salt
Freshly ground white pepper
Instructions: In a saucepan, over medium heat, melt the butter. Stir in the flour and cook for 2 minutes. Whisk in the stock, ½ cup at a time. Whisk until smooth. Season with salt and pepper. Bring the liquid to a boil and reduce the heat to low and cook for 15 minutes. Remove the heat and serve.

Recipe Name: Veloute Sauce for Pompano En Papillote
Ingredients:
2 tbsp. butter
2 tbsp. flour
Salt and ground white pepper
1 ½ cups warm fish or chicken stock
Instructions: Melt the butter and stir in the flour. Stir and cook until the mixture becomes foamy. Add the warm stock and bring to a boil. Turn fire down to a simmer and add salt and pepper to taste. Remove from the first and dot top of sauce with a few pieces of butter to prevent a film from forming.

Recipe Name: Vermouth, Leek, and Tarragon Pan Sauce
Ingredients:
2 tsp. vegetable oil
1 medium leek, white part only, halved lengthwise, sliced into 1/4-inch-thick pieces, washed, and dried (about 1 c)
1 tsp. all-purpose flour
3/4 c low-sodium chicken broth
1/2 c dry vermouth or white wine
1 tsp. whole-grain mustard
2 tsp. chopped fresh tarragon leaves
1 Tbsp. cold unsalted butter
Table salt and ground black pepper
Instructions: Add oil to empty skillet used to cook chicken and return pan to medium heat. Add leek and cook, stirring often, until softened and browned around edges, 2 to 3 minutes. Add flour and cook, stirring constantly, 30 seconds. Add broth and vermouth, increase heat to medium-high, and bring to simmer, scraping pan bottom to loosen browned bits. Simmer rapidly until reduced to 3/4 c, 3 to 5 minutes. Stir in any accumulated chicken juices; return to simmer and cook 30 sec­onds. Off heat, whisk in mustard, tarragon, and butter; season with salt and pepper. Spoon over cutlet and serve immediately.

Recipe Name: White Sauce (Bechamel), Cheese, Curry, Herb-Garlic, Lemon-Chive, Mexicali, Sherry
Ingredients:
1 Tbsp. butter
1 Tbsp. all-purpose flour
1/8 tsp. salt
Dash pepper
¾ c milk
Instructions:
In a small saucepan melt margarine or butter. Stir in flour, salt, and pepper. Add milk all at once. Cook and stir over medium heat till thickened and bubbly. Cook and stir 1 minute more.
Cheese Sauce– Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above, except omit salt. Over low heat, stir ¾ c shredded cheese of choice into cooked sauce till melted. Serve with vegetables.
Curry Sauce – Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above, except cook ½ to 1 tsp. curry powder in the melted butter for 1 minute. If desired, stir in 1 Tbsp. chopped chutney into the cooked sauce. Serve with fish or poultry.
Herb-Garlic Sauce – Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above, except cook 1 clove garlic, minced, in the melted butter. Stir in ¼ tsp. caraway seed or celery seed, or dried basil, oregano, or sage, crushed, with the flour. Serve with vegetables or poultry.
Lemon-Chive Sauce – Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above, except stir in 1 Tbsp. snipped chives and ½ tsp. finely shredded lemon peel with the flour. Serve with vegetables, poultry, or fish.
Mexicali Sauce – Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above except cook ½ tsp. chili powder in melted butter for 30 seconds. Stir 2 Tbsp. diced green chili peppers into cooked sauce. Serve with beef or pork.
Sherry Sauce  – Better Homes & Gardens
Prepare as above, except stir 1 to 2 Tbsp. dry sherry or dry white wine into cooked sauce. Serve with veal.

Ingredients:
1 cup mayonnaise
2 to 3 green onions, finely chopped
1 rib celery, finely chopped
2 tbsp prepared horseradish, drained
1 tbsp finely chopped chives
1 tbsp Dijon mustard
1 tbsp fresh lemon juice
1 clove garlic, finely chopped
1/2 tsp Tabasco brand Pepper Sauce
Instructions: Combine all ingredients in medium bowl. Cover and refrigerate 1 hour to blend flavors. Serve chilled.

Ingredients:
1cup uncooked long-grain rice
10 tbsp. butter, divided
1 lb. frozen cooked peeled crawfish tails, thawed and drained
1 medium onion, chopped
1 green bell pepper, chopped
3 celery ribs, chopped
4 garlic cloves, minced
6 tbsp. all-purpose flour
2 ¾ c chicken broth
¼ cup chopped fresh parsley
1 tbsp. salt-free Cajun seasoning
¼ tsp. salt
¼ tsp. cayenne
Instructions:
Prepare rice according to package directions. Melt 4 tbsp. butter in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat; add crawfish, and cook 5 minutes or until thoroughly heated. Remove crawfish, and keep warm.
Add onion, bell pepper, and celery to Dutch oven. Cook over medium-high heat 8 minutes or until tender. Add garlic, and cook 1 minute. Remove vegetables, and keep warm.
Melt remaining 6 tbsp. butter in Dutch oven over medium heat. Add flour, and cook, stirring constantly, 20 minutes or until caramel colored. Reduce heat to low, and gradually stir in chicken broth and next 5 ingredients. Cook over medium heat 10 minutes or until slightly thickened. Stir in vegetables and crawfish; cook 5 minutes. Serve with rice.

Ingredients:
4 Catfish fillets
1/3 c butter, melted
2 tbsp. lemon juice
2 tbsp. dried thyme leaves
2 tsp. salt
2 tsp. cracked black pepper
2 tsp. paprika
1 tsp. garlic powder
½ tsp. cayenne
1/3c dry white wine
Instructions: Heat large cast-iron skillet over high heat for 10 minutes. Rinse fillets and pat dry. Mix melted butter and lemon juice in small bowl. Mix dry seasonings in a second bowl. Brush both sides of fillets with lemon butter and sprinkle each side with spice mixture. Place 2 fillets, rounded side down, in hot skillet. Cook over high heat for 2 minutes. Turn fillets and cook 2 to 3 minutes, or until fish flakes easily when tested with a fork. Repeat procedure with remaining fillets while keeping cooked fillets warm. Remove skillet from heat and add wine any remaining lemon butter. Stir well and spoon over catfish.

Ingredients:
4 catfish fillets
2 eggs, beaten
1/2 c milk
1 c fresh, soft breadcrumbs
1 c grated Parmesan cheese
2 tsp. chopped garlic
1/4 c chopped parsley
2 tsp. dried oregano
1 tsp. salt
1 tsp. ground black pepper
Nonstick cooking spray
Instructions: Preheat oven to 400. Rinse fillets and pat dry. Combine eggs and milk in shallow bowl. Combine remaining ingredients in another shallow bowl. Dip fillets in egg mixture and then into breadcrumb mixture. Coat fillets evenly and pat firmly. Arrange fillets, rounded side up on greased baking sheet. Spray fillets lightly with nonstick cooking spray. Bake uncovered for 15 minutes or until fish flakes easily when tested with a fork.

Ingredients:
4 catfish fillets
Cooking oil
1 ½ c yellow cornmeal
½ c all-purpose flour
2 tsp. salt
1 tsp. ground red pepper
½ tsp. ground black pepper
¼ tsp. garlic powder
Instructions: Rinse fillets. Do not pat dry. Preheat cooking oil to 350 degrees in heavy skillet or deep fryer. Combine all dry ingredients in shallow dish. Place fillets into cornmeal mixture. Coat fillets evenly and shake off excess coating. Fry fillets in hot oil, rounded side down, for 3 to 4 minutes. Turn fillets and cook 3 to 4 minutes. Drain on paper towels.

Recipe Name: Fish Tacos with Chipotle Cream Sauce
Ingredients:
For the fish
2 Tbsp. olive oil
2 Tbsp. freshly squeezed lime juice
¼ tsp. salt
Freshly ground black pepper to taste
1 pound white, flaky fish fillets, like tilapia, mahi mahi or halibut
For the chipotle cream
½ c plain nonfat yogurt or 1/3 cup Greek-style nonfat yogurt
2 Tbsp. mayonnaise
2 tsp. finely chopped canned chipotle chile in adobo sauce
For the tacos
Eight 6-inch corn tortillas
1 ½ c shredded green cabbage or lettuce
½ c cooked corn kernels
¼ c fresh cilantro leaves
Lime wedges
Instructions:
To make the fish, in a small bowl, whisk together the oil, lime juice, salt and pepper. Pour over the fish and let marinate at room temperature for 20 minutes. To make the chipotle cream, if using regular yogurt, put it into a strainer lined with paper towel set over a bowl to drain and thicken for 30 minutes. Preheat a grill or nonstick grill pan over medium-high heat.
Remove the fish from the marinade and grill until cooked through, about 3 minutes per side. Set the fish aside on a plate and tent with aluminum foil to keep warm.
In a small bowl combine the drained or Greek-style yogurt, the mayonnaise, and chipotle.
To prepare the tacos, heat the tortillas on the grill or grill pan for 30 seconds on each side.
Flake the fish with a fork. Spread each tortilla with 1 tbsp of the chipotle cream. Top with the fish, cabbage, corn, and cilantro and serve with lime wedges.
Related Recipes: Refried Beans to go with Fish Tacos

Recipe Name: Fish Three Quick Recipes - Ginger-Glazed, Spice Crusted, Citrus Herb
Ingredients:
Ginger-Glazed Fish
1 tbsp. hoisin or soy sauce
1 tbsp. chopped scallion
1 tsp. sesame oil
1 tsp. grated ginger
½ tsp. brown sugar
Spice Crusted Fish
2 tbsp. softened butter
1 clove garlic (crushed in a press)
½ tsp. paprika
¼ tsp. ground cumin
¼ tsp. lemon peel
¼ tsp. salt
Citrus Herb Fish
2 tbsp. orange juice
1 tbsp. olive oil
2 tsp. chopped fresh thyme
1 tsp. grated orange peel
¼ tsp. salt
¼ tsp. pepper
Instructions:
Place four fish fillets or steaks (each 5 to 6oz) on a baking sheet. Spread or drizzle with one of the above mixtures, and roast or broil.
Fish - perfectly cooked
Roast at 450, broil, sauté, grill, or steam 10 minutes per inch of thickness. Cook to 145 degrees.

Ingredients:
1 scallion, minced
2 Tbsp. reduced-sodium soy sauce
1 Tbsp. rice vinegar
1 Tbsp. honey
1 tsp. minced fresh ginger
1 pound center-cut salmon fillet, skinned and cut into 4 portions
Instructions:
Whisk scallion, soy sauce, vinegar, honey and ginger in a medium bowl until the honey is dissolved.
Place salmon in a sealable plastic bag, add 3 Tbsp. of the sauce and refrigerate; let marinate for 15 minutes. Reserve the remaining sauce.
Preheat broiler.
Line a small baking pan with foil and coat with cooking spray. Transfer the salmon to the pan, skinned-side down. (Discard the marinade.)
Broil the salmon 4 to 6 inches from the heat source until cooked through, 6 to 10 minutes. Drizzle with the reserved sauce.

Recipe Name: Pan-Seared Cod in White Wine Tomato Basil Sauce
Ingredients:
For the White Wine Tomato Basil Sauce:
2 tbsp olive oil
1/2 tsp crushed red pepper flakes
2 large cloves garlic, finely minced
1 pint cherry tomatoes, sliced in half
1/4 c dry white wine
1/2 c fresh basil, finely chopped
2 tbsp fresh lemon juice
1/2 tsp fresh lemon zest
1/2 tsp salt (more to taste)
1/4 tsp fresh ground black pepper (more to taste)
For the Cod:
2 tbsp olive oil
1 1/2 lbs fresh cod, cut into 4 fillets
Salt and pepper
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 375 degrees, if finishing the Cod in the oven.
Make the Sauce: Heat oil in a large saute pan over medium heat. Add crushed red pepper flakes and garlic and saute for 1 minute, or until garlic is fragrant. Add the cherry tomatoes and cook, stirring occasionally, until soft and blistered, but still holding shape, 9 - 12 minutes. Add in the white wine, stir and allow the mixture to come to a gentle simmer. Stir in the basil, lemon juice, lemon zest, salt, and pepper and cook for 2 minutes. Transfer the sauce to a bowl, set aside until needed.
For the Cod: Heat oil in a large saute pan over medium heat. Season both sides of cod with salt and pepper. Cook cod in the oil until golden brown on the bottom, about 5 minutes. Carefully flip the cod over and place the pan in the oven to continue cooking for 5 minutes or cook on stove top until cooked through.
Place the Cod in a serving dish and pour the sauce over top.

Recipe Name: Pan-Seared Tuna with Ginger-Shiitake Cream Sauce
Ingredients:
6 6-ounce tuna steaks, each about 1 inch thick
2 Tbsp. peanut oil
3 Tbsp. butter
1/3 c thinly sliced green onions
¼ c chopped cilantro
2 Tbsp. finely chopped peeled fresh ginger
4 garlic cloves, chopped
8 ounces fresh shiitake mushrooms, stemmed, caps sliced
6 Tbsp. soy sauce
1 ½ c whipping cream
3 Tbsp. fresh lime juice
Lime wedges
Fresh cilantro sprigs
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 200. Sprinkle 1 side of tuna steaks with pepper. Heat 2 Tbsp. oil in heavy large skillet over high heat. Place tuna steaks, pepper side down, in hot oil and sear 2 minutes. Turn tuna over and continue cooking to desired doneness, about 2 minutes for rare. Transfer tuna to rimmed baking sheet; keep warm in oven.
Add butter, sliced green onions, cilantro, ginger and chopped garlic to same skillet and sauté until fragrant, about 30 seconds. Mix in mushrooms and soy sauce and simmer 30 seconds. Add whipping cream and simmer until sauce lightly coats back of spoon, about 3 minutes. Stir in lime juice. Spoon sauce onto plates; arrange tuna atop sauce. Garnish with lime wedges and cilantro sprigs, if desired.

Ingredients:
3 c low-sodium chicken broth
1/2 medium red onion, very thinly sliced
2 c cherry tomatoes, cut in half lengthwise, divided
1/2 lb small potatoes, preferably fingerling and purple, sliced into 1/4-inch rounds
3 sprigs basil, plus leaves for garnish
1/4 tsp red-pepper flakes
coarse salt and freshly ground pepper
4 skinless cod fillets (4 oz each)
4 oz snap peas, trimmed and thinly sliced on the bias
1 tsp fresh lemon juice, plus 4 lemon wedges, for serving
Extra-virgin olive oil for drizzling
Instructions:
In a large saute pan with a tight-fitting lid, bring broth, onion, 1 1/2 cups tomatoes, potatoes, basil sprigs, red-pepper flakes, and 2 tsps salt to a boil over high heat. Reduce heat and simmer, uncovered, until potatoes are crisp-tender, 6 to 8 minutes. Season cod with salt and pepper, add to broth mixture, and cover, simmering about 7 minutes more.
Remove and discard basil sprigs. Add peas, remaining 1/2 c tomatoes, and lemon juice to skillet, gently stirring to combine; cook just until warmed through. Divide fish, vegetables, and broth among 4 bowls. Garnish with basil leaves, drizzle with olive oil, and serve with lemon wedges.

Ingredients:
3 tbsp. butter
1 cup chopped green onions
1 cup raw peeled shrimp
1 ½ cups white wine
2 cups Fish Veloute Sauce
1 cup lump crabmeat
Salt, ground white pepper and cayenne
6 skinned pompano fillets, 6 oz. each
1 onion, sliced
5 whole black peppercorns
2 bay leaves
Juice from 1 lemon
Water
Parchment paper
Instructions: Melt the butter and sauté the green onions until they become limp. Add the raw shrimp, 1 cup of the wine and bring to a boil. Blend in the Veloute Sauce and the crabmeat. Season to taste with salt, pepper and cayenne. Simmer gently for 10 minutes, then let cool. Poach the pompano fillets in a shallow pan with the sliced onion, 2 tsp. salt, the whole black peppercorns, the remaining ½ cup wine, the bay leaves, the lemon juice and enough water to cover. Remove the fillets from the poaching liquid and keep warm. From the white parchment paper, cut 6 heart-shaped pieces about 10 inches high and 14 inches wide. Spoon some of the sauce onto the center of one-half of the heart-shaped paper and top with a pompano fillet. Fold the other half of the paper over the top and seal the edges by folding together. Place these on an oiled baking pan, and into a 400 degree oven for 15 minutes or until the paper begins to brown. Bring to the table and cut the top of the paper open.

Notes: In a traditional Vercruzan household, you would find papas cambray (roasted tiny white potatoes) and a bowl of savory white rice served alongside.
Ingredients:
1 28-ounce can diced tomatoes in juice, well drained, juices reserved
¼ c finely chopped white onion
3 large garlic cloves, chopped
3 small bay leaves
2 Tbsp. chopped fresh parsley
1 tsp. dried Mexican oregano
¼ c chopped pitted green olives
2 Tbsp. raisins
2 Tbsp. drained capers
6 4-5 ounce red snapper fillets
3 pickled jalapeno chilies, halved lengthwise
Instructions:
Place drained tomatoes in medium bowl. Using potato masher, crush tomatoes to coarse puree. Drain again, reserving juices.
Heat oil in heavy large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and stir 30 seconds. Add garlic and stir 30 seconds. Add tomato puree and cook 1 minute. Add bay leaves, parsley, oregano and ¼ c reserved tomato juices. Simmer until sauce thickens, about 3 minutes. Add olives, raisins, capers, and all remaining reserved tomato juices. Simmer until sauce thickens again, stirring occasionally, about 8 minutes. Season sauce to taste with salt and pepper. (Can be made 1 day ahead. Cover and refrigerate)
Preheat oven to 425. Spread 3 Tbsp. sauce in bottom of 15x10x2-inch glass baking dish. Arrange fish atop sauce. Sprinkle fish lightly with salt and pepper. Spoon remaining sauce over. Bake uncovered until fish is just opaque in center, about 18 minutes. Using long spatula, transfer fish with sauce to plates. Garnish with pickled jalapeno halves.

Recipe Name: Salmon with Honey Mustard Pan Sauce
Ingredients:
2 Tbsp. olive oil
2 Tbsp. unsalted butter
2 Tbsp. Whole Grain Dijon Mustard
A few grinds black pepper
1 pound Salmon, frozen or defrosted
1 Tbsp. honey
1/8 c water
Instructions: Add olive oil and butter to 12” skillet and cook over medium heat to melt butter. Add mustard, stir to combine and cook for 1-2 minutes to toast mustard seeds. Add fish to pan, sprinkle with pepper and sauté until lightly browned, about 3 minutes per side for defrosted or 6 minutes per side for frozen. Turn fish, mix in honey and cover with a lid to complete the cooking. Fish should be opaque but do not overcook. Remove fish, add water to deglaze pan and heat over high heat reduce the sauce by about half. Pour over fish and serve.

Ingredients:
1 egg, beaten
¼ c milk
¼ c chopped green onion
½ tsp. Creole seasoning
2 cans tuna, drained, or equivalent of other fish
¼ c fine dry bread crumbs
1 Tbsp. cooking oil
Instructions: Combine all ingredients in a bowl and drop by Tbsp. full into pan heated with oil. Cook about 3 minutes per side over medium heat. Serve with Rose Marie’s Tartar Sauce.

Ingredients:
4 fresh tuna steaks, 8 ounces each, 1 inch thick
1 lemon, zested
3 sprigs fresh rosemary, about 2 T leaves stripped from stem
Handful flat leaf parsley
3 cloves garlic, crushed
Coarse salt and black pepper or grill seasoning
Extra-virgin olive oil, for cooking
Rinse and pat tuna steaks dry.
Instructions:
Place zest on top of cutting board. Pile rosemary and parsley leaves on top of zest. Pile garlic and some coarse salt and black pepper or grill seasoning on top of herbs. Finely chop the garlic, herbs, and spices.
Drizzle the olive oil over the tuna steaks just enough to coat each side. Rub herb and garlic mixture into fish, coating pieces evenly on each side. Let stand 10 minutes.
Grill tuna steaks 6 minutes on each side or 4 minutes on each side, if you prefer pink tuna at the center. Cook steaks over high setting on indoor electric grill, medium high heat on outdoor gas grill or 6 inches from hot, prepared charcoal.

Ingredients:
3 dozen oysters in their water
3 tbsp. butter
3 tbsp. flour
½ cup dry white wine
¾ cup chopped green onions
1 tbsp. chopped parsley
Salt and ground white pepper
1 cup lump crabmeat
3 tbsp. each grated Swiss, Romano and Mozzarella cheeses
¼ c breadcrumbs
Instructions: Poach the oysters in their own water. Strain and set aside, retaining the liquor. Melt the butter in a saucepan and blend in the flour. Cook for two minutes or until the mixture becomes foamy. Add the white wine and green onions. Bring to a boil and add the oyster liquor. Add the chopped parsley, season to taste with salt and pepper and simmer gently for 15 minutes. Carefully mix in the oysters and crabmeat. Adjust the seasoning, if necessary, and keep warm. To serve, spoon the mixture into individual ovenproof dishes or ramekins. Mix the grated cheeses and breadcrumbs together and sprinkle over the top. Place in a preheated 400 degree oven until the cheese is melted and the top begins to brown. Serves 6

Ingredients:
1 1/2-pt. Fresh shucked oysters (about 2 1/2 ounces medium-sized oysters), drained
5 to 6 slices lean bacon
1/2 c finely minced French shallots
1/2 c freshly grated sharp Cheddar cheese
1/4 c dry white wine
1/2 tsp. freshly ground black pepper
1/2 tsp. salt
2 Tbsp. salted butter, softened
1/2 c Pickapeppa Sauce
Instructions:
Drain the oysters in a colander and dry them thoroughly between several layers of paper towels.
Place them, widely spaced, in the bottom of a heavy shallow baking or gratin dish about 10 inches long and 5 inches wide.
Cut the bacon into 1-inch squares and place a square on top of each oyster. Distribute the minced shallots evenly between the oysters on the bottom of the baking dish, and then cover the whole surface with the grated sharp Cheddar cheese. Sprinkle evenly with the white wine, pepper, and salt, then cut the softened butter into about 10 small pieces and distribute evenly over the dish. Spoon on the Pickapeppa Sauce in widening concentric circles from the center.
Preheat the broiler. Place the baking dish 3 1/2 inches below the heat and broil for about 8 to 10 minutes, or until the sauce begins to glaze and turn dark brown in color.
Remove the dish from the oven and carefully spoon the portions onto heated plates so that some of the browned cheese crust covers each one. Also, spoon any sauce remaining in the baking dish around the individual portions.

Ingredients:
4 strips bacon, *approximately - used 1/2 slice of bacon for each scallop.
2 tbsp butter
1 lb scallops
1 tsp sea salt
1 tsp pepper, fresh cracked
1 tsp garlic powder
3 tbsp brown sugar
1 tsp ginger
Instructions: Pat scallops dry with a paper towel. In a large pan, start to crisp bacon. Cook bacon until golden brown - but still soft and pliable. In a small bowl, mix salt, pepper, and garlic and then sprinkle generously over scallops. Wrap each scallop with 1/2 bacon strip and secure by pushing a toothpick through the bacon and scallop. In another small bowl, mix brown sugar and ginger. Sprinkle brown sugar and ginger mixture all over scallop, but reserve much of the sugar and ginger to sprinkle after the scallops flip once. Heat butter in large pan until foamy and melted. Add scallops to pan and cook on medium high heat. Flip after 5-6 minutes, sprinkle with more sugar and ginger, and continue repeating steps until scallops are cooked. Serve immediately.

Ingredients:
2 pounds peeled raw shrimp
1 stick butter, melted
2 Tbsp. Creole seasoning
Instructions: Mix seasoning in melted butter. Put peeled shrimp and margarine mixture into plastic bag. Let shrimp marinate in refrigerator several hours (butter will solidify again). Do not pre-heat grill! Pour shrimp and marinade into a grill pan with holes. Grill shrimp until done, stirring frequently. Watch closely as the butter causes the grill to flame up easily.

Recipe Name: Cheese Grits for Shrimp and Grits
Ingredients:
1 (14-ounce) can low-sodium fat-free chicken broth
1 c fat-free milk
½ tsp. salt
1 c uncooked quick-cooking grits
¼ c (3 ounces) shredded 2 % reduced-fat sharp Cheddar cheese
¼ c freshly grated Parmesan cheese
½ tsp. hot sauce
¼ tsp. ground white pepper
Instructions: Bring first 3 ingredients and 1⅓c water to a boil in a medium saucepan over medium-high heat; gradually whisk in grits. Reduce heat to low, and simmer, stirring occasionally, 10 minutes or until thickened. Stir in Cheddar cheese and next 3 ingredients.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp salted butter
6 cloves garlic, finely diced
1 lb shrimp, tails on or off
1 small yellow onion, diced
1/2 c white wine
5 oz jarred sun dried tomato strips in oil, drained (reserve 1 tsp oil)
1 3/4 c half & half
salt and pepper
3 c baby spinach leaves
2/3 c fresh grated Parmesan cheese
1 tsp cornstarch mixed with 1 tbsp water
2 tsp dried Italian herbs
1 tbsp fresh parsley, chopped
Instructions:
Heat a large skillet over medium-high heat. Melt the butter and add in the garlic and cook until fragrant, about 1 minute. Add in the shrimp and cook two minutes on each side, until just cooked through and pink. Transfer to a bowl with a slotted spoon.
Cook the onion in the butter remaining in the skillet. Pour in the white wine and reduce by half, scraping up the fond in the skillet. Add the sun dried tomatoes with the 1 tsp reserved oil and cook for a couple of minutes. Reduce heat to medium-low, add the half & half and bring to a gentle simmer, stirring occasionally. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Add in the spinach leaves and allow to wilt, then add in the Parmesan cheese. Once the cheese is melted, you can stir in the cornstarch/water mixture to get the right consistency in the sauce. Add the shrimp back, sprinkle with herbs and parsley and serve.

Ingredients:
2 bacon slices
1 pound unpeeled, medium-size raw shrimp
1/8 tsp. salt
¼ tsp. pepper
¼ c all-purpose flour
1 c sliced fresh mushrooms
2 tsp. canola oil
½ c chopped green onions
2 garlic cloves, minced
1 c low-sodium, fat-free chicken broth
2 Tbsp. fresh lemon juice
¼ tsp. hot sauce
Cheese Grits*
Instructions:
Cook bacon in a large nonstick skillet over medium heat 10 minutes or until crisp; remove bacon, and drain on paper towels, reserving 1 tsp. drippings in skillet. Crumble bacon.
Peel shrimp, devein, if desired. Sprinkle shrimp with salt and pepper; dredge in flour.
Sauté mushrooms in hot drippings with oil in skillet 5 minutes or until tender. Add green onions and sauté 2 minutes. Add shrimp and garlic and sauté 2 minutes or until shrimp are lightly browned. Stir in chicken broth, lemon juice, and hot sauce, and cook 2 more minutes, stirring to loosen particles from bottom of skillet. Spoon shrimp mixture over hot Cheese Grits; sprinkle with crumbled bacon.

Ingredients:
3 to 4 Tbsp. salt
15 to 20 whole allspice
6 or 8 whole black peppercorns
Generous sprinkling of black pepper
3 or 4 dashes of cayenne or Tabasco
15 to 20 whole cloves
6 cloves garlic, sliced
3 small or 2 large onions, sliced
2 large bay leaves
Few bits of dried red pepper
1 Tbsp. Worcestershire
2 ½ to 3 pounds shrimp
Instructions: Season 2 to 2 ½ quarts water with a heaping T of salt to each quart of water. Add spices to the water and bring to a boil and let simmer about 30 minutes before adding shrimp.

Ingredients:
¼ c butter
1 c coarsely chopped onions
1 c diced celery
1 small clove garlic, finely minced
2 Tbsp. flour
1 tsp. salt
1 tsp. sugar
Dash cayenne
1 tsp. paprika
½ small bay leaf
4 drops Tabasco sauce
½ c diced green pepper
1 large can tomatoes
2 c cooked, cleaned shrimp
Instructions: Melt butter in fry pan. Add onion, celery and garlic; cook slowly until tender but not brown. Add flour and seasonings; stir until blended. Stir in green pepper and tomatoes. Cook 10 minutes over low heat, stirring occasionally. Add shrimp and heat.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp oil
1 c chopped onions
1 c chopped celery
1 green bell pepper, chopped
2 cloves garlic, minced
2 c chopped peeled tomatoes
1 lb fresh or frozen and thawed uncooked shrimp, peeled, deveined
1 can (8 oz) tomato sauce
1/2 c Marsala
1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper
1/2 to 1/2 tsp hot pepper sauce
4 c hot cooked rice or 1 (10 oz) package of egg noodles, cooked, drained
Instructions: Heat oil in large saucepan over medium-high heat. Add onions, celery, bell pepper and garlic; cook 2 to 3 minutes. Add tomatoes,; cook 2 to 3 minutes, stirring occasionally. Add remaining ingredients except rice; cook 2 to 3 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Serve over hot cooked rice.

Ingredients:
4 large mirlitons
1 pound unpeeled, medium shrimp
1/3 c minced onion
1/4 c chopped fresh parsley
4 green onions, chopped
3 garlic cloves, minced
2 Tbsp. bacon drippings
8 ounces cooked ham, coarsely chopped
1 1/4 c soft breadcrumbs, divided
1 Tbsp. Creole seasoning
1 large egg, lightly beaten
2 Tbsp. butter, melted
Instructions:
Bring mirlitons and water to cover to a boil in a Dutch oven; boil 25 to 30 minutes or until tender. Peel shrimp, and devein, if desired. Set shrimp aside. Drain and cool mirlitons. Cut in half lengthwise; remove seeds, and discard. Peel skin from mirlitons using a paring knife and discard with seeds. Chop mirliton pulp.
Sauté onion and next 3 ingredients in hot bacon drippings 3 minutes. Add shrimp and ham; cook, stirring constantly, 3 to 5 minutes or until shrimp turn pink. Stir in mirliton pulp, 3/4-c breadcrumbs, and Creole seasoning; cook, stirring occasionally, 5 minutes. Let cool slightly; stir in egg. Pour into greased casserole.
Stir together remaining 1/2-c breadcrumbs and butter. Sprinkle evenly over mirliton casserole. Bake at 375° for 15 to 20 minutes or until lightly browned.

Ingredients:
1 1/2 tbsp olive oil
1 lb shrimp, peeled and deveined
salt to taste
6 cloves garlic, finely minced
1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
3 tbsp lemon juice
1 tbsp caper brine
1 1/2 tsp cold butter
1/3 c chopped parsley, divided
1 1/2 tbsp cold butter
water, as needed
Instructions:
Heat olive oil in heavy skillet over high heat. Place shrimp in an even layer on the bottom and cook for 1 minute without stirring. Season shrimp with salt; cook and stir until shrimp begin to turn pink, about 1 minute. Stir in garlic and red pepper flakes, stir 1 minute. Stir in lemon juice, caper brine, 1 1/2 tsp cold butter and half the parsley. Cook until butter melts, about 1 minute. Turn heat to low and stir in 1 1/2 tbsp cold butter. Cook and stir to form thick sauce and shrimp are pink and opaque, about 2 to 3 minutes. Remove shrimp with a slotted spoon to a bowl and continue to cook butter sauce, adding water 2 tsp at a time, if too thick, about 2 minutes. Season to taste with salt.
Serve shrimp topped with pan sauce and garnish with remaining flat-leaf parsley.

Ingredients:
2 egg yolks
2 c ice water
1 3/4 c flour
1/4 c corn starch
Instructions:
In medium bowl beat egg yolks and water. With wire whisk, stir in flour and cornstarch. Do not over beat, batter should be lumpy. Using a pie plate with plain flour, coat item to be fried light, dip into batter and back into flour and place on a cookie sheet to rest.
Usually served with Rose Marie's Tartar Sauce and/or Comeback Sauce.

Ingredients:
1 (15-ounce) can black beans, rinsed and drained
1 egg
½ yellow onion, chopped
1 c whole wheat bread crumbs
1 tsp. dried oregano
1 tsp. dried basil
½ tsp. garlic powder or granules
Salt and pepper to taste
Hot sauce to taste
1 to 2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive or canola oil
6 whole what hamburger buns
6 green leaf lettuce leaves
2 tomatoes, sliced
½ small red onion, thinly sliced
Instructions:
Put beans in a large bowl and mash well with a fork. Add egg, yellow onion, bread crumbs, oregano, basil, garlic powder, salt, pepper and hot sauce. Mix well to combine then shape into 6 patties.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Arrange patties in a single layer (working in batches, if needed) and cook, flipping once, until golden brown on both sides and cooked through, about 10 minutes total. Transfer to buns, top with lettuce, tomatoes and red onions and serve.

Ingredients:
1 tsp. olive oil
1 onion, chopped
2 cloves garlic, minced
¾ c uncooked white rice
1 ½ c vegetable broth
1 tsp. ground cumin
¼ tsp. cayenne pepper
3 ½ c canned black beans, drained
Instructions:
In a stockpot over medium-high heat, heat the oil. Add the onion and garlic and sauté for 4 minutes. Add the rice and sauté for 2 minutes.
Add the vegetable broth, bring to a boil, cover and lower the heat and cook for 20 minutes. Add the spices and black beans.

Ingredients:
1/2 pound bacon, chopped
1 large onion, chopped
1 tbsp dried Mexican oregano
1 garlic clove, chopped fine
2 c dried pinto beans, picked over, soaked in cold water at least 4 hours and up to 12, drained
1 1/2 quarts water plus additional if necessary
1/2 c sliced pickled jalapenos
a 12 oz bottle dark Mexican beer such as Negra Model or Dos Equis
2 tsp salt plus 1/2 tsp if necessary
Instructions: Preheat oven to 300. In a 6 to 7 quart ovenproof kettle cook bacon, onion, oregano, and garlic over moderately high heat, stirring and scraping up brown bits, until union is browned lightly. Add beans, water, jalapeno, and beer and bring to a boil. Bake mixture, covered, in middle of oven until beans are soft, 1 1/2 to 2 hours (add additional water if beans begin to out. Mixture should be soupy with beans very soft, but not falling apart.) Stir in 2 tsp salt and bake beans 10 minutes more. Check seasoning, adding remaining 1/2 tsp salt if necessary. (Beans may be made 2 days ahead, cooled, uncovered, and chilled, covered.)

Ingredients:
1 can cream of mushroom soup
1/2 cup milk
4 cups cooked green beans
1 tsp soy sauce
dash pepper
1 1/3 cups French's fried onions
Instructions: Stir together the soup, milk, beans, soy sauce, pepper and 2/3 cups of the onions together in a 1 1/2 quart casserole dish. Bake at 350 degrees for 25 to 30 minutes until hot and bubbly. Stir mixture and then top with the rest of the onions. Bake for 5 minutes until onions are browned.

Ingredients:
1 bag frozen or some fresh tender green beans
bacon
onion
Better than Bouillon
onion powder
salt & pepper
Instructions: Cut up bacon and brown in skillet with onion slices. Add green beans, a teaspoon dipped in the Better than Bouillon chicken, I dip the spoon in and get the tip of the spoon piled up with goodie, stir that in and sprinkle generously with onion powder and salt, stir. I usually let folks add pepper at the table.

Ingredients:
1 lb dried pinto beans (I used 4 regular sized cans of pintos including the can liquid)
6 strips bacon, chopped
2 cloves garlic, chopped
4 medium fresh plum tomatoes, chopped
2 medium onions, chopped
1 medium green pepper, chopped
1 (12 oz) can beer
1/2 bunch fresh cilantro, chopped
2 jalapeno peppers, seeded and chopped
salt and pepper
Instructions: Rinse and pick over beans, if using dry. Bring the beans to a boil in a large pot of water and cook over medium heat for 1 1/2 to 2 hours until they are tender, but still firm. If using canned beans, bring to a simmer while preparing the bacon mixture. Fry the bacon in a large skillet until halfway cooked. Add garlic, tomatoes, onions and green pepper; continue cooking with bacon until the bacon is done. Add beer to bacon mixture and simmer a few additional minutes. Add chopped cilantro and jalapenos; combine everything in one large pot. Add salt and pepper to taste.

Ingredients:
1 (1 pound) bag Red Beans, Camellia brand preferred
8 cups chicken broth
1 teaspoon bacon grease
1 ham hock (optional)
1 teaspoon minced garlic
1 small onion, chopped
1 teaspoon thyme
1 teaspoon black pepper
1/2 teaspoon ground sage
16 oz smoked sausage
1 bay leaf
1 teaspoon salt
onion powder and garlic powder to taste
Cooked rice
Instructions:
Decide whether the beans are for dinner tonight or tomorrow night. If tomorrow night, soaks beans in water in a covered pot overnight. Drain beans, add chicken broth and skip next sentence. If tonight, put beans and chicken broth in pot and bring to boil and turn off for an hour and let rest. Bring beans and chicken broth to a simmer.
Decide how you want your sausage pieces in your red beans. I like smaller pieces and I like to brown the sausage in a separate skillet in bacon grease. Then soften the onion and garlic in that skillet as well. You can add everything to your pot of red beans and broth and not brown anything ahead. Good red beans can be made with just red beans, water, salt, pepper, garlic and onion powder. So don't let it stop you if you don't have all the other meat and herbs, or if you want them vegetarian.
Cook the pot of red beans until they are tender. This should take anywhere from 1 1/2 hours to 2 1/2 hours.
Rice is always 1 to 2 parts ratio. 1 cup of rice to 2 cups of water and 1 tsp salt and 1 tbsp butter. Bring all to a boil, cover, turn heat to just simmer and cook about 15 minutes or until all water is absorbed. Fluff rice with a fork.

Recipe Name: Refried Beans to go with Fish Tacos
Ingredients:
1 Tbsp. olive oil
1/2 medium onion, diced
2 cloves garlic, minced
1 t ancho chili powder or other chili powder
1 (15-ounce) can pinto beans, preferably low-sodium, drained and rinsed
2/3 c low-sodium chicken broth, plus more if needed
Salt and pepper
2 Tbsp. chopped fresh cilantro leaves
Instructions: Heat the oil in a large skillet over medium heat. Add the onion and cook until tender, about 3 minutes. Stir in the garlic and chili powder and cook for 2 minutes more. Stir in the beans and chicken broth and cook until the beans are warmed through, about 5 minutes. Mash the beans coarsely with the back of a wooden spoon, adding more chicken broth to moisten, if needed. Season with salt and pepper to taste. Stir in the cilantro.
Related Recipes: Fish Tacos with Chipotle Cream Sauce

Ingredients:
12 oz fresh or frozen shelled edamame, about 2 cups
1/2 cup fresh corn kernels, about 2 ears of corn
1/4 cup finely diced scallion
1 clove garlic, minced
1 tbsp olive
3/4 tsp kosher salt
1/4 tsp freshly ground black pepper
1 cup chopped fresh tomato
1/4 cup chopped fresh basil leaves
1 tbsp red wine vinegar
