Instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. Combine ingredients down to last three. in a 9x13 baking dish. Roast for 10 to 15 minutes or just until the edamame begins to brown. Remove from the oven and refrigerate until completely cool. Add the tomato, basil and vinegar to the edamame mixture and toss to combine. Taste and adjust seasoning. Serve chilled or at room temperature.

Recipe Name: Artichokes Steamed with Lemon Butter Sauce
Notes: The traditional way to eat steamed artichokes is to pull one artichoke leaf out at a time and scrape the tender meat from the bottom of the leaf with your teeth. Be sure to remove the thistle-like choke in the center of the artichoke before enjoying the meaty heart. These artichokes are delicious served chilled with Balsamic Vinaigrette, Dijon Vinaigrette or Tarragon Mayonnaise for dipping, too.
Ingredients:
1 lemon, halved
4 large globe artichokes
1 c (2 sticks) butter, melted
½ tsp. lemon zest
1 Tbsp. lemon juice
Salt and pepper, to taste
Instructions:
Arrange a large bowl of water next to you, Squeeze lemon into water then add squeezed lemon halves to water, too; stir well. Cut stems off of artichokes so that they will sit flat, then discard stems and transfer artichokes to bowl with lemon water to prevent artichokes from discoloring.
Working with one at a time, cut off the top third of each artichoke, then clip off and discard the sharp points and remaining bottom outer leaves, returning each artichoke to the lemon water as you finish.
Pour enough water into a large pot to reach a depth of 1 to 2 inches. Cover and bring to a boil. Uncover pot and arrange trimmed artichokes, bottoms up, in the bottom in a single layer. Cover pot, reduce heat to medium and stem until tender. 20 to 40 minutes. Cooking time will depend on size of artichokes. When the leaves pull out easily and the base can be pierced with a knife, they’re ready. Drain well and transfer to a large platter.
Meanwhile, whisk together butter, zest, juice, salt and pepper to make a lemon sauce; transfer to a bowl. Serve artichokes warm, with lemon butter sauce on the side for dipping.

Ingredients:
1 pound asparagus
1/4 pound Sugar snap peas
2 Tbsp. good olive oil
Kosher salt
Freshly ground black pepper
Red pepper flakes
Instructions: Cut off the tough ends of the asparagus and slice the stalks diagonally into 2-inch pieces. Snap off the stem ends of the snap peas and pull the string down the length of the vegetable. Warm the olive oil in a large sauté pan over a medium heat and add the asparagus and snap peas. Add the salt, pepper, and red pepper flakes, to taste, if desired. Cook for approximately 5 to 10 minutes until al dente, tossing occasionally.

Ingredients:
1 chicken bouillon cube
1 c water
1 strip bacon
2 pinches red pepper flakes
1 clove garlic, peeled and lightly smashed
1 16-ounce bag frozen baby limas
Instructions: Bring water and bouillon to oil. Add all ingredients. Cover pan and turn heat to simmer. Cook at least 25 minutes. Salt and pepper to taste.

Source: Rose Marie Luke from Piccadilly Cafeteria
Ingredients:
2 pounds peeled carrots, chopped up
2/3 c sugar
1 ½ tsp. baking powder
1 ½ tsp. vanilla
2 Tbsp. flour
3 eggs, beaten with an electric mixer
6 T butter at room temperature
Powdered sugar
Instructions: Steam or boil carrots. Drain well and transfer to a large mixing bowl. While carrots are still warm, add sugar, baking powder and vanilla. Beat with mixer until smooth. Add flour and beat well. Add beaten eggs and beat well. Add butter and beat well. Pour mixture into a 2 quart baking dish. Bake at 350 for 1 hour or until top is a light golden brown. Sprinkle lightly with powdered sugar over top before serving.

Ingredients:
½ pound smoked meat (ham hocks, smoked turkey wings, or smoked neck bones)
1 tsp. salt
1/2 tsp. black pepper
1/2 tsp. garlic powder
1 Tbsp. seasoned salt
1 Tbsp. hot sauce
1 large bunch collard greens
1 Tbsp. butter
Instructions:
In a large pot, bring 3 quarts of water to a boil and add smoked meat, salt, pepper, garlic powder, seasoned salt and hot sauce. Reduce heat to medium and cook for 1 hour.
Wash the collard greens thoroughly. Remove the stems that run down the center by holding the leaf in your left hand and stripping the leaf down with your right hand. The tender young leaves in the heart of the collards don’t need to be stripped. Stack 6 to 8 leaves on top of one another, roll up and slice into ½ to 1-inch thick slices. Please greens in pot with meat and add butter. Cook for 45 to 60 minutes, stirring occasionally. When done taste and adjust seasoning.

Ingredients:
1 large ham hock (not smoked)
1 medium onion, diced
1 garlic clove, minced
3 (16-ounce) packages frozen chopped collard greens, thawed and drained
Pinch of crushed red pepper flakes (or more, to taste)
Salt and pepper, to taste
Instructions:
Simmer the ham hock in 2 quarts of water until tender, about four hours. Remove hock from water and allow to cool. When cool enough to handle, remove the meat from the bone, roughly chop and set aside in the refrigerator.
Transfer the cooking liquid to a fat separator or other container and refrigerate until the fat solidifies on top of the liquid. Skim off the fat from the liquid and reserve both separately.
Heat a heavy-bottomed stock pot over medium heat. Add pork fat and allow to get hot. Add the onion and garlic, and sauté until translucent, 3-5 minutes.
Add the thawed collards and enough water to cover, saving the rest of the liquid in case more is needed to keep collars from drying out as they cook.
Reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 45 minutes or so. Stir in the chopped ham hock meat, crushed pepper flakes, and salt and pepper to taste. Continue to simmer until collards are very tender, about 30 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 can cream style corn
2 eggs, well beaten
1 c milk
1 Tbsp. sugar
3 Tbsp. flour
2 Tbsp. melted butter
Instructions: Mix flour and sugar. Add milk gradually and stir. Add rest of ingredients and place in greased casserole. Bake at 350 for 1 hour. Rose Marie now microwaves this dish.

Notes: Gumbo is the African word for okra, which was brought to Louisiana by slaves. Today no garden in Louisiana is planted without including this staple of Cajun-Creole cooking. Cooking the okra slightly in salted water helps tenderize the okra, be careful not to overcook or you will have gumbo.
Ingredients:
2 c water
1 tsp. salt
1 pound whole okra
Ice water
2 c Lou Ana peanut oil
2 eggs
½ c milk
½ c buttermilk
1 tsp. hot sauce
2 tsp. Cajun seasoning
3 c flour
Instructions:
Combine water and salt in a pot and bring to a boil. Add okra and cook for 2 minutes. Drain and chill in ice water to stop cooking process. Set aside
Heat peanut oil in frying pot to 350 degrees.
In a medium bowl, combine eggs, milk, buttermilk and hot sauce. Whip together.
In another bowl, blend together 1 tsp. of Cajun seasoning and flour.
Season okra with remaining tsp. of Cajun seasoning.
Batter okra by first dusting in flour, then the egg batter. Dip back into the flour.
Carefully drop okra in frying oil and cook until golden brown.
Drain okra on paper towels.

Ingredients:
Coarse salt and ground pepper
3 to 4 bunches (2 ½ pounds. total) flat-leaf spinach, trimmed and cleaned
2 Tbsp. butter
½ medium onion, minced
4 garlic cloves, minced
4 ounce bar cream cheese, cut into pieces
½ c milk
Pinch ground nutmeg
Instructions:
Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil. Add spinach, and cook just until wilted, about 1 minute. Drain in a colander; rinse with cold water until cool. Squeeze spinach to remove as much liquid as possible; coarsely chop, and set aside.
In a large saucepan, heat butter over medium. Add onion and garlic; season with salt and pepper. Cook, stirring occasionally, until onion softens, 3 to 5 minutes.
Add cream cheese and milk; cook, stirring, until cream cheese is melted and smooth. Stir in spinach. Simmer over medium heat until mixture thickens, 8 to 10 minutes. Add nutmeg; season with salt and pepper.

Ingredients:
1/4 cup cornstarch
1 large egg
1/2 tsp. coarse salt plus additional for sprinkling the eggplant
1 c fresh breadcrumbs
1 small eggplant (about 1/4 pound), cut crosswise into 1/4-inch-thick slices
Vegetable oil for deep-frying
Instructions:
Put the cornstarch, the egg beaten with 1/2 tsp. of the salt, and the breadcrumbs in separate small dishes. Dredge each eggplant slice in the cornstarch, coating it thoroughly and shaking off the excess, coat it with the egg mixture, and dredge it in the breadcrumbs, pressing on the crumbs to make them adhere.
Transfer the eggplant as it is coated to paper towels and let it dry slightly.
In a deep heavy skillet fry the eggplant slices in batches in 1/2-inch of 375 degree oil for 1 minute on each side, or until they are golden brown, and transfer them with tongs to paper towels to drain. Sprinkle the eggplant lightly with the additional salt and serve it with the lemon wedges.

Ingredients:
10 oz Frozen Spinach
3 tbsp Parmesan cheese
3 oz cream cheese
2 tbsp sour cream
1/4 tsp garlic powder
1/4 tsp onion powder
salt and pepper to taste
Instructions: Defrost spinach in the microwave. Add to pan on medium high heat and let excess water boil off. Add seasoning and cream cheese to the pan. Stir together until cream cheese has melted. Add sour cream and Parmesan and mix together well until the creamed spinach is thickened.

Ingredients:
1 eggplant (2 pounds), sliced ½ inch thick crosswise
1 Tbsp. olive oil
Coarse salt and ground pepper
1 c milk
3 Tbsp. all-purpose flour
2 garlic cloves, minced
1 c marinara sauce
½ c grated mozzarella
1/3 c grated Parmesan
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 450 degrees, with racks in upper and lower thirds. Arrange eggplant on two rimmed baking sheets. Brush eggplant on both sides with oil, and season with salt and pepper. Bake until golden brown and very tender, 20 to 25 minutes, turning slices and rotating sheets halfway through.
Meanwhile, make sauce: Off heat, in a medium saucepan, whisk together ¼ c milk, four and garlic. Gradually whisk in remaining ¾ c milk and ½ c marinara sauce. Bring to a boil; reduce to a simmer, and cook until pink sauce has thickened, 2 to 3 minutes.
Spread ¼ c marinara sauce in the bottom of a shallow 2-quart baking dish. Alternate layers of baked eggplant with pink sauce. Dollop with remaining ¼ c marinara sauce. Sprinkle with mozzarella and Parmesan. Bake on upper rack until browned and bubbling. 10 to 15 minutes.

Ingredients:
3 tbsp. oil
4 Chinese eggplants, halved lengthwise and cut into 1-inch half-moons
1 c water
1 tbsp. crushed red pepper flakes
3 tbsp. garlic powder
5 tsp. white sugar
1 tsp. cornstarch
2 tbsp. light soy sauce
2 tbsp. oyster sauce
Instructions: Heat the oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook and stir the eggplant until soft, about 4 minutes. Stir in the water, red pepper flakes, and garlic powder. Cover and simmer until all the water is absorbed. Meanwhile, mix sugar, cornstarch, soy sauce, and oyster sauce in a bowl until sugar and cornstarch have dissolved. Stir sauce into the eggplant, making sure to evenly coat the eggplant. Cook until the sauce has thickened.

Ingredients:
1 large egg, lightly beaten
½ c buttermilk
½ c all-purpose flour, divided
½ c cornmeal
1 tsp. salt
½ tsp. pepper
3 medium-size green tomatoes, cut into 1/3-inch slices
Vegetable oil
Salt to taste
Combine egg and buttermilk; set aside.
Instructions:
Combine ¼ c all-purpose flour, cornmeal, 1 tsp. salt, and pepper in a shallow bowl or pan.
Dredge tomato slices in remaining ¼ c flour; dip in egg mixture, and dredge in cornmeal mixture.
Pour oil to a depth of ¼ to ½ inch in a large cast-iron skillet; heat to 375 degrees. Drop tomatoes, in batches, into hot oil, and cook 2 minutes on each side or until golden. Drain on paper towels or a wire rack. Sprinkle hot tomatoes with salt to taste.

Ingredients:
1 ½ pounds baby spinach leaves
2 tbsp. good olive oil
2 tbsp. chopped garlic (6 cloves)
2 tsp. kosher salt
¾ tsp. freshly ground black pepper
1 tbsp. unsalted butter
Lemon
Sea or kosher salt, optional
Instructions:
Rinse the spinach well in cold water to make sure it’s very clean. Spin it dry in a salad spinner, leaving just a little water clinging to the leaves.
In a very large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil and sauté the garlic over medium heat for about 1 minute, but not until it’s browned. Add all the spinach, the salt, and pepper to the pot, toss with the garlic and oil, cover the pot, and cook it for 2 minutes. Uncover the pot, turn the heat on high, and cook the spinach for another minute, stirring with a wooden spoon, until all the spinach is wilted. Using a slotted spoon, lift the spinach to a serving bowl and top with the butter, a squeeze of lemon, and a sprinkling of sea or kosher salt. Serve hot.

Ingredients:
2 Tbsp. balsamic vinegar
1 large lemon, juiced
2 tsp. Worcestershire sauce
2 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
Coarse salt and black pepper, or grill seasoning spice blend
4 medium to large Portobello mushroom caps
1 Tbsp. extra-virgin olive oil
3 large, firm plum tomatoes, seeded and diced
2 sprigs fresh rosemary leaves finely chopped to about 2 Tbsp.
1/2 pound fresh smoked mozzarella, cubed
Instructions: Combine first 5 ingredients and mushrooms in a baggie. Marinate for at least 30 minutes. Prepare broiler pan by spraying with nonstick spray and broil mushroom cap side down for 4 minutes and then 4 or 5 minutes on the other side until tender. In the meantime, combine tomatoes, rosemary and mozzarella in a small bowl with 1 T olive oil and toss. When mushrooms are tender, fill caps with tomato mixture and broil until cheese is melty.

Ingredients:
1 pound turnips
1 pound rutabagas
1 pound carrots
1 pound parsnips
3 shallots, halved
½ c olive oil
2 tbsp chopped fresh rosemary
2 tsp kosher salt
1 tsp black pepper
8 garlic cloves
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Peel first 4 ingredients; cut into 1-inch pieces. (If your carrots are small enough, leave them whole.) Toss with shallots and next 4 ingredients. Place in a single layer in a 17- x 11-inch jelly-roll pan. Bake 30 minutes, stirring halfway through. Add garlic; bake 45 minutes or until tender, stirring at 15-minute intervals.
Note: You can prepare 4 hours ahead ; cool in pan 30 minutes or to room temperature; bake at 450 for 10 to 15 minutes or until hot.

Ingredients:
2 tbsp olive oil
2 medium garlic cloves, minced
1 tsp ginger, freshly grated
1/4 tsp red pepper flakes
1 1/2 lbs bok choy
1 tbsp soy sauce
1 tbsp water
1/4 tsp toasted sesame oil
salt
Instructions:
In a large frying pan with a tight fitting lid, heat the oil over medium-high heat until shimmering. Add the garlic, ginger and red pepper flakes and cook, stirring constantly, until fragrant but not brown, about 30 seconds. Add the bok choy and using tongs fold it into the garlic-ginger mixture until coated, about 1 minute. Add the soy sauce and water, cover, and cook until steam accumulates about 1 minute. Uncover and cook, stirring occasionally, until the greens are just wilted, the stalks are just fork tender but still crisp, and most of the water has evaporated, about 2 minutes. Turn off the heat, stir in the sesame oil, and season with salt if desired.
We like to buy bags of baby bok choy at H Mart for this dish.

Ingredients:
1/2 c butter
1 c Sherry
1 clove garlic, crushed
18 fresh mushrooms, sliced
Salt and black pepper
Instructions: Melt butter in medium skillet over medium heat. Add cooking wine and garlic. Add mushrooms; cook until tender, about 5 minutes, stirring frequently. Season to taste with salt and pepper.

Ingredients:
6 ounces cream cheese
4 ounces butter
10 ounces frozen chopped spinach (undrained)
2 c plain bread crumbs
Salt and pepper
Parmesan cheese
Instructions: Melt butter and cream cheese. Add chopped spinach (uncooked and undrained) and season with salt and pepper. Add bread crumbs until soggy and place in casserole dish. Top with Parmesan cheese. Bake at 350 for 15 minutes.

Ingredients:
1 pound ground beef or pork or lamb
1/2 c chopped onion
1 can (10 1/2 oz) beef broth
1/2 cup raw rice or bulgur wheat or brown rice
1/2 tsp grated lemon peel
1/2 c chopped apple, golden raisins or dried apricot
1/4 c chopped walnuts
1/4 c chopped fresh parsley
1/2 tsp dried mint leaves (optional)
1 medium head cabbage
1 can (10 1/2) condensed tomato soup plus 1 tbsp lemon juice
1/4 tsp ground cinnamon
Instructions:
In 10-inch skillet over medium heat, cook meat and onion until meat is well browned, stirring occasionally to break up meat. Pour off fat.
Add beef broth, grain and lemon peel. Heat to boiling. Reduce heat to low. cover; simmer until grain is tender, about 45 minutes for brown rice or 20 to 25 minutes for regular rice or bulgur; add a little water during cooking if mixture becomes dry. Remove from heat. Stir in fruit, walnuts, parsley and mint.
Meanwhile, in a 4-quart saucepan over high heat, heat about 6 cups water to boiling. Add whole head of cabbage to boiling water. Reduce heat to low. Cover; simmer 1 to 2 minutes or until outer leaves are softened. Remove cabbage from water. Carefully remove 6 outer leaves. Reserve remaining cabbage for another use.
Drain cabbage leaves on paper towels. Lay leaves flat on cutting board and cut out any tough stems. Spoon about 3/4 c meat filling into center of one leaf. fold in sides, then roll up from stem end to form a bundle. Repeat with remaining leaves and filling.
In a medium bowl, stir together tomato soup, lemon juice and cinnamon. Pour 1/2 of the soup mixture into a 12x8-inch baking dish. Place cabbage rolls seam side down in prepared dish. Pour remaining soup mixture over all. cover with foil. Bake at 350 degrees for 35 minutes or until heated through. Makes 6 servings.

Source: Vivian Howard's Grandmother's - A Chef's Life
Ingredients:
sweet potatoes
cinnamon
brown sugar
nutmeg
butter
Instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees and place clean sweet potatoes on parchment paper on baking sheet and cook 45 minutes to an hour or until almost tender all the way through. You don't want them soft enough to mash. Peel hot sweet potatoes, slice 1/2 inch thick and place in an oblong baking dish. Sprinkle with spices and brown sugar and then dot with butter and bake until the butter and sugar have melted.

Ingredients:
3 sweet potatoes peeled and cut in 1/2-inch rounds
Canola Oil
Salt
Instructions: In a large saucepot, boil sweet potatoes just until tender. Allow to cool completely. Then press them between 2 pieces of parchment paper or foil, working in batches, so the slices flatten slightly and the edges spread. This can be done up to two days ahead. To finish, fill a deep skillet with an inch of oil and heat until shimmering. Fry sweet potatoes for about 4 minutes on each side or until browned and crispy. Drain on paper towels and sprinkle with salt. To serve stack the sweet potatoes, top with syrup and garnish with green onions

Ingredients:
12 whole new potatoes
3 Tbsp. olive oil
Kosher salt to taste
Black pepper to taste
Rosemary to taste
Instructions:
Bring a pot of salted water to a boil. Add in as many potatoes as you wish to make and cook them until they are fork-tender. On a sheet pan, generously drizzle olive oil. Place tender potatoes on the cookie sheet leaving plenty of room between each potato.
With a potato masher, gently press down each potato until is slightly mashes, rotate the potato masher 90 degrees and mash again. Brush the tops of each crushed potato generously with more olive oil.
Sprinkle potatoes with kosher salt, fresh ground black pepper and fresh chopped rosemary.
Bake in a 450 degree oven to 20-25 minutes until golden brown.

Ingredients:
6 medium baking potatoes (about 2 pounds)
1/3 c butter, melted
1 clove garlic, minced
¼ c grated Parmesan cheese
½ tsp. Italian seasoning, crushed
Instructions: Line a baking pan with foil; set aside. Cut each potato lengthwise into eight wedges. In a large bowl stir together butter, garlic, Parmesan cheese, Italian seasoning, ¼ tsp. salt, and 1/8 tsp. black pepper. Add potato wedges and stir to thoroughly coat. Place the wedges on the prepared baking pan. Bake, uncovered, in a 425 degree oven about 30 minutes or until tender.

Ingredients:
2 cups bacon cut into small dice
1/4 tsp chili flakes
2 cups pecans, chopped
2/3 cup sliced green onion
2/3 cup lemon juice
1/4 cup molasses
2 1/3 cups maple syrup
1 tsp salt
1/4 tsp nutmeg
1/2 tsp cinnamon
2 tbsp plus 2 tsp butter
Instructions: Heat skillet over medium, cook bacon until rendered. Add pecans and scallions and toast for one minute. Add all other ingredients and simmer until mixture has reduced by 1/3. Whisk in butter.

Recipe Name: Perfect Thin and Crispy French Fries
Ingredients:
2 pounds russet potatoes (about 4 large), peeled and cut into ¼-inch by ¼-inch fries (keep potatoes stored in a bowl of water)
2 Tbsp. distilled white vinegar
Kosher salt
2 quarts peanut oil
Instructions:
Place potatoes and vinegar in saucepan and add 2 quarts of water and 2 Tbsp. of salt. Bring to a boil over high heat. Boil for 10 minutes. Potatoes should be full tender, but not falling apart. Drain and spread on paper towel-lined rimmed baking sheet. Allow to dry for five minutes.
Meanwhile, heat oil in 5-quart Dutch oven or large wok over high heat to 400 degrees. Add 1/3 of fries to oil (oil temperature should drop to around 360). Cook for 50 seconds, agitating occasionally with wire mesh spider, and then remove to second paper-towel lined rimmed baking sheet. Repeat with remaining potatoes (working in tow more batches), allowing oil to return to 400 after each addition. Allow potatoes to cool to room temperature about 30 minutes. Continue with step 3, or for best results, freeze potatoes at least overnight, or up to 2 months.
Return oil to 400 over high heat. Fry half of potatoes until crisp and light golden brown about 3 ½ minutes, adjusting heat to maintain at around 360. Drain in a bowl lined with paper towels and season immediately with kosher salt. Cooked fries can be kept hot and crisp on a wire rack set on a sheet tray in a 200 oven while second batch is cooked. Serve immediately.

Ingredients:
5 pound bag new red potatoes
Green onion bunch, chopped
1 pound bacon, cooked and crumbled
Mayo to taste
Salt
Pepper
Instructions: Cook potatoes in salted boiling water until tender. Drain potatoes and return to hot cooking pot and add the rest of the ingredients, stir and serve immediately, or refrigerate.

Ingredients:
2 pounds new potatoes (the smaller the better)
2 Tbsp. Zatarains Crab Boil
4-6 cloves minced garlic
4 Tbsp. butter
2 Tbsp. salt
Instructions: Buy new potatoes that are about the size of ping pong balls or smaller. If you can’t find those, then cut your potatoes into mouth-size pieces, leaving the peel on. Place in an appropriate pot and cover with water. Add crab boil and salt. Bring to a boil and boil until tender; 10 – 15 minutes. Pour potatoes into a strainer and immediately put back into pot. Add garlic and butter and check for salt. Stir and combine flavors.

Ingredients:
1 head of garlic
1 tsp. olive oil
3 pounds Yukon gold potatoes, scrubbed and cut into 1-inch cubes
Coarse salt and ground pepper
1 c half and half
4 Tbsp. butter
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees. Cut off and discard top quarter of garlic heads. Drizzle garlic with oil; wrap in foil. Bake until tender, 1 hour. Squeeze out garlic cloves; discard skins.
Place potatoes in a large saucepan, and cover with cold water by 1 inch; season with salt. Bring to a boil; reduce to a simmer. Cook until potatoes are easily pierced with a paring knife, 15 to 20 minutes. Drain, and return to pan. Heat over medium, stirring, until a thin starchy film covers bottom of pan. Remove from heat; add half-and-half, butter, and garlic. Mash until smooth; season with salt and pepper.

Ingredients:
4 to 6 servings
1 1/2 c heavy cream
3 bay leaves
2 sprigs fresh thyme, plus more to finish
2 garlic cloves, chopped
1/2 tsp freshly grated nutmeg
salt and freshly ground black pepper
unsalted butter
2 lbs russet potatoes, peeled and cut into 1/8-inch-thick slices
1/2 c grated Parmesan, plus more for broiling
Instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees. In a saucepan, heat up the cream with the bay leaves, thyme, garlic, nutmeg and some salt and pepper. While the cream is heating up, butter a casserole dish. Use a slotted spoon to remove the bay leaves and thyme. Pour the heated cream into a large bowl with the potato slices. Mix gently to coat the potatoes. Dust the Parmesan over the potatoes. Season the mix with a little bit of salt and pepper. Mix to gently incorporate. Spoon a little bit of the cream into the bottom of the casserole dish. Then spoon the potatoes in. Level out the potatoes for uniform cooking time. Pour the remaining cream at the bottom of the bowl over the top. Top with some Parmesan and fresh thyme leaves. Cover the dish with aluminium foil, but pull back one corner for the steam to escape. Bake for 40 minutes.

Ingredients:
About 1/2 c vegetable or canola oil, bacon fact, butter, or any combination
2 pounds of russet potatoes (about 4 to 5), peeled and diced
1/2 cup of finely chopped onion
Seasonings to taste
Instructions:
Add enough oil just to cover the bottom of a 10-inch skillet and heat over medium high. Add the potatoes and onion; season to taste with salt, pepper and whatever and toss to coat with oil. Cover skillet and steam cook for 10 minutes covered, before stirring. Remove cover, turn in sections, and continue cooking over medium high, uncovered, turning and stirring occasionally, until potatoes are browned as desired.
These potatoes make a great base for any leftover meats, veggies, or even beans that you have in the fridge. Stir them in toward the end, just to warm through. Eggs are also a great addition. Beat, add to potatoes and let set slightly before stirring.

Ingredients:
6-8 servings
4 1/2 pounds sweet potatoes
1 c sugar
1/4 c milk
1/2 c butter, softened
2 large eggs
1 tsp. vanilla extract
1/4 tsp. salt
1 1/4 c Cornflakes cereal, crushed
1/4 c chopped pecans
1 Tbsp. brown sugar
1 Tbsp. butter, melted
1 1/2 c miniature marshmallows
Instructions:
Bake sweet potatoes at 400 for about 1 hour or until tender. Let cool to touch; peel and mash sweet potatoes. Beat mashed sweet potatoes, sugar and next 5 ingredients at medium speed with an electric mixer until smooth.
Spoon potato mixture into a greased 11 X 7-inch baking dish.
Combine Cornflakes cereal and next 3 ingredients in a small bowl. Sprinkle diagonally over casserole in rows 2 inches apart.
Bake at 350 for 30 minutes. Remove from oven; let stand 10 minutes. Sprinkle alternate rows with marshmallows; bake 10 additional minutes. Let stand 10 minutes before serving.

Ingredients:
4 baking potatoes
½ c sour cream or plain yogurt
¼ tsp. garlic powder
1/8 tsp. black pepper
Milk (optional)
¾ c finely shredded cheddar cheese (3 ounces)
1 Tbsp. snipped fresh chives (optional)
Crumbled bacon (optional)
Chopped cooked ham (optional)
Instructions: Bake potatoes and let stand about 10 minutes. Cut a lengthwise slice off the top of each baked potato; discard skin from slices and place pulp in bowl. Scoop pulp out of potatoes; add pulp to the bowl. Mash the potato pulp with a potato masher or an electric mixer on low speed. Add sour cream, garlic, salt and pepper; beat until smooth. (If necessary, stir in 1 to 2 Tbsp. milk to reach desired consistency.) Season to taste with salt and additional pepper. Stir in ½ c of the cheddar cheese and, if desired, chives. Divide the mashed potato mixture evenly among the potato shells. Place in a 2-quart baking dish. Bake, uncovered, in a 425 oven to 20-25 minutes or until light brown. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. Bake for 2 to 3 minutes more or until cheese melts.

Ingredients:
4 large acorn squash
Salt and pepper
¼ c parmesan cheese, to taste
4 Tbsp. butter
Instructions:
Cut squashes in half, remove seeds, place in baking dish 1/2” water and bake cut side down in 350 oven 1 hour (remove sooner if soft).
Criss-cross cut pulp; sprinkle with salt and pepper, top with parmesan and dot with butter.
Drain water from baking dish.
Return squash to oven, face up; broil just long enough to melt butter and lightly brown cheese.

Ingredients:
2 medium butternut squash, halved lengthwise and seeded
4 tsp. butter
4 tsp. brown sugar
Salt and pepper
Instructions:
Preheat oven to 400 degrees.
Place butternut squash halves on a large baking sheet flesh side up. Place 1 tsp. butter in the middle of each squash. Sprinkle brown sugar over each squash. Season with salt and black pepper. Roast 25 minutes, until flesh is fork-tender. Reserve 2 halves for future meal.

Ingredients:
4 medium zucchini
1/4 c marinara sauce
1 tsp oil
1/2 small onion, finely diced
3 cloves garlic, minced
1/2 c diced red bell pepper
2 Roma tomatoes, diced
14 oz Italian sausage, removed from casing
1/2 c shredded mozzarella
2 tbsp grated Parmesan cheese
salt and pepper, to taste
Instructions:
Bring a large pot of water to boil while also preheating the oven to 400 degrees.
Cut the zucchini in half lengthwise and scoop out the flesh using a spoon, so each "zucchini boat" is about 1/4-inch thick. Put the zucchini in the boiling water and let cook for a minute, then remove from the water.
Lightly grease a 9x13 baking dish, then add the marinara sauce so it covers the bottom of the pan.
In a large saute pan, brown the sausage and break it up into small pieces while it is cooking. Once browned thoroughly, set it aside. Add the oil to the pan and add the onion, garlic, and bell pepper and let cook on medium-low heat for 2-3 minutes. Add the sausage and some salt and pepper to the pan, letting everything cook together for another 2-3 minutes.
Use a spoon to scoop the sausage mixture into the zucchini (each should hold about 1/3 cup of sausage mixture. Top each zucchini with a sprinkle of tomatoes, a tablespoon of mozzarella, and a sprinkle of Parmesan cheese. Cover the dish with foil and bake for about 35 minutes, or the cheese is melted.

Ingredients:
1 large (about 2 1/2 pounds.) spaghetti squash
3/4 c heavy cream
1 garlic clove, pushed through a press
3 finely chopped fresh sage leaves or 1/2 tsp. dried sage
Salt & pepper
1/4 c Parmesan cheese
Instructions:
Heat oven to 400. Prick squash in several places. Bake 45 minutes until tender. Allow to cool slightly, cut in half and scoop out seeds. Pull out squash strands from each side with a fork. Transfer to a warmed bowl.
While squash is baking, heat cream, garlic and sage in a small saucepan over medium heat. Cook 10 minutes, until cream is thick enough to coat the back of a spoon. Pour sauce over squash; toss lightly until combined. Add salt and pepper to taste. Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and serve immediately.
I made this dish by cutting the squash in half while it’s raw, sprinkling with salt, white pepper, garlic powder, sage and parmesan. Then I put about 2 tsp. of cream on each half of the squash, covered it with plastic wrap, set my microwave for two potatoes and cooked it that way. It was perfectly done and delicious!

Ingredients:
3 c cooked yellow squash
1 c cracker crumbs
1/2 c finely cut bell pepper
1 c milk
1 c grated cheese
1/2 c melted margarine
1 egg, well-beaten
1 pound raw, peeled shrimp (optional)
Instructions: Mix all ingredients – Put in large greased loaf dish. Bake at 350 for about 45 minutes.

Ingredients:
2 ½ c grated zucchini
1 egg, beaten
2 Tbsp. butter, melted
1 c low-carb bread crumbs
¼ c minced onion
1 tsp. Old Bay seasoning
Oil for pan fryer
Instructions: In a large bowl, combine all ingredients except oil. Mix well. Shape mixture into patties. In a medium skillet, heat oil over medium high heat until hot. Fry patties until they are golden brown on both sides.

Ingredients:
2 liter ginger ale
8 scoops of food lion rainbow sherbet 
1 cup lemonade
1 cup orange juice
Instructions: Mix and enjoy

Notes: A recipe developed by Van Eure's mother Alice in the late 1960s—with help from kitchen manager Betty Shugart (a 50-plus-year employee of the restaurant)—an estimated 50,000 chocolate chess pies are now made and sold each year.
Ingredients:
1 unbaked pie shell
1 stick of butter
3 ounces of semi-sweet chocolate
1 cup sugar
2 eggs, beaten
1 teaspoon vanilla
A dash of salt
Instructions:
Melt butter and chocolate over a double broiler or on a low power setting in the microwave
Blend together the other ingredients in medium-sized bowl, then mix in the melted butter and chocolate
Pour ingredients into the pie shell and bake 35 minutes at 350 degrees
Allow pie to cool completely, then optionally top with whipped cream and/or serve with vanilla ice cream
Related Recipes: Chocolate Chess Pie — Rose Marie Luke
